<td style="text-align: center;">4</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">4</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">4</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">4</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">4</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Wideband CQI for the second TB</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">0</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">0</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">0</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">0</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">4</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Subband differential CQI for the first TB</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">2</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">2</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">2</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">2</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">2</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Subband differential CQI for the second TB</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">0</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">0</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">0</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">0</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">2</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">CRI if <em>csi-ReportMode= Mode 1</em> and <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 = 1</em></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\left\lceil \log_{2}\left( M_{1} + M_{2} \right) \right\rceil\]</span></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\left\lceil \log_{2}\left( M_{1} + M_{2} \right) \right\rceil\]</span></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\left\lceil \log_{2}\left( M_{1} + M_{2} \right) \right\rceil\]</span></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\left\lceil \log_{2}\left( M_{1} + M_{2} \right) \right\rceil\]</span></td>

</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">CRI if <em>csi-ReportMode= Mode 1</em> and <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 = 2</em></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><p><span class="math inline">\(\left\lceil \log_{2}\left( M_{1} \right) \right\rceil\)</span> for the first CRI;</p>
<p><span class="math inline">\(\left\lceil \log_{2}\left( M_{2} \right) \right\rceil\)</span> for the second CRI</p></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><p><span class="math inline">\(\left\lceil \log_{2}\left( M_{1} \right) \right\rceil\)</span> for the first CRI;</p>
<p><span class="math inline">\(\left\lceil \log_{2}\left( M_{2} \right) \right\rceil\)</span> for the second CRI</p></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><p><span class="math inline">\(\left\lceil \log_{2}\left( M_{1} \right) \right\rceil\)</span> for the first CRI;</p>
<p><span class="math inline">\(\left\lceil \log_{2}\left( M_{2} \right) \right\rceil\)</span> for the second CRI</p></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><p><span class="math inline">\(\left\lceil \log_{2}\left( M_{1} \right) \right\rceil\)</span> for the first CRI;</p>
<p><span class="math inline">\(\left\lceil \log_{2}\left( M_{2} \right) \right\rceil\)</span> for the second CRI</p></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><p><span class="math inline">\(\left\lceil \log_{2}\left( M_{1} \right) \right\rceil\)</span> for the first CRI;</p>
<p><span class="math inline">\(\left\lceil \log_{2}\left( M_{2} \right) \right\rceil\)</span> for the second CRI</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">CRI if <em>csi-ReportMode= Mode 2</em></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\left\lceil \log_{2}\left( M_{1} + M_{2} + N \right) \right\rceil\]</span></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\left\lceil \log_{2}\left( M_{1} + M_{2} + N \right) \right\rceil\]</span></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\left\lceil \log_{2}\left( M_{1} + M_{2} + N \right) \right\rceil\]</span></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\left\lceil \log_{2}\left( M_{1} + M_{2} + N \right) \right\rceil\]</span></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\left\lceil \log_{2}\left( M_{1} + M_{2} + N \right) \right\rceil\]</span></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

$n_{RI,NCJT}$ in Table 6.3.1.1.2-3A is the number of allowed rank combination indicator values associated with one CSI-RS resource pair according to Clause 5.2.1.4.2 \[6, TS 38.214\]. The values of the rank combination indicator field are mapped to allowed rank combinations in the following order: {1,1}, {1,2}, {2,1},{2,2}, where '0' is mapped to the first allowed rank combination. $v_{1}$ and $v_{2}$ are the values of the first and the second rank associated with two CSI‑RS resources of the CSI-RS resource pair respectively.

$n_{RI,\ sTRP}$ in Table 6.3.1.1.2-3B is the number of allowed rank indicator values associated with one CSI-RS resource according to Clause 5.2.1.4.2 \[6, TS 38.214\]. *v* is the value of the rank associated with the CSI-RS resource. The values of the rank indicator field are mapped to allowed rank indicator values with increasing order, where '0' is mapped to the smallest allowed rank indicator value.

The value of *N* in Table 6.3.1.1.2-3A and Table 6.3.1.1.2-3B is the number of CSI-RS resource pairs configured within a CSI-RS resource set. The values of M<sub>1</sub> and M<sub>2</sub> in Table 6.3.1.1.2-3A and Table 6.3.1.1.2-3B are given by

- If *sharedCMR* = "Enabled", *M*<sub>1</sub> = *K*<sub>1</sub> and *M*<sub>2</sub> = *K*<sub>2</sub>

- If *sharedCMR* is absent and *N* = 1, *M*<sub>1</sub> = *K*<sub>1</sub> - 1 and *M*<sub>2</sub> = *K*<sub>2</sub> - 1

- If *sharedCMR* is absent and *N* = 2,

*- M*<sub>1</sub> = *K*<sub>1</sub> - 2 and *M*<sub>2</sub> = *K*<sub>2</sub> - 2, if the two resource pairs do not share any CSI-RS resource

*- M*<sub>1</sub> = *K*<sub>1</sub> - 1 and *M*<sub>2</sub> = *K*<sub>2</sub> - 2, if the two resource pairs share the same CSI-RS resource from the first CSI-RS resource group

*- M*<sub>1</sub> = *K*<sub>1</sub> - 2 and *M*<sub>2</sub> = *K*<sub>2</sub> - 1, if the two resource pairs share the same CSI-RS resource from the second CSI-RS resource group

where the values of *K*<sub>1</sub> and *K*<sub>2</sub> are the numbers of CSI-RS resources in the first and second CSI-RS resource groups within the CSI-RS resource set respectively.

The bitwidth for RI/LI/CQI/CRI of *codebookType=typeI-SinglePanel-r19* is provided in Table 6.3.1.1.2-3C.

The values of the rank indicator field are mapped to allowed rank indicator values with increasing order, where '0' is mapped to the smallest allowed rank indicator value.</td>

The values of the rank indicator field are mapped to allowed rank indicator values with increasing order, where '0' is mapped to the smallest allowed rank indicator value.</td>

<table style="width:80%;">
<colgroup>

<col style="width: 17%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;"><strong>Field</strong></th>
<th style="text-align: center;"><strong>Bitwidth</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Rank Indicator</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">$\min \left(1,\left\lceil \log _{2}n_{\text{RI}}\right\rceil \right)$</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Layer Indicator</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">$\min \left(2,\left\lceil \log _{2}\upsilon \right\rceil \right)$</td>

wideband amplitude coefficients $M_{l}$ for layer $l$</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">$\left\lceil \log _{2}\left(2L-1\right)\right\rceil$</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="2" style="text-align: center;">NOTE: <span class="math inline">\(n_{RI}\)</span> is the number of allowed rank indicator values according to Clauses 5.2.2.2.3 and 5.2.2.2.4 [6, TS 38.214] and <span class="math inline">\(\upsilon\)</span> is the value of the rank. The values of the rank indicator field are mapped to allowed rank indicator values with increasing order, where '0' is mapped to the smallest allowed rank indicator value.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

The bitwidth for CRI, SSBRI, RSRP, differential RSRP, and CapabilityIndex are provided in Table 6.3.1.1.2-6.

Table 6.3.1.1.2-6: CRI, SSBRI, RSRP, and CapabilityIndex

<table style="width:79%;">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 33%" />
<col style="width: 45%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;"><strong>Field</strong></th>
<th style="text-align: center;"><strong>Bitwidth</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">CRI</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">$\left\lceil \log _{2}\left(K_{s}^{\text{CSI}-\text{RS}}\right)\right\rceil$</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">SSBRI</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">$\left\lceil \log _{2}\left(K_{s}^{\text{SSB}}\right)\right\rceil$</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">RSRP</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">7</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Differential RSRP</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">4</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">CapabilityIndex</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">2</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="2" style="text-align: center;">NOTE: <span class="math inline">\(K_{s}^{CSI - RS}\)</span> is the number of CSI-RS resources in the corresponding resource set, <span class="math inline">\(K_{s}^{SSB}\)</span> is the configured number of SS/PBCH blocks in the corresponding resource set for reporting 'ssb-Index-RSRP' or 'ssb-Index-RSRP-Index'.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

The bitwidth for CRI, SSBRI, SINR, differential SINR, and CapabilityIndex are provided in Table 6.3.1.1.2-6A.

Table 6.3.1.1.2-6A: CRI, SSBRI, SINR, and CapabilityIndex

<table style="width:80%;">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 43%" />
<col style="width: 35%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;"><strong>Field</strong></th>
<th style="text-align: center;"><strong>Bitwidth</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">CRI</td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\left\lceil \log_{2}\left( K_{s}^{CSI - RS} \right) \right\rceil\]</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">SSBRI</td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\left\lceil \log_{2}\left( K_{s}^{SSB} \right) \right\rceil\]</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">SINR</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">7</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Differential SINR</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">4</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">CapabilityIndex</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">2</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="2" style="text-align: center;">NOTE: <span class="math inline">\(K_{s}^{CSI - RS}\)</span> is the number of CSI-RS resources in the corresponding resource set, and <span class="math inline">\(K_{s}^{SSB}\)</span> is the configured number of SS/PBCH blocks in the corresponding resource set for reporting 'ssb-Index-SINR' or 'ssb-Index-SINR-Index'.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

The bitwidth for MRI and CLI-RSSI are provided in Table 6.3.1.1.2-6B.

Table 6.3.1.1.2-6B: Bitwidth for MRI and CLI-RSSI

<table style="width:81%;">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 21%" />
<col style="width: 59%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;"><strong>Field</strong></th>
<th style="text-align: center;"><strong>Bitwidth</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">MRI</td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\left\lceil \log_{2}\left( K_{s}^{CLI - RSSI} \right) \right\rceil\]</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">CLI-RSSI</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">7</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Differential CLI-RSSI</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">4</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="2" style="text-align: center;">NOTE: <span class="math inline">\(K_{s}^{CLI - RSSI}\)</span> is the number of CLI-RSSI measurement resources in the corresponding resource set.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

The bitwidth for predicted CRI, predicted SSBRI, predicted RSRP, differential predicted RSRP are provided in Table 6.3.1.1.2-6C.

Table 6.3.1.1.2-6C: Predicted CRI, Predicted SSBRI, Predicted RSRP and Differential predicted RSRP

<table style="width:71%;">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 29%" />
<col style="width: 41%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;"><strong>Field</strong></th>
<th style="text-align: center;"><strong>Bitwidth</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Predicted CRI</td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\left\lceil \log_{2}\left( K_{s}^{CSI - RS} \right) \right\rceil\]</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Predicted SSBRI</td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\left\lceil \log_{2}\left( K_{s}^{SSB} \right) \right\rceil\]</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Predicted RSRP</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">7</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Differential predicted RSRP</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">4</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Time instance indicator</td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\left\lceil \log_{2}(N) \right\rceil\]</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="2" style="text-align: center;">NOTE: <span class="math inline">\(K_{s}^{CSI - RS}\)</span> is the number of CSI-RS resources in the resource set configured by <em>resourcesForChannelPrediction</em>, <span class="math inline">\(K_{s}^{SSB}\)</span> is the number of SS/PBCH blocks in the resource set configured by <em>resourcesForChannelPrediction,</em> and <span class="math inline">\(N\)</span> is the number of predicted time instances configured by <em>nrofTimeInstance.</em></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

The bitwidth for RS-PAI is provided in Table 6.3.1.1.2-6D.

Table 6.3.1.1.2-6D: RS-PAI

<table style="width:71%;">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 29%" />
<col style="width: 41%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;"><strong>Field</strong></th>
<th style="text-align: center;"><strong>Bitwidth</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">RS-PAI</td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\left\lceil \log_{2}\left( N^{Mon} + 1 \right) \right\rceil\]</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="2" style="text-align: center;">NOTE: <span class="math inline">\(N^{Mon}\)</span> is the number of transmission occasion(s) of monitoring resources configured by <em>nrofTransmissionOccasion.</em></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

If *CSI-ReportSubConfig* is configured, for a corresponding CSI sub-report, the mapping order of CSI fields of one CSI sub-report is determined following the procedure in this clause 6.3.1.1.2, by replacing CSI report \#n in the following Tables 6.3.1.1.2-7, 6.3.1.1.2-9 and 6.3.1.1.2-10 with CSI sub-report \#n, and taking only Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1/1A/1B/2/2A/3/4 for the determination of the bitwidth of a CSI field.

Table 6.3.1.1.2-7: Mapping order of CSI fields of one CSI report,  
*pmi-FormatIndicator=widebandPMI* and *cqi-FormatIndicator=widebandCQI* or *reportQuantity  *
set to 'cri-RI-CQI' and *cqi-FormatIndicator=widebandCQI*

<table style="width:98%;">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 18%" />
<col style="width: 80%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>CSI report number</th>
<th>CSI fields</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td rowspan="8">CSI report #n</td>
<td>CRI as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3/4, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Rank Indicator as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3/3C/4, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Layer Indicator as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3/3C/4, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Zero padding bits $O_{P}$, if needed</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>PMI wideband information fields $X_{1}$, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1/1A/1B/2/2A, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>PMI wideband information fields $X_{2}$, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1/1A/1B/2/2A, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214], if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Wideband CQI for the first TB as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3/3C/4, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Wideband CQI for the second TB as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3/3C/4, if reported</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

The number of zero padding bits $O_{P}$ in Table 6.3.1.1.2-7 is 0 for 1 CSI-RS port and $O_{P}=N_{\max }-N_{\text{reported}}$ for more than 1 CSI-RS port, where:

- $N_{\max }=\max_{r\in S_{\text{Rank}}}B\left(r\right)$ and $S_{\text{Rank}}$ is the set of rank values $r$ that are allowed to be reported;

- $N_{\text{reported}}=B\left(R\right)$, where $R$ is the reported rank;

- For 2 CSI-RS ports, $B\left(r\right)=N_{\text{PMI}}\left(r\right)+N_{\text{CQI}}\left(r\right)+N_{\text{LI}}\left(r\right)$;

- For more than 2 CSI-RS ports, $B\left(r\right)=N_{\text{PMI,i1}}\left(r\right)+N_{\text{PMI,i2}}\left(r\right)+N_{\text{CQI}}\left(r\right)+N_{\text{LI}}\left(r\right)$;

- if PMI is reported, $N_{\text{PMI}}\left(1\right)=2$ and $N_{\text{PMI}}\left(2\right)=1$; otherwise, $N_{\text{PMI}}\left(r\right)=0$;

- if PMI $i1$ is reported, $N_{\text{PMI,i1}}\left(r\right)$ is obtained according to Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1/1A/1B/2/2A; otherwise, $N_{\text{PMI,i1}}\left(r\right)=0$;

- if PMI $i2$ is reported, $N_{\text{PMI,i2}}\left(r\right)$ is obtained according to Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1/1A/1B/2/2A; otherwise, $N_{\text{PMI,i2}}\left(r\right)=0$;

- if CQI is reported, $N_{\text{CQI}}\left(r\right)$ is obtained according to Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3/3C/4; otherwise, $N_{\text{CQI}}\left(r\right)=0$;

- if LI is reported, $N_{\text{LI}}\left(r\right)$ is obtained according to Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3/3C/4; otherwise, $N_{\text{LI}}\left(r\right)=0$.

Table 6.3.1.1.2-7A: Mapping order of CSI fields of one CSI report, *pmi-FormatIndicator=widebandPMI*, *cqi-FormatIndicator=widebandCQI, csi-ReportMode= Mode 1* and *numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1=0*

<table style="width:98%;">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 18%" />
<col style="width: 80%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>CSI report number</th>
<th>CSI fields</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td rowspan="9">CSI report #n</td>
<td>CRI as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3A, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Rank Combination Indicator as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3A, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Two Layer Indicators as in Table 6.3.1.1.2-3A, where the first Layer Indicator and the second Layer Indicator are associated with the first resource and the second resource within the resource pair respectively and if reported;</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Zero padding bits <span class="math inline">\(O_{P}\)</span>, if needed</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>PMI wideband information fields <em>X<sub>1</sub></em>, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1 associated with the first resource within the CSI-RS resource pair, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>PMI wideband information fields <em>X<sub>2</sub></em>, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] associated with the first CSI-RS resource within the CSI-RS resource pair, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>PMI wideband information fields <em>X<sub>1</sub></em>, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1 associated with the second resource within the CSI-RS resource pair, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>PMI wideband information fields <em>X<sub>2</sub></em>, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] associated with the second CSI-RS resource within the CSI-RS resource pair, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Wideband CQI for the first TB as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3A, if reported</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

The number of zero padding bits $O_{P}$ in Table 6.3.1.1.2-7A is 0 for 1 CSI-RS port and $O_{P} = N_{\max} - N_{reported}$ for more than 1 CSI-RS port, where:

- $N_{\max} = \max_{r \in S_{Rank}}{B(r)}\$ and $S_{Rank}$ is the set of rank combination values of $r = \{ r_{1},r_{2}\}$ that are allowed to be reported;

- $N_{reported} = B(R)$ where R is the reported rank combination;

- For 2 CSI-RS ports, $B(r) = N_{PMI}(r_{1}) + N_{PMI}(r_{2}) + N_{CQI}(r) + N_{LI}(r_{1}) + N_{LI}(r_{2})$;

- For more than 2 CSI-RS ports,$\ B(r) = N_{PMI,i_{1}}(r_{1}) + N_{PMI,i_{1}}(r_{2}) + N_{PMI,i_{2}}(r_{1}) + N_{PMI,i_{2}}(r_{2}) + N_{CQI}(r) + N_{LI}(r_{1}) + N_{LI}(r_{2})$ ;

- if PMI is reported, $N_{PMI}(1) = 2$ and $N_{PMI}(2) = 1$; otherwise,$\ N_{PMI} = 0$;

- if PMI $i_{1}$ is reported,$\ N_{PMI,i_{1}}(r_{1})$ and $N_{PMI,i_{1}}(r_{2})$ are obtained according to Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1; otherwise, $N_{PMI,i_{1}} = 0$;

- if PMI $i_{2}$ is reported, $N_{PMI,i_{2}}(r_{1})$ and $N_{PMI,i_{2}}(r_{2})$ are obtained according to Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1; otherwise, $\ N_{PMI,i_{2}} = 0$;

- if CQI is reported, $N_{CQI}(r)$ is obtained according to Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3A; otherwise,$\ N_{CQI}(r) = 0$;

- if LI is reported, $N_{LI}(r_{1})$ and $N_{LI}(r_{2})$ are obtained according to Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3A; otherwise , $N_{LI} = 0$.

Table 6.3.1.1.2-7B: Mapping order of CSI fields of one CSI report configured with *valueOfM*,  
*pmi-FormatIndicator=widebandPMI* and *cqi-FormatIndicator=widebandCQI*

<table style="width:98%;">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 18%" />
<col style="width: 80%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;"><strong>CSI report number</strong></th>
<th style="text-align: center;"><strong>CSI fields</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td rowspan="17" style="text-align: center;">CSI report #n</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">The first reported CRI as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Rank Indicator for the first reported CRI as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Layer Indicator for the first reported CRI as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Zero padding bits <span class="math inline">\(O_{P,1}\)</span> for the first reported CRI, if needed</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">PMI wideband information fields <span class="math inline">\(X_{1}\)</span> for the first reported CRI, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">PMI wideband information fields <span class="math inline">\(X_{2}\)</span> for the first reported CRI, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports for the first reported CRI according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214], if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Wideband CQI for the first TB for the first reported CRI as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Wideband CQI for the second TB for the first reported CRI as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">…</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">The <em>M</em>-th reported CRI as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Rank Indicator for the <em>M</em>-th reported CRI as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Layer Indicator for the <em>M</em>-th reported CRI as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Zero padding bits <span class="math inline">\(O_{P,M}\)</span> for the <em>M</em>-th reported CRI, if needed</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">PMI wideband information fields <span class="math inline">\(X_{1}\)</span> for the <em>M</em>-th reported CRI, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">PMI wideband information fields <span class="math inline">\(X_{2}\)</span> for the <em>M</em>-th reported CRI, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports for the <em>M</em>-th reported CRI according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214], if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Wideband CQI for the first TB for the <em>M</em>-th reported CRI as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Wideband CQI for the second TB for the <em>M</em>-th reported CRI as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3, if reported</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

The number of zero padding bits $O_{P,k}$ for *k*-th reported CRI in Table 6.3.1.1.2-7B is 0 for 1 CSI-RS port and $O_{P,k} = N_{\max} - N_{reported,k}$ for more than 1 CSI-RS port, where:

- $N_{\max} = \ \max_{j \in Q\ \ }{(N_{max,j}})$, where *Q* is the set of CRIs corresponding to the *K<sub>s</sub>* resources and $N_{max,j}$ is the maximum payload size of associated CSI fields for the *j*-th CRI;

- $N_{max,j} = N_{RI}(j){+ \max_{r_{j} \in S_{rank,j}}}{B(r_{j})}$, where $N_{RI}(j)$ is the payload size of RI field for the *j*-th CRI, and $S_{rank,j}$ is the set of rank values $r_{j}\$that are allowed to be reported for the *j*-th CRI obtained according to Table 6.3.1.1.2-3;

- $N_{reported,k} = \ N_{RI}(k) + {B(R_{k})}$, where $R_{k}$ is the reported rank for the *k*-th CRI;

- For 2 CSI-RS ports, $B(r) = N_{\text{PMI}}(r) + N_{\text{CQI}}(r) + N_{\text{LI}}(r)$;

- For more than 2 CSI-RS ports, $B(r) = N_{\text{PMI,}i_{1}}(r) + N_{\text{PMI,}i_{2}}(r) + N_{\text{CQI}}(r) + N_{\text{LI}}(r)$;

- if PMI is reported, $N_{\text{PMI}}(1) = 2$ and $N_{\text{PMI}}(2) = 1$; otherwise, $N_{\text{PMI}}(r) = 0$;

- if PMI $i1$ is reported, $N_{\text{PMI,}i_{1}}(r)$ is obtained according to Table 6.3.1.1.2-1; otherwise, $N_{\text{PMI,}i_{1}}(r) = 0$;

- if PMI $i2$ is reported, $N_{\text{PMI,}i_{2}}(r)$ is obtained according to Table 6.3.1.1.2-1; otherwise, $N_{\text{PMI,}i_{2}}(r) = 0$;

- if CQI is reported, $N_{\text{CQI}}(r)$ is obtained according to Table 6.3.1.1.2-3; otherwise, $N_{\text{CQI}}(r) = 0$;

- if LI is reported, $N_{\text{LI}}(r)$ is obtained according to Table 6.3.1.1.2-3; otherwise, $N_{\text{LI}}(r) = 0$.

Table 6.3.1.1.2-8: Mapping order of CSI fields of one report for CRI/RSRP or SSBRI/RSRP or CRI/RSRP/CapabilityIndex or SSBRI/RSRP/CapabilityIndex reporting, or mapping order of CSI fields of one report for inter-cell SSBRI/RSRP reporting

</tr>

</tr>
<tr>
<td>Differential RSRP #2 as in Table 6.3.1.1.2-6, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Differential RSRP #3 as in Table 6.3.1.1.2-6, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Differential RSRP #4 as in Table 6.3.1.1.2-6, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>CapabilityIndex #1 as in Table 6.3.1.1.2-6, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>CapabilityIndex #2 as in Table 6.3.1.1.2-6, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>CapabilityIndex #3 as in Table 6.3.1.1.2-6, if reported</td>
</tr>

</table>

where the 1-bit resource set indicator, with value of 0 or 1, indicates the 1<sup>st</sup> or the 2<sup>nd</sup> channel measurement resource set respectively, from which CRI or SSBRI \#1 of 1<sup>st</sup> resource group is reported from; and all remaining resource groups, if reported, follow the same mapping order as the 1<sup>st</sup> resource group where CRI or SSBRI \#1 of all remaining resource groups is reported from the indicated channel measurement resource set. For all reported resource groups, CRI or SSBRI \#1 and CRI or SSBRI \#2 are reported from different channel measurement resource sets.

</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Differential predicted RSRP #2 as in Table 6.3.1.1.2-6C of time instance #<span class="math inline">\(N\)</span>, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">…</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Differential predicted RSRP #<span class="math inline">\(K\)</span> as in Table 6.3.1.1.2-6C of time instance #<span class="math inline">\(N\)</span>, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="2" style="text-align: center;"><p>NOTE:</p>
<p><span class="math inline">\(K\)</span> is the number of predicted CRI or predicted SSBRI to be reported for a time instance configured by the higher layer parameter <em>nrofReportedPredictedRS</em>.</p>
<p><span class="math inline">\(N\)</span> is the number of time instances for prediction and the value of <span class="math inline">\(N\)</span> is configured by the higher layer parameter <em>nrofTimeInstance</em>. Time instance indicator is reported only when <span class="math inline">\(N\)</span> is larger than 1. The <span class="math inline">\(N\)</span> time instances for prediction are ordered in ascending order in time domain. The time instance indicator with value <span class="math inline">\(n\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\(n = 0,1,\ldots,N - 1\)</span>, indicates the (<em>n</em>+1)-th time instance among the <span class="math inline">\(N\)</span> time instances. Time instance #1 corresponds to the time instance indicated by the time instance indicator among the <span class="math inline">\(N\)</span> time instances if <span class="math inline">\(N\)</span> is larger than 1, and corresponds to the time instance for prediction otherwise. Time instance # <span class="math inline">\(i\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\(i = 2,3,\ldots,N\)</span>, corresponds to the (<span class="math inline">\(i\)</span>-1)-th time instance among the remaining <span class="math inline">\(N - 1\)</span> time instances.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

Table 6.3.1.1.2-9A: Mapping order of CSI fields of one CSI report, CSI part 1, *csi-ReportMode= Mode 1*

<table style="width:100%;">
<colgroup>

<col style="width: 79%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>

<tbody>
<tr>
<td rowspan="12"><p>CSI report #n</p>
<p>CSI part 1</p></td>
<td>CRI as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3A, if associated with one CSI-RS resource pair and if reported</td>
</tr>

<td>Rank Combination Indicator as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3A, if reported</td>
</tr>

<tr>
<td><p>CRI as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3B, if associated with one CSI-RS resource, <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 = 1</em> and if reported;</p>

</tr>
<tr>
<td><p>Wideband CQI associated with CRI for the first TB as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3B, if <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 1 and if reported;</p>
<p>Wideband CQI associated with the first CRI for the first TB as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3B, if <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 = 2</em> and if reported</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><p>Subband differential CQI associated with CRI for the first TB with increasing order of subband number as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3B, if <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 1 if reported;</p>
<p>Subband differential CQI associated with the first CRI for the first TB with increasing order of subband number as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3B, if <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 2 and if reported</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Second CRI as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3B, if associated with one CSI-RS resource, <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 2 and if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Rank Indicator associated with the second CRI as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3B, if <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 2 and if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Wideband CQI associated with the second CRI for the first TB as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3B, if <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 2 and if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Subband differential CQI associated with the second CRI for the first TB with increasing order of subband number as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3B, if <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 2 and if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="2">NOTE: Subbands for given CSI report <em>n</em> indicated by the higher layer parameter <em>csi-ReportingBand</em> with value set to '1' are numbered continuously in the increasing order with the lowest subband of <em>csi-ReportingBand</em> with value set to '1' as subband 0.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

Table 6.3.1.1.2-9B: Mapping order of CSI fields of one CSI report, CSI part 1, *csi-ReportMode= Mode 2*

<table style="width:100%;">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 20%" />
<col style="width: 79%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>CSI report number</th>
<th>CSI fields</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td rowspan="4"><p>CSI report #n</p>
<p>CSI part 1</p></td>
<td><p>CRI as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3A, if associated with one CSI-RS resource pair and if reported;</p>
<p>CRI as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3B, if associated with one CSI-RS resource and if reported</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><p>Rank Combination Indicator as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3A, if associated with one CSI-RS resource pair and if reported;</p>
<p>Rank Indicator as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3B, if associated with one CSI-RS resource and if reported;</p>
<p>Zero padding bits <span class="math inline">\(O_{P}\)</span>, if needed</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><p>Wideband CQI for the first TB as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3A, if associated with one CSI-RS resource pair and if reported;</p>
<p>Wideband CQI for the first TB as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3B, if associated with one CSI-RS resource and if reported</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><p>Subband differential CQI for the first TB with increasing order of subband number as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3A, if associated with one CSI-RS resource pair and if reported;</p>
<p>Subband differential CQI for the first TB with increasing order of subband number as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3B, if associated with one CSI-RS resource and if reported</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="2">NOTE: Subbands for given CSI report <em>n</em> indicated by the higher layer parameter <em>csi-ReportingBand</em> with value set to '1' are numbered continuously in the increasing order with the lowest subband of <em>csi-ReportingBand</em> with value set to '1' as subband 0.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

The number of zero padding bits $O_{P}$ in Table 6.3.1.1.2-9B is 0 for 1 CSI-RS port and $O_{P} = N_{\max} - N_{reported}(R)$ for more than 1 CSI-RS port, where:

- $N_{\max} = \max_{r \in S_{Rank}}{N(r)}\$. $S_{Rank}\$is the set of rank and rank combination values *r* that are allowed to be reported. $N(r)$ is obtained according to Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3A/3B for rank combination indicator and rank indicator respectively.

- $N_{reported}\ (R)\$is obtained according to Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3A for rank combination indicator and *R* is the reported rank combination.

- $N_{reported}\ (R)$ is obtained according to Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3B for rank indicator and *R* is the reported rank.

Table 6.3.1.1.2-9C: Mapping order of CSI fields of one CSI report configured with *valueOfM*, CSI part 1, *pmi-FormatIndicator=* *subbandPMI* or *cqi-FormatIndicator=subbandCQI*

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 20%" />
<col style="width: 79%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;"><strong>CSI report number</strong></th>
<th style="text-align: center;"><strong>CSI fields</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td rowspan="11" style="text-align: center;"><p>CSI report #n</p>
<p>CSI part 1</p></td>
<td style="text-align: center;">CRI for the first reported CRI as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Rank Indicator for the first reported CRI as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Zero padding bits <span class="math inline">\(O_{P,1}\)</span> for the first reported CRI, if needed</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Wideband CQI for the first TB for the first reported CRI as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Subband differential CQI for the first TB with increasing order of subband number for the first reported CRI as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">…</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">CRI for the <em>M</em>-th reported CRI as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Rank Indicator for the <em>M</em>-th reported CRI as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Zero padding bits <span class="math inline">\(O_{P,M}\)</span> for the <em>M</em>-th reported CRI, if needed</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Wideband CQI for the first TB for the <em>M</em>-th reported CRI as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Subband differential CQI for the first TB with increasing order of subband number for the <em>M</em>-th reported CRI as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="2" style="text-align: center;">NOTE: Subbands for given CSI report <em>n</em> indicated by the higher layer parameter <em>csi-ReportingBand</em> with value set to '1' are numbered continuously in the increasing order with the lowest subband of <em>csi-ReportingBand</em> with value set to '1' as subband 0.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

The number of zero padding bits $O_{P,k}$ in Table 6.3.1.1.2-9C is 0 for 1 CSI-RS port and $O_{P,k} = N_{\max} - N_{reported,k}$ for more than 1 CSI-RS port, where:

- $N_{\max} = \max_{i \in S}\left( N_{i} \right)$, where *S* is the set of CRIs corresponding to the *K<sub>s</sub>* resources and $N_{i}$ is the payload size of RI field for the *i*-th CRI obtained according to Table 6.3.1.1.2-3;

- $N_{reported,k}$ is the payload size of RI field for the *k*-th CRI obtained according to Table 6.3.1.1.2-3.

<p>PMI wideband information fields $X_{1}$, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, if associated with the first CRI in CSI part 1, <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 2 and if reported</p></td>

<p>PMI wideband information fields $X_{2}$, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214], if associated with the first CRI in CSI part 1, <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>widebandPMI,</em> <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 2 and if reported</p></td>
</tr>

</tr>

</tr>

</tr>

</tr>

</tr>

</tr>
<tr>

<p>Subband differential CQI for the second TB of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number associated with the first CRI in CSI part 1, as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3B, if <em>cqi-FormatIndicator=subbandCQI</em>, <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 2 and if reported</p></th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th><p>PMI subband information fields $X_{2}$ of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports associated with CRI in CSI part 1 according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number, if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>subbandPMI</em>, <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 1 and if reported;</p>
<p>PMI subband information fields $X_{2}$ of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports associated with the first CRI in CSI part 1 according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number, if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>subbandPMI</em>, <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 2 and if reported</p></th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th>Subband differential CQI for the second TB of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number associated with the second CRI in CSI part 1, as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3B, if <em>cqi-FormatIndicator=subbandCQI</em>, <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 2 and if reported</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th>PMI subband information fields $X_{2}$ of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports associated with the second CRI in CSI part 1 according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number, if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>subbandPMI</em>, <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 2 and if reported</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th>PMI subband information fields $X_{2}$ of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports associated with the first resource within the CSI-RS resource pair, according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number, if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>subbandPMI</em> and if reported</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th>PMI subband information fields $X_{2}$ of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports associated with the second resource within the CSI-RS resource pair, according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number, if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>subbandPMI</em> and if reported</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th><p>Subband differential CQI for the second TB of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number associated with CRI in CSI part 1, as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3B, if <em>cqi-FormatIndicator=subbandCQI</em>, <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 1 and if reported;</p>
<p>Subband differential CQI for the second TB of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number associated with the first CRI in CSI part 1, as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3B, if <em>cqi-FormatIndicator=subbandCQI</em>, <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 2 and if reported</p></th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th><p>PMI subband information fields $X_{2}$ of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports associated with CRI in CSI part 1 according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number, if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>subbandPMI</em>, <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 1 and if reported;</p>
<p>PMI subband information fields $X_{2}$ of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports associated with the first CRI in CSI part 1 according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number, if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>subbandPMI</em>, <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 2 and if reported</p></th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th>Subband differential CQI for the second TB of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number associated with the second CRI in CSI part 1, as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3B, if <em>cqi-FormatIndicator=subbandCQI</em>, <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 2 and if reported</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th>PMI subband information fields $X_{2}$ of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports associated with the second CRI in CSI part 1 according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number, if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>subbandPMI</em>, <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 2 and if reported</th>
</tr>

</tr>

CSI sub-report #1, CSI sub-report #2, …, CSI sub-report #<span class="math inline">\(N_{n}^{sub}\)</span> correspond to the CSI sub-reports in increasing order of <em>reportSubConfigId</em>.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

Table 6.3.1.1.2-11D: Mapping order of CSI fields of one CSI report configured with *valueOfM*, CSI part 2 subband, *pmi-FormatIndicator=* *subbandPMI* or *cqi-FormatIndicator=subbandCQI*

<table style="width:98%;">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 15%" />
<col style="width: 82%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th rowspan="9" style="text-align: center;"><p>CSI report #n</p>
<p>Part 2 subband</p></th>
<th style="text-align: center;">Subband differential CQI for the second TB of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number for the first reported CRI, as in Table 6.3.1.1.2-3, if <em>cqi-FormatIndicator=subbandCQI</em> and if reported</th>

</tr>

</tr>

<td colspan="2">NOTE: Subbands for given CSI report <em>n</em> indicated by the higher layer parameter <em>csi-ReportingBand</em> with value set to '1' are numbered continuously in the increasing order with the lowest subband of <em>csi-ReportingBand</em> with value set to '1' as subband 0.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

If none of the CSI reports for transmission on a PUCCH is of two parts, the CSI fields of all CSI reports, in the order from upper part to lower part in Table 6.3.1.1.2-12, are mapped to the UCI bit sequence $a_{0},a_{1},a_{2},a_{3},...,a_{A-1}$ starting with $a_{0}$. The most significant bit of each field is mapped to the lowest order information bit for that field, e.g. the most significant bit of the first field is mapped to$a_{0}$. If the length of UCI bit sequence $a_{0},a_{1},a_{2},a_{3},...,a_{A - 1}$ is less than 3 bits, zeros shall be appended to the UCI bit sequence until its length equals 3.

Table 6.3.1.1.2-12: Mapping order of CSI reports to UCI bit sequence $a_{0},a_{1},a_{2},a_{3},...,a_{A-1}$,  
without two-part CSI report(s)

<table style="width:82%;">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 19%" />
<col style="width: 62%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>UCI bit sequence</th>
<th style="text-align: center;">CSI report number</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td rowspan="4">$\begin{matrix} a_{0} \\ a_{1} \\ a_{2} \\ a_{3} \\ \ \vdots  \\ a_{A-1} \end{matrix}$</td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><p>CSI report #1</p>
<p>as in Table 6.3.1.1.2-7/7A/7B/8/8A/8B/8C/8D/8E/8F/8G/8H</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;"><p>CSI report #2</p>
<p>as in Table 6.3.1.1.2-7/7A/7B/8/8A/8B/8C/8D/8E/8F/8G/8H</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">…</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;"><p>CSI report #n</p>
<p>as in Table 6.3.1.1.2-7/7A/7B/8/8A/8B/8C/8D/8E/8F/8G/8H</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="2">NOTE: For a CSI report #i containing <span class="math inline">\(N_{i}^{sub}\)</span> CSI sub-reports, where <span class="math inline">\(i \in \{ 1,2,\ldots,n\}\)</span>, all CSI sub-reports within the CSI report #i are mapped to the corresponding segment of the UCI bit sequence of CSI report #i, from upper part to lower part of the segment, in increasing order of CSI sub-report number. CSI sub-report #1, CSI sub-report #2, …, CSI sub-report #<span class="math inline">\(N_{i}^{sub}\)</span> correspond to the CSI sub-reports in increasing order of <em>reportSubConfigId</em>.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

If at least one of the CSI reports for transmission on a PUCCH is of two parts, two UCI bit sequences are generated, $a_{0}^{(1)},a_{1}^{(1)},a_{2}^{(1)},a_{3}^{(1)},...,a_{A^{(1)}-1}^{(1)}$ and $a_{0}^{(2)},a_{1}^{(2)},a_{2}^{(2)},a_{3}^{(2)},...,a_{A^{(2)}-1}^{(2)}$. The CSI fields of all CSI reports, in the order from upper part to lower part in Table 6.3.1.1.2-13, are mapped to the UCI bit sequence $a_{0}^{(1)},a_{1}^{(1)},a_{2}^{(1)},a_{3}^{(1)},...,a_{A^{(1)}-1}^{(1)}$ starting with $a_{0}^{(1)}$. The most significant bit of each field is mapped to the lowest order information bit for that field, e.g. the most significant bit of the first field is mapped to$a_{0}^{(1)}$. The CSI fields of all CSI reports, in the order from upper part to lower part in Table 6.3.1.1.2-14, are mapped to the UCI bit sequence $a_{0}^{(2)},a_{1}^{(2)},a_{2}^{(2)},a_{3}^{(2)},...,a_{A^{(2)}-1}^{(2)}$ starting with $a_{0}^{(2)}$. The most significant bit of each field is mapped to the lowest order information bit for that field, e.g. the most significant bit of the first field is mapped to $a_{0}^{(2)}$. If the length of UCI bit sequence $a_{0}^{(2)},a_{1}^{(2)},a_{2}^{(2)},a_{3}^{(2)},...,a_{A^{(2)}-1}^{(2)}$ is less than 3 bits, zeros shall be appended to the UCI bit sequence until its length equals 3.

Table 6.3.1.1.2-13: Mapping order of CSI reports to UCI bit sequence $a_{0}^{(1)},a_{1}^{(1)},a_{2}^{(1)},a_{3}^{(1)},...,a_{A^{(1)}-1}^{(1)}$,  
with two-part CSI report(s)

<table style="width:89%;">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 19%" />
<col style="width: 69%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>UCI bit sequence</th>
<th style="text-align: center;">CSI report number</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td rowspan="4">$\begin{matrix} a_{0}^{(1)} \\ a_{1}^{(1)} \\ a_{2}^{(1)} \\ a_{3}^{(1)} \\ \ \vdots  \\ a_{A^{(1)}-1}^{(1)} \end{matrix}$</td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><p>CSI report #1 if CSI report #1 is not of two parts, or</p>
<p>CSI report #1, CSI part 1, if CSI report #1 is of two parts,</p>
<p>as in Table 6.3.1.1.2-7/7A/7B/8/8A/8B/8C/8D/8E/8F/8G/8H/9/9A/9B/9C</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;"><p>CSI report #2 if CSI report #2 is not of two parts, or</p>
<p>CSI report #2, CSI part 1, if CSI report #2 is of two parts,</p>
<p>as in Table 6.3.1.1.2-7/7A/7B/8/8A/8B/8C/8D/8E/8F/8G/8H/9/9A/9B/9C</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">…</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;"><p>CSI report #n if CSI report #n is not of two parts, or</p>
<p>CSI report #n, CSI part 1, if CSI report #n is of two parts,</p>
<p>as in Table 6.3.1.1.2-7/7A/7B/8/8A/8B/8C/8D/8E/8F/8G/8H/9/9A/9B/9C</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="2">NOTE: For a CSI report #i containing <span class="math inline">\(N_{i}^{sub}\)</span> CSI sub-reports, where <span class="math inline">\(i \in \{ 1,2,\ldots,n\}\)</span>, either all CSI sub-reports not of two parts or CSI part 1 of all CSI sub-reports of two parts, are mapped to the corresponding segment of the UCI bit sequence of CSI report #i, from upper part to lower part of the segment, in increasing order of CSI sub-report number. CSI sub-report #1, CSI sub-report #2, …, CSI sub-report #<span class="math inline">\(N_{i}^{sub}\)</span> correspond to the CSI sub-reports in increasing order of <em>reportSubConfigId</em>.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

where CSI report \#1, CSI report \#2, …, CSI report \#n in Table 6.3.1.1.2-13 correspond to the CSI reports in increasing order of CSI report priority values according to Clause 5.2.5 of \[6, TS38.214\].

Table 6.3.1.1.2-14: Mapping order of CSI reports to UCI bit sequence $a_{0}^{(2)},a_{1}^{(2)},a_{2}^{(2)},a_{3}^{(2)},...,a_{A^{(2)}-1}^{(2)}$,  
with two-part CSI report(s)

if CSI part 2 exists for CSI report #n</td>
</tr>

if CSI part 2 exists for CSI report #1</td>

if CSI part 2 exists for CSI report #n</td>

<td colspan="2"><p>NOTE: For a CSI report #i containing<span class="math inline">\(\ N_{i}^{sub}\)</span> CSI sub-reports, where <span class="math inline">\(i \in \{ 1,2,\ldots,n\}\)</span>:</p>
<blockquote>
<p>- CSI part 2 widebands of all CSI sub-reports are mapped to the corresponding segment of the UCI bit sequence of CSI report #i, from upper part to lower part of the segment, in increasing order of CSI sub-report number;</p>
<p>- CSI sub-report #1, CSI sub-report #2, …, CSI sub-report #<span class="math inline">\(N_{i}^{sub}\)</span> correspond to the CSI sub‑reports in increasing order of <em>reportSubConfigId</em>.</p>
</blockquote></td>
</tr>
