</table>

where CSI report \#1, CSI report \#2, …, CSI report \#n in Table 6.3.1.1.2-14 correspond to the CSI reports in increasing order of CSI report priority values according to Clause 5.2.5 of \[6, TS38.214\].

##### 6.3.1.1.3 HARQ-ACK/SR and CSI

If none of the CSI reports for transmission on a PUCCH is of two parts, the UCI bit sequence $a_{0},a_{1},a_{2},a_{3},...,a_{A-1}$ is generated according to the following, where $A=O^{\text{ACK}}+O^{\text{SR}}+O^{\text{CSI}}$:

- if there is HARQ-ACK for transmission on the PUCCH, the HARQ-ACK bits are mapped to the UCI bit sequence $a_{0},a_{1},a_{2},a_{3},...,a_{O^{\text{ACK}}-1}$, where $a_{i}=\tilde{o}_{i}^{ACK}$ for $i=0,\ 1,\ ...,\ O^{\text{ACK}}-1$, the HARQ-ACK bit sequence $\tilde{o}_{0}^{ACK},\ \tilde{o}$ is given by Clause 9.1 of \[5, TS38.213\], and $O^{\text{ACK}}$ is number of HARQ-ACK bits; if there is no HARQ-ACK for transmission on the PUCCH, set $O^{\text{ACK}}=0$;

- if there is SR for transmission on the PUCCH, set $a_{i} = {\widetilde{o}}_{i - O^{ACK}}^{SR}$ for $i=\ O^{\text{ACK}},O^{\text{ACK}}+1,...,O^{\text{ACK}}+O^{\text{SR}}-1$, where the SR bit sequence $\tilde{o}_{0}^{SR},\ \tilde{o}$ is given by Clause 9.2.5.1 of \[5, TS 38.213\]; if there is no SR for transmission on the PUCCH, set $O^{\text{SR}}=0$;

- the CSI fields of all CSI reports, in the order from upper part to lower part in Table 6.3.1.1.2-12, are mapped to the UCI bit sequence $a_{O^{\text{ACK}}+O^{\text{SR}}},a_{O^{\text{ACK}}+O^{\text{SR}}+1},...,a_{O^{\text{ACK}}+O^{\text{SR}}+O^{\text{CSI}}-1}$ starting with $a_{O^{\text{ACK}}+O^{\text{SR}}}$, where $O^{\text{CSI}}$ is the number of CSI bits.

If at least one of the CSI reports for transmission on a PUCCH is of two parts, two UCI bit sequences are generated, $a_{0}^{(1)},a_{1}^{(1)},a_{2}^{(1)},a_{3}^{(1)},...,a_{A^{(1)}-1}^{(1)}$ and $a_{0}^{(2)},a_{1}^{(2)},a_{2}^{(2)},a_{3}^{(2)},...,a_{A^{(2)}-1}^{(2)}$, according to the following, where $A^{(1)}=O^{\text{ACK}}+O^{\text{SR}}+O^{\text{CSI-part1}}$ and $A^{(2)}=O^{\text{CSI-part2}}$:

- if there is HARQ-ACK for transmission on the PUCCH, the HARQ-ACK bits are mapped to the UCI bit sequence $a_{0}^{(1)},a_{1}^{(1)},a_{2}^{(1)},a_{3}^{(1)},...,a_{O^{\text{ACK}}-1}^{(1)}$, where $a_{i}^{(1)}=\tilde{o}_{i}^{ACK}$ for $i=0,\ 1,\ ...,\ O^{\text{ACK}}-1$, the HARQ-ACK bit sequence $\tilde{o}_{0}^{ACK},\ \tilde{o}$ is given by Clause 9.1 of \[5, TS38.213\], and $O^{\text{ACK}}$ is number of HARQ-ACK bits; if there is no HARQ-ACK for transmission on the PUCCH, set $O^{\text{ACK}}=0$;

- if there is SR for transmission on the PUCCH, set $a_{i} = {\widetilde{o}}_{i - O^{ACK}}^{SR}$ for $i=\ O^{\text{ACK}},O^{\text{ACK}}+1,...,O^{\text{ACK}}+O^{\text{SR}}-1$, where the SR bit sequence $\tilde{o}_{0}^{SR},\ \tilde{o}$ is given by Clause 9.2.5.1 of \[5, TS 38.213\]; if there is no SR for transmission on the PUCCH, set $O^{\text{SR}}=0$;

- the CSI fields of all CSI reports, in the order from upper part to lower part in Table 6.3.1.1.2-13, are mapped to the UCI bit sequence $a_{O^{\text{ACK}}+O^{\text{SR}}}^{(1)},a_{O^{\text{ACK}}+O^{\text{SR}}+1}^{(1)},...,a_{O^{\text{ACK}}+O^{\text{SR}}+O^{\text{CSI-part1}}-1}^{(1)}$ starting with $a_{O^{\text{ACK}}+O^{\text{SR}}}^{(1)}$, where $O^{\text{CSI-part1}}$ is the number of CSI bits in CSI part 1 of all CSI reports;

- the CSI fields of all CSI reports, in the order from upper part to lower part in Table 6.3.1.1.2-14, are mapped to the UCI bit sequence $a_{0}^{(2)},a_{1}^{(2)},a_{2}^{(2)},a_{3}^{(2)},...,a_{A^{(2)}-1}^{(2)}$ starting with $a_{0}^{(2)}$, where $O^{\text{CSI-part2}}$ is the number of CSI bits in CSI part 2 of all CSI reports. If the length of UCI bit sequence $a_{0}^{(2)},a_{1}^{(2)},a_{2}^{(2)},a_{3}^{(2)},...,a_{A^{(2)}-1}^{(2)}$ is less than 3 bits, zeros shall be appended to the UCI bit sequence until its length equals 3.

##### 6.3.1.1.4 UCI with different priority indexes

If *uci-MuxWithDiffPrio* is configured, and HARQ-ACK bits associated with priority index 0, HARQ-ACK bits associated with priority index 1, and SR associated with priority index 1 if any are transmitted on a PUCCH, two UCI bit sequences are generated, $a_{0}^{(1)},\ a_{1}^{(1)},\ a_{2}^{(1)},\ a_{3}^{(1)},\ \ldots,a_{A^{(1)} - 1}^{(1)}$ and $a_{0}^{(2)},\ a_{1}^{(2)},\ a_{2}^{(2)},\ a_{3}^{(2)},\ \ldots,a_{A^{(2)} - 1}^{(2)}$, according to the following, where $A^{(1)} = O^{ACK - HP} + O^{SR - HP}$ and $A^{(2)} = O^{ACK - LP}$:

- the HARQ-ACK bits associated with priority index 1 are mapped to the UCI bit sequence $a_{0}^{(1)},\ a_{1}^{(1)},\ a_{2}^{(1)},\ a_{3}^{(1)},\ \ldots,a_{O^{ACK - HP} - 1}^{(1)}$, where $a_{i}^{(1)} = {\widetilde{o}}_{i}^{ACK - HP}$ for $i = 0,1,\ldots,O^{ACK - HP} - 1$, the HARQ-ACK bit sequence ${\widetilde{o}}_{0}^{ACK - HP},\ {\widetilde{o}}_{1}^{ACK - HP},\ \ldots,\ {\widetilde{o}}_{O^{ACK - HP} - 1}^{ACK - HP}$ is given by Clause 9.1 of \[5, TS 38.213\], and $O^{ACK - HP}$ is the number of HARQ-ACK bits associated with priority index 1;

- if there is SR associated with priority index 1 for transmission on the PUCCH, set $a_{i}^{(1)} = {\widetilde{o}}_{i - O^{ACK - HP}}^{SR - HP}$ for $i = O^{ACK - HP},O^{ACK - HP} + 1,\ldots,O^{ACK - HP} + O^{SR - HP} - 1$, where the SR bit sequence ${\widetilde{o}}_{0}^{SR - HP},\ {\widetilde{o}}_{1}^{SR - HP},\ \ldots,{\widetilde{o}}_{O^{SR - HP} - 1}^{SR - HP}$ is given by Clause 9.2.5.1 of \[5, TS 38.213\]; if there is no SR associated with priority index 1 for transmission on the PUCCH, set $O^{SR - HP} = 0$;

- the HARQ-ACK bits associated with priority index 0 are mapped to the UCI bit sequence $a_{0}^{(2)},\ a_{1}^{(2)},\ a_{2}^{(2)},\ a_{3}^{(2)},\ \ldots,a_{O^{ACK - LP} - 1}^{(2)}$, where $a_{i}^{(2)} = {\widetilde{o}}_{i}^{ACK - LP}$ for $i = 0,1,\ldots,O^{ACK - LP} - 1$, the HARQ-ACK bit sequence ${\widetilde{o}}_{0}^{ACK - LP},\ {\widetilde{o}}_{1}^{ACK - LP},\ \ldots,\ {\widetilde{o}}_{O^{ACK - LP} - 1}^{ACK - LP}$ is given by Clause 9.1 of \[5, TS 38.213\], and $O^{ACK - LP}$ is the number of HARQ-ACK bits associated with priority index 0.

#### 6.3.1.2 Code block segmentation and CRC attachment

The UCI bit sequence from clause 6.3.1.1 is denoted by $a_{0},a_{1},a_{2},a_{3},...,a_{A-1}$, where $A$ is the payload size. The procedure in Clause 6.3.1.2.1 applies for $A\geq 12$ and the procedure in Clause 6.3.1.2.2 applies for $A\leq 11$.

##### 6.3.1.2.1 UCI encoded by Polar code

If the payload size $A\geq 12$, code block segmentation and CRC attachment is performed according to Clause 5.2.1. If ($A\geq 360$ and $E\geq 1088$) or if $A\geq 1013$, $I_{seg}=1$; otherwise $I_{seg}=0$, where $E$ is the rate matching output sequence length as given in Clauses 6.3.1.4.1 and 6.3.1.4.3.

If $12\leq A\leq 19$, the parity bits $p_{r0},p_{r1},p_{r2},...,p_{r\left(L-1\right)}$ in Clause 5.2.1 are computed by setting $L$ to 6 bits and using the generator polynomial $g_{\text{CRC6}}\left(D\right)$ in Clause 5.1, resulting in the sequence $c_{r0},c_{r1},c_{r2},c_{r3},...,c_{r\left(K_{r}-1\right)}$ where $r$ is the code block number and $K_{r}$ is the number of bits for code block number $r$.

If $A\geq 20$, the parity bits $p_{r0},p_{r1},p_{r2},...,p_{r\left(L-1\right)}$ in Clause 5.2.1 are computed by setting $L$ to 11 bits and using the generator polynomial $g_{\text{CRC11}}\left(D\right)$ in Clause 5.1, resulting in the sequence $c_{r0},c_{r1},c_{r2},c_{r3},...,c_{r\left(K_{r}-1\right)}$ where $r$ is the code block number and $K_{r}$ is the number of bits for code block number $r$.

##### 6.3.1.2.2 UCI encoded by channel coding of small block lengths

If the payload size $A\leq 11$, CRC bits are not attached.

The output bit sequence is denoted by $c_{0},c_{1},c_{2},c_{3},...,c_{K-1}$, where $c_{i}=a_{i}$ for $i=0,\ 1,\ ...,\ A-1$ and $K=A$.

#### 6.3.1.3 Channel coding of UCI

##### 6.3.1.3.1 UCI encoded by Polar code

Information bits are delivered to the channel coding block. They are denoted by $c_{r0},c_{r1},c_{r2},c_{r3},...,c_{r\left(K_{r}-1\right)}$ , where $r$ is the code block number, and $K_{r}$ is the number of bits in code block number $r$. The total number of code blocks is denoted by $C$ and each code block is individually encoded by the following:

If $18\leq K_{r}\leq 25$, the information bits are encoded via Polar coding according to Clause 5.3.1, by setting $n_{\max }=10$, $I_{IL}=0$, $n_{PC}=3$, $n_{PC}^{wm}=1$ if $E_{r}-K_{r}+3>192$ and $n_{PC}^{wm}=0$ if $E_{r}-K_{r}+3\leq 192$, where $E_{r}$ is the rate matching output sequence length as given in Clauses 6.3.1.4.1 and 6.3.1.4.3.

If $K_{r}>30$, the information bits are encoded via Polar coding according to Clause 5.3.1, by setting $n_{\max }=10$, $I_{IL}=0$, $n_{PC}=0$, and $n_{PC}^{wm}=0$.

After encoding the bits are denoted by $d_{r0},d_{r1},d_{r2},d_{r3},...,d_{r(N_{r}-1)}$, where $N_{r}$ is the number of coded bits in code block number $r$.

##### 6.3.1.3.2 UCI encoded by channel coding of small block lengths

Information bits are delivered to the channel coding block. They are denoted by $c_{0},c_{1},c_{2},c_{3},...,c_{K-1}$, where $K$ is the number of bits.

The information bits are encoded according to Clause 5.3.3.

After encoding the bits are denoted by $d_{0},d_{1},d_{2},d_{3},...,d_{N-1}$, where $N$ is the number of coded bits.

#### 6.3.1.4 Rate matching

For PUCCH formats 2/3/4, the total rate matching output sequence length $E_{\text{tot}}$ is given by Table 6.3.1.4-1, where $N_{\text{symb, UCI}}^{\text{PUCCH, 2}}$ , $N_{\text{symb, UCI}}^{\text{PUCCH, 3}}$, and $N_{\text{symb, UCI}}^{\text{PUCCH, 4}}$ are the number of symbols carrying UCI for PUCCH formats 2/3/4 respectively; $N_{\text{PRB}}^{\text{PUCCH, 2}}$, $N_{\text{PRB}}^{\text{PUCCH, 3}}$ and $N_{PRB}^{PUCCH,4}\$are the number of PRBs that are determined by the UE for PUCCH formats 2/3/4 transmission respectively according to Clause 9.2 of \[5, TS38.213\]; and $N_{SF}^{PUCCH,2}$, $N_{SF}^{PUCCH,3}$, and $N_{\text{SF}}^{\text{PUCCH, 4}}$ are the spreading factors for PUCCH format 2, PUCCH format 3, and PUCCH format 4, respectively.

Table 6.3.1.4-1: Total rate matching output sequence length $E_{\text{tot}}$

<table style="width:94%;">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 25%" />
<col style="width: 36%" />
<col style="width: 32%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th rowspan="2"><em>PUCCH format</em></th>
<th colspan="2"><em>Modulation order</em></th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th>QPSK</th>
<th>π/2-BPSK</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td>PUCCH format 2</td>
<td><span class="math display">\[16 \bullet N_{symb,UCI}^{PUCCH,2} \bullet N_{PRB}^{PUCCH,2}/N_{SF}^{PUCCH,2}\]</span></td>
<td>N/A</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>PUCCH format 3</td>
<td><span class="math display">\[24 \bullet N_{symb,UCI}^{PUCCH,3} \bullet N_{PRB}^{PUCCH,3}/N_{SF}^{PUCCH,3}\]</span></td>
<td><span class="math display">\[12 \bullet N_{symb,UCI}^{PUCCH,3} \bullet N_{PRB}^{PUCCH,3}/N_{SF}^{PUCCH,3}\]</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>PUCCH format 4</td>
<td><span class="math display">\[\frac{24 \bullet N_{symb,UCI}^{PUCCH,4} \bullet N_{PRB}^{PUCCH,4}}{N_{SF}^{PUCCH,4}}\]</span></td>
<td><span class="math display">\[\frac{12 \bullet N_{symb,UCI}^{PUCCH,4} \bullet N_{PRB}^{PUCCH,4}}{N_{SF}^{PUCCH,4}}\]</span></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

##### 6.3.1.4.1 UCI encoded by Polar code

The input bit sequence to rate matching is $d_{r0},d_{r1},d_{r2},d_{r3},...,d_{r(N_{r}-1)}$ where $r$ is the code block number, and $N_{r}$ is the number of coded bits in code block number $r$.

Table 6.3.1.4.1-1: Rate matching output sequence length $E_{\text{UCI}}$

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 21%" />
<col style="width: 18%" />
<col style="width: 60%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: left;">UCI(s) for transmission on a PUCCH</th>
<th style="text-align: left;">UCI for encoding</th>
<th>Value of $E_{\text{UCI}}$</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: left;">HARQ-ACK</td>
<td style="text-align: left;">HARQ-ACK</td>
<td>$E_{\text{UCI}}=E_{\text{tot}}$</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: left;">HARQ-ACK, SR</td>
<td style="text-align: left;">HARQ-ACK, SR</td>
<td>$E_{\text{UCI}}=E_{\text{tot}}$</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: left;"><p>CSI</p>
<p>(CSI not of two parts)</p></td>
<td style="text-align: left;">CSI</td>
<td>$E_{\text{UCI}}=E_{\text{tot}}$</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: left;"><p>HARQ-ACK, CSI</p>
<p>(CSI not of two parts)</p></td>
<td style="text-align: left;">HARQ-ACK, CSI</td>
<td>$E_{\text{UCI}}=E_{\text{tot}}$</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: left;"><p>HARQ-ACK, SR, CSI</p>
<p>(CSI not of two parts)</p></td>
<td style="text-align: left;">HARQ-ACK, SR, CSI</td>
<td>$E_{\text{UCI}}=E_{\text{tot}}$</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td rowspan="2" style="text-align: left;"><p>CSI</p>
<p>(CSI of two parts)</p></td>
<td style="text-align: left;">CSI part 1</td>
<td>$E_{\text{UCI}}=\min \left(E_{\text{tot}},\left\lceil \left(O^{\text{CSI-part1}}+L\right)/R_{\text{UCI}}^{\text{max}}/Q_{m}\right\rceil \cdot Q_{m}{}\right)$</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: left;">CSI part 2</td>
<td>$E_{\text{UCI}}=E_{\text{tot}}-\min \left(E_{\text{tot}},\left\lceil \left(O^{\text{CSI-part1}}+L\right)/R_{\text{UCI}}^{\text{max}}/Q_{m}\right\rceil \cdot Q_{m}{}\right)$</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td rowspan="2" style="text-align: left;"><p>HARQ-ACK, CSI</p>
<p>(CSI of two parts)</p></td>
<td style="text-align: left;">HARQ-ACK, CSI part 1</td>
<td>$E_{\text{UCI}}=\min \left(E_{\text{tot}},\left\lceil \left(O^{\text{ACK}}+O^{\text{CSI-part1}}+L\right)/R_{\text{UCI}}^{\text{max}}/Q_{m}\right\rceil \cdot Q_{m}{}\right)$</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: left;">CSI part 2</td>
<td>$E_{\text{UCI}}=E_{\text{tot}}-\min \left(E_{\text{tot}},\left\lceil \left(O^{\text{ACK}}+O^{\text{CSI-part1}}+L\right)/R_{\text{UCI}}^{\text{max}}/Q_{m}\right\rceil \cdot Q_{m}{}\right)$</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td rowspan="2" style="text-align: left;"><p>HARQ-ACK, SR, CSI</p>
<p>(CSI of two parts)</p></td>
<td style="text-align: left;">HARQ-ACK, SR, CSI part 1</td>
<td>$E_{\text{UCI}}=\min \left(E_{\text{tot}},\left\lceil \left(O^{\text{ACK}}+O^{\text{SR}}+O^{\text{CSI-part1}}+L\right)/R_{\text{UCI}}^{\text{max}}/Q_{m}\right\rceil \cdot Q_{m}{}\right)$</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: left;">CSI part 2</td>
<td>$E_{\text{UCI}}=E_{\text{tot}}-\min \left(E_{\text{tot}},\left\lceil \left(O^{\text{ACK}}+O^{\text{SR}}+O^{\text{CSI-part1}}+L\right)/R_{\text{UCI}}^{\text{max}}/Q_{m}\right\rceil \cdot Q_{m}{}\right)$</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

Rate matching is performed according to Clause 5.4.1 by setting $I_{BIL}=1$ and the rate matching output sequence length to $E_{r}=\left\lfloor E_{\text{UCI}}/C_{\text{UCI}}\right\rfloor$, where $C_{\text{UCI}}$ is the number of code blocks for UCI determined according to Clause 6.3.1.2.1 and the value of $E_{\text{UCI}}$ is given by Table 6.3.1.4.1-1:

- $O^{\text{ACK}}$ is the number of bits for HARQ-ACK for transmission on the current PUCCH;

- $O^{\text{SR}}$ is the number of bits for SR for transmission on the current PUCCH;

- $O^{\text{CSI-part1}}$ is the number of bits for CSI part 1 for transmission on the current PUCCH;

- $O^{\text{CSI-part2}}$ is the number of bits for CSI part 2 for transmission on the current PUCCH;

- if $A\geq 360$, $L=11$ ; otherwise, $L$ is the number of CRC bits determined according to Clause 6.3.1.2.1, where $A$ equals $O^{\text{CSI-part1}}$ for "CSI (CSI of two parts)", equals $O^{\text{ACK}}+O^{\text{CSI-part1}}$ for "HARQ-ACK, CSI (CSI of two parts)", and equals $O^{\text{ACK}}+O^{\text{SR}}+O^{\text{CSI-part1}}$ for "HARQ-ACK, SR, CSI (CSI of two parts)" respectively in Table 6.3.1.4.1-1;

- $R_{\text{UCI}}^{\text{max}}$ is the configured maximum PUCCH coding rate;

- $E_{\text{tot}}$ is given by Table 6.3.1.4-1.

The output bit sequence after rate matching is denoted as $f_{r0},f_{r1},f_{r2},...,f_{r(E_{r}-1)}$ where $E_{r}$ is the length of rate matching output sequence in code block number $r$.

##### 6.3.1.4.2 UCI encoded by channel coding of small block lengths

The input bit sequence to rate matching is $d_{0},d_{1},d_{2},...,d_{N-1}$.

The value of $E_{\text{UCI}}$ is determined according to Table 6.3.1.4.1-1 by setting $L=0$.

Rate matching is performed according to Clause 5.4.3 by setting the rate matching output sequence length $E=E_{\text{UCI}}$.

The output bit sequence after rate matching is denoted as $f_{0},f_{1},f_{2},...,f_{E-1}$.

##### 6.3.1.4.3 UCI with different priority indexes encoded by Polar code

The following procedure in this clause 6.3.1.4.3 applies if *uci-MuxWithDiffPrio* is configured, and HARQ-ACK bits associated with priority index 0, HARQ-ACK bits associated with priority index 1 and SR associated with priority index 1 if any are transmitted on a PUCCH.

The input bit sequence to rate matching is $d_{r0},d_{r1},d_{r2},d_{r3},\ldots,d_{r(N_{r} - 1)}$ where $r$ is the code block number, and $N_{r}$ is the number of coded bits in code block number$\ r$.

Table 6.3.1.4.3-1: Rate matching output sequence length $\mathbf{E}_{\mathbf{UCI}}$ for UCIs with different priority indexes

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 23%" />
<col style="width: 20%" />
<col style="width: 56%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: left;">UCIs for transmission on a PUCCH</th>
<th style="text-align: left;">UCI for encoding</th>
<th>Value of <span class="math inline">\(\mathbf{E}_{\mathbf{UCI}}\)</span></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td rowspan="2" style="text-align: left;">HARQ-ACK of priority index 1, HARQ-ACK of priority index 0</td>
<td style="text-align: left;">HARQ-ACK of priority index 1</td>
<td><span class="math display">\[E_{UCI} = min\left( E_{tot},\left\lceil \left( O^{ACK - HP} + L \right)/R_{UCI}^{max - HP}/Q_{m} \right\rceil \bullet Q_{m} \right)\]</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: left;">HARQ-ACK of priority index 0</td>
<td><span class="math display">\[E_{UCI} = E_{tot} - min\left( E_{tot},\left\lceil \left( O^{ACK - HP} + L \right)/R_{UCI}^{max - HP}/Q_{m} \right\rceil \bullet Q_{m} \right)\]</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td rowspan="2" style="text-align: left;">HARQ-ACK of priority index 1, SR of priority index 1, HARQ-ACK of priority index 0</td>
<td style="text-align: left;">HARQ-ACK of priority index 1, SR of priority index 1</td>
<td><span class="math display">\[E_{UCI} = min\left( E_{tot},\left\lceil \left( O^{ACK - HP} + O^{SR - HP} + L \right)/R_{UCI}^{max - HP}/Q_{m} \right\rceil \bullet Q_{m} \right)\]</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: left;">HARQ-ACK of priority index 0</td>
<td><span class="math display">\[E_{UCI} = E_{tot} - min\left( E_{tot},\left\lceil \left( O^{ACK - HP} + O^{SR - HP} + L \right)/R_{UCI}^{max - HP}/Q_{m} \right\rceil \bullet Q_{m} \right)\]</span></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

Rate matching is performed according to Clause 5.4.1 by setting $I_{BIL} = 1$ and the rate matching output sequence length to $E_{r} = \left\lfloor \frac{E_{UCI}}{C_{UCI}} \right\rfloor$, where $C_{UCI}$ is the number of code blocks for UCI determined according to Clause 6.3.1.2.1 and the value of $E_{UCI}$ is given by Table 6.3.1.4.3-1:

- $O^{ACK - HP}$ is the number of bits for HARQ-ACK associated with priority index 1 for transmission on the current PUCCH;

- $O^{SR - HP}$ is the number of bits for SR associated with priority index 1 for transmission on the current PUCCH;

- if $A \geq 360$, $L$=11; otherwise, $L$ is the number of CRC bits determined according to clause 6.3.1.2.1, where $A$ equals $O^{ACK - HP}$ for the case of "HARQ-ACK of priority index 1, HARQ-ACK of priority index 0", and equals $O^{ACK - HP} + O^{SR - HP}$ for the case of "HARQ-ACK of priority index 1, SR of priority index 1, HARQ-ACK of priority index 0" respectively in Table 6.3.1.4.3-1;

- $R_{UCI}^{max - HP}$ is the configured maximum PUCCH coding rate of priority index 1;

- $E_{tot}$ is given by Table 6.3.1.4-1.

The output bit sequence after rate matching is denoted as $f_{r0},f_{r1},f_{r2},\ldots,f_{r(E_{r} - 1)}$ where $E_{r}$ is the length of rate matching output sequence in code block number$\ r$.

##### 6.3.1.4.4 UCI with different priority indexes encoded by channel coding of small block lengths

The following procedure in this clause 6.3.1.4.4 applies if *uci-MuxWithDiffPrio* is configured, and HARQ-ACK bits associated with priority index 0, HARQ-ACK bits associated with priority index 1 and SR associated with priority index 1 if any are transmitted on a PUCCH.

The input bit sequence to rate matching is $d_{0},d_{1},d_{2},\ldots,d_{N - 1}$.

The value of $E_{UCI}$ is determined according to Table 6.3.1.4.3-1 by setting $L$=0.

Rate matching is performed according to Clause 5.4.3 by setting the rate matching output sequence length ${E = E}_{UCI}$.

The output bit sequence after rate matching is denoted as $f_{0},f_{1},f_{2},\ldots,f_{E - 1}$.

#### 6.3.1.5 Code block concatenation

The input bit sequence for the code block concatenation block are the sequences$f_{r0},f_{r1},f_{r2},...,f_{r(E_{r}-1)}$, for $r=0,...,C-1$ and where $E_{r}$ is the number of rate matched bits for the $r$-th code block.

Code block concatenation is performed according to Clause 5.5.

The bits after code block concatenation are denoted by$g_{0},g_{1},g_{2},g_{3},...,g_{G'-1}$, where $G'=\left\lfloor E_{\text{UCI}}/C_{\text{UCI}}\right\rfloor \cdot C_{\text{UCI}}$ with the values of $E_{\text{UCI}}$ and $C_{\text{UCI}}$ given in Clauses 6.3.1.4.1 and 6.3.1.4.3. Let $G$ be the total number of coded bits for transmission and $G=G'+\mod \left(E_{\text{UCI}},C_{\text{UCI}}\right)$. Set $g_{i}=0$ for $i=G',G'+1,...,G-1$.

#### 6.3.1.6 Multiplexing of coded UCI bits to PUCCH

If CSI of two parts or UCIs with different priority indexes are transmitted on a PUCCH, the coded bits corresponding to UCI bit sequence $a_{0}^{(1)},a_{1}^{(1)},a_{2}^{(1)},a_{3}^{(1)},...,a_{A^{(1)}-1}^{(1)}$ is denoted by $g_{0}^{(1)},g_{1}^{(1)},g_{2}^{(1)},g_{3}^{(1)},...,g_{G^{(1)}-1}^{(1)}$and the coded bits corresponding to UCI bit sequence $a_{0}^{(2)},a_{1}^{(2)},a_{2}^{(2)},a_{3}^{(2)},...,a_{A^{(2)}-1}^{(2)}$ is denoted by $g_{0}^{(2)},g_{1}^{(2)},g_{2}^{(2)},g_{3}^{(2)},...,g_{G^{(2)}-1}^{(2)}$.

For PUCCH format 2 when *uci-MuxWithDiffPrio* is configured, the coded bit sequence $g_{0},g_{1},g_{2},g_{3},\ldots,g_{G - 1}$ is generated for UCIs with different priority indexes by setting $g_{i} = g_{i}^{(1)}$ for $i = 0,1,\ldots,G^{(1)} - 1$, and setting $g_{i} = g_{i - G^{(1)}}^{(2)}$ for $i = G^{(1)},G^{(1)} + 1,\ldots,{G^{(1)} + G}^{(2)} - 1$.

For PUCCH format 3/4, the coded bit sequence $g_{0},g_{1},g_{2},g_{3},...,g_{G-1}$, where $G=G^{(1)}+G^{(2)}$, is generated according to the following.

Table 6.3.1.6-1: PUCCH DMRS and UCI symbols

| PUCCH duration (symbols) | PUCCH DMRS symbol indices | Number of UCI symbol indices sets $N_{\text{UCI}}^{\text{set}}$ | 1<sup>st</sup> UCI symbol indices set $S_{\text{UCI}}^{(1)}$ | 2<sup>nd</sup> UCI symbol indices set $S_{\text{UCI}}^{(2)}$ | 3<sup>rd</sup> UCI symbol indices set $S_{\text{UCI}}^{(3)}$ |
|----|----|:--:|:--:|----|----|
| 4 | {1} | 2 | {0,2} | {3} | \- |
| 4 | {0,2} | 1 | {1,3} | \- | \- |
| 5 | {0, 3} | 1 | {1, 2, 4} | \- | \- |
| 6 | {1, 4} | 1 | {0, 2, 3, 5} | \- | \- |
| 7 | {1, 4} | 2 | {0, 2, 3, 5} | {6} | \- |
| 8 | {1, 5} | 2 | {0, 2, 4, 6} | {3, 7} | \- |
| 9 | {1, 6} | 2 | {0, 2, 5, 7} | {3, 4, 8} | \- |
| 10 | {2, 7} | 2 | {1, 3, 6, 8} | {0, 4, 5, 9} | \- |
| 10 | {1, 3, 6, 8} | 1 | {0,2,4,5,7,9} | \- | \- |
| 11 | {2, 7} | 3 | {1,3,6,8} | {0,4,5,9} | {10} |
| 11 | {1,3,6,9} | 1 | {0,2,4,5,7,8,10} | \- | \- |
| 12 | {2, 8} | 3 | {1,3,7,9} | {0,4,6,10} | {5, 11} |
| 12 | {1,4,7,10} | 1 | {0,2,3,5,6,8,9,11} | \- | \- |
| 13 | {2, 9} | 3 | {1,3,8,10} | {0,4,7,11} | {5,6,12} |
| 13 | {1,4,7,11} | 2 | {0,2,3,5,6,8,10,12} | {9} | \- |
| 14 | {3, 10} | 3 | {2,4,9,11} | {1,5,8,12} | {0,6,7,13} |
| 14 | {1,5,8,12} | 2 | {0,2,4,6,7,9,11,13} | {3, 10} | \- |

Denote $s_{l}$ as UCI OFDM symbol index. Denote $N_{\text{UCI}}^{\text{(i)}}$ as the number of elements in UCI symbol indices set $S_{\text{UCI}}^{(i)}$ for $i=1,...,N_{\text{UCI}}^{\text{set}}$, where $S_{\text{UCI}}^{(i)}$ and $N_{\text{UCI}}^{\text{set}}$ are given by Table 6.3.1.6-1 according to the PUCCH duration and the PUCCH DMRS configuration. Denote $N_{\text{symb, UCI}}^{\text{PUCCH,}}=\sum_{i=1}^{N_{\text{UCI}}^{\text{set}}}{N_{\text{UCI}}^{(i)}}$ as the number of OFDM symbols carrying UCI in the PUCCH. Denote $Q_{m}$ as the modulation order of the PUCCH.

For PUCCH formats 3/4, set $N_{\text{UCI}}^{\text{symbol}} = 12 \cdot N_{\text{PRB}}^{\text{PUCCH,s}}/N_{\text{SF}}^{\text{PUCCH,}s}$ , where $N_{\text{PRB}}^{\text{PUCCH,s}}$ is the number of PRBs that is determined by the UE for the corresponding PUCCH format transmission according to Clause 9.2 of \[5, TS 38.213\], and $N_{\text{SF}}^{\text{PUCCH,}s}$ is the spreading factor for the corresponding PUCCH format \[4, TS 38.211\], where $s \in \left\{ 3,4 \right\}$.

Find the smallest$j>0$ such that $\left(\sum_{i=1}^{j}{N_{\text{UCI}}^{\text{(i)}}}\right)\cdot N_{\text{UCI}}^{\text{symbol}}\cdot Q_{m}\geq G^{(1)}$.

Set $n_{1}=0$;

Set $n_{2}=0$;

Set $\bar{N}_{\text{UCI}}^{\text{symbol}}=\left\lfloor \left(G^{(1)}-\left(\sum_{i=1}^{j-1}{N_{\text{UCI}}^{\text{(i)}}}\right)\cdot N_{\text{UCI}}^{\text{symbol}}\cdot Q_{m}\right)/\left(N_{\text{UCI}}^{(j)}\cdot Q_{m}\right)\right\rfloor$;

Set $M=\mod \left(\left(G^{(1)}-\left(\sum_{i=1}^{j-1}{N_{\text{UCI}}^{\text{(i)}}}\right)\cdot N_{\text{UCI}}^{\text{symbol}}\cdot Q_{m}\right)/Q_{m},N_{\text{UCI}}^{(j)}\right)$;

for $l=0$ to $N_{\text{symb, UCI}}^{\text{PUCCH,}}-1$

if $s_{l}\in \cup_{i=1}^{j-1}S_{\text{UCI}}^{(i)}$

for $k=0$ to $N_{\text{UCI}}^{\text{symbol}}-1$

for $v=0$ to $Q_{m}-1$

$$\bar{g}_{l,k,v}=g_{n_{1}}^{(1)}$$;

$$n_{1}=n_{1}+1$$;

end for

end for

elseif $s_{l}\in S_{\text{UCI}}^{(j)}$

if $M>0$

$$\gamma =1$$;

else

$$\gamma =0$$;

end if

$$M=M-1$$;

for $k=0$ to $\bar{N}_{\text{UCI}}^{\text{symbol}}+\gamma -1$

for $v=0$ to $Q_{m}-1$

$$\bar{g}_{l,k,v}=g_{n_{1}}^{(1)}$$;

$$n_{1}=n_{1}+1$$;

end for

end for

for $k=\bar{N}_{\text{UCI}}^{\text{symbol}}+\gamma$ to $N_{\text{UCI}}^{\text{symbol}}-1$

for $v=0$ to $Q_{m}-1$

$$\bar{g}_{l,k,v}=g_{n_{2}}^{(2)}$$;

$$n_{2}=n_{2}+1$$;

end for

end for

else

for $k=0$ to $N_{\text{UCI}}^{\text{symbol}}-1$

for $v=0$ to $Q_{m}-1$

$$\bar{g}_{l,k,v}=g_{n_{2}}^{(2)}$$;

$$n_{2}=n_{2}+1$$;

end for

end for

end if

end for

Set $n=0$

for $l=0$ to $N_{\text{symb, UCI}}^{\text{PUCCH,}}-1$

for $k=0$ to $N_{\text{UCI}}^{\text{symbol}}-1$

for $v=0$ to $Q_{m}-1$

$$g_{n}=\bar{g}_{l,k,v}$$;

$$n=n+1$$;

end for

end for

end for

### 6.3.2 Uplink control information on PUSCH

The following clauses 6.3.2.2, 6.3.2.3, and 6.3.2.5 apply regardless of whether the higher layer parameter *uci-MuxWithDiffPrio* is configured or not. The following clauses 6.3.2.1, 6.3.2.4, and 6.3.2.6 apply by assuming *uci-MuxWithDiffPrio* is not configured, or *uci-MuxWithDiffPrio* is configured and the UCIs for transmission on a PUSCH are of the same priority index, unless stated otherwise.

If the UE is configured with a PUCCH-SCell, *uci-MuxWithDiffPrio* is replaced by *uci-MuxWithDiffPrioSecondaryPUCCHgroup* for the secondary PUCCH group in this clause*.*

#### 6.3.2.1 UCI bit sequence generation

##### 6.3.2.1.1 HARQ-ACK

If HARQ-ACK bits are transmitted on a PUSCH, the UCI bit sequence $a_{0},a_{1},a_{2},a_{3},...,a_{A-1}$ is determined as follows:

- If UCI is transmitted on PUSCH without UL-SCH and the UCI includes CSI part 1 without CSI part 2,

- if there is no HARQ-ACK bit given by Clause 9.1 of \[5, TS 38.213\], set $a_{0}=0$, $a_{1}=0$, and $A=2$;

- if there is only one HARQ-ACK bit $\tilde{o}_{0}^{ACK}$ given by Clause 9.1 of \[5, TS 38.213\], set $a_{0}=\tilde{o}_{0}^{ACK}$, $a_{1}=0$, and $A=2$;

- otherwise, set $a_{i}=\tilde{o}_{i}^{ACK}$ for $i=0,\ 1,\ ...,\ O^{\text{ACK}}-1$ and $A=O^{\text{ACK}}$, where the HARQ-ACK bit sequence $\tilde{o}_{0}^{ACK},\ \tilde{o}$ is given by Clause 9.1 of \[5, TS 38.213\].

##### 6.3.2.1.2 CSI

If *cqi-BitsPerSubband* is configured, this Clause 6.3.2.1.2 applies by taking Subband CQI as Subband differential CQI and replacing the corresponding number of bits 2 by 4.

If *CSI-ReportSubConfig* is configured, for a corresponding CSI sub-report, the bitwidth of a CSI field of the CSI sub-report is determined following the procedure in this clause 6.3.2.1.2 by taking configurations in *CSI-ReportSubConfig* when applicable. If *CSI-ReportSubConfig* configures a list of CSI-RS resource IDs, for the determination of the bitwidth of a CRI field, the value of $K_{s}^{CSI - RS}$ is the number of CSI-RS resources configured in the corresponding *CSI-ReportSubConfig*.

The bitwidth for PMI of *codebookType=typeI-SinglePanel, codebookType=typeI-SinglePanel-r19,* *codebookType=typeI-MultiPanel* and *codebookType=typeI-MultiPanel-r19* is specified in Clause 6.3.1.1.2.

The bitwidth for RI/LI/CQI/CRI of *codebookType=typeI-SinglePanel, codebookType=typeI-SinglePanel-r19,* *codebookType=typeI-MultiPanel* and *codebookType=typeI-MultiPanel-r19* is specified in Clause 6.3.1.1.2.

The bitwidth for PMI/RI/LI/CQI/CRI with 1 CSI-RS port is specified in Clause 6.3.1.1.2.

The bitwidth for PMI of *codebookType=typeII* is provided in Tables 6.3.2.1.2-1, where the values of $\left(N_{1},N_{2}\right)$, $\left(O_{1},O_{2}\right)$, $L$, $N_{\text{PSK}}$, $M_{1}$, $M_{2}$, and $K^{\left(2\right)}$ are given by Clause 5.2.2.2.3 in \[6, TS 38.214\].

Table 6.3.2.1.2-1: PMI of *codebookType=* *typeII*

<td colspan="19">Note: the bitwidth for <span class="math inline">\({{\{ i}_{1,7,l}\}}_{l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon}\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\({{\{ i}_{2,4,l}\}}_{l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon}\)</span> and <span class="math inline">\({{\{ i}_{2,5,l}\}}_{l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon}\)</span> shown in Table 6.3.2.1.2-1A is the total bitwidth of <span class="math inline">\({\{ i}_{1,7,l}\}\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\({\{ i}_{2,4,l}\}\)</span> and <span class="math inline">\({\{ i}_{2,5,l}\}\)</span> up to Rank = <span class="math inline">\(\upsilon\)</span>, respectively, and the corresponding per layer bitwidths are <span class="math inline">\(2LM_{\upsilon}\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\(3\left( K_{l}^{NZ} - 1 \right)\)</span>, and 4<span class="math inline">\(\left( K_{l}^{NZ} - 1 \right)\)</span>, (i.e., 1, 3, and 4 bits for each respective indicator elements <span class="math inline">\(k_{l,i,f}^{(3)}\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\(k_{l,i,f}^{(2)}\)</span>, and <span class="math inline">\(c_{l,i,f}\)</span>, respectively), where <span class="math inline">\(K_{l}^{NZ}\)</span> as defined in Clause 5.2.2.2.5 in TS 38.214 [6] is the number of nonzero coefficients for layer <span class="math inline">\(l\)</span> such that <span class="math inline">\(K^{NZ} = \sum_{l = 1}^{\upsilon}K_{l}^{NZ}\)</span>.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

The bitwidth for PMI of *codebookType=typeII-CJT* is provided in Tables 6.3.2.1.2-1B, where the values of $(N_{1},\ N_{2})$, $(O_{1},\ O_{2})$, $O_{3}$, $K^{NZ}$, $N_{3}$, $N_{0}$, $L_{n}$, $\sigma(n)$ and $\left\{ M_{l} \right\}_{l = 1,\ldots,\ \upsilon}$ are given by Clause 5.2.2.2.8 in \[6, TS 38.214\].

<td colspan="11" style="text-align: center;">NOTE: the bitwidth for <span class="math inline">\({{\{ i}_{1,7,l}\}}_{l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon}\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\({{\{ i}_{2,4,l}\}}_{l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon}\)</span> and <span class="math inline">\({{\{ i}_{2,5,l}\}}_{l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon}\)</span> shown in Table 6.3.2.1.2-1B is the total bitwidth of <span class="math inline">\({\{ i}_{1,7,l}\}\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\({\{ i}_{2,4,l}\}\)</span> and <span class="math inline">\({\{ i}_{2,5,l}\}\)</span> up to Rank = <span class="math inline">\(\upsilon\)</span>, respectively, and the corresponding per layer bitwidths are <span class="math inline">\(2\sum_{n = 1}^{N_{0}}L_{\sigma(n)}M_{\upsilon}\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\(3\left( K_{l}^{NZ} - 1 \right)\)</span>, and 4<span class="math inline">\(\left( K_{l}^{NZ} - 1 \right)\)</span>, (i.e., 1, 3, and 4 bits for each respective indicator elements <span class="math inline">\(k_{l,i,f,j}^{(3)}\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\(k_{l,i,f,j}^{(2)}\)</span>, and <span class="math inline">\(c_{l,i,f,j}\)</span>, respectively), where <span class="math inline">\(K_{l}^{NZ}\)</span> as defined in Clause 5.2.2.2.8 in [6, TS 38.214] is the number of nonzero coefficients for layer <span class="math inline">\(l\)</span> such that <span class="math inline">\(K^{NZ} = \sum_{l = 1}^{\upsilon}K_{l}^{NZ}\)</span>.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

The bitwidth for PMI of *codebookType=typeII-Doppler* and *codebookType=typeII-Doppler-r19* is provided in Tables 6.3.2.1.2-1C, where the values of $(N_{1},\ N_{2})$, $(O_{1},\ O_{2})$, $L$, $K^{NZ}$, $N_{3}$, $N_{4}$, *Q* and $\left\{ M_{l} \right\}_{l = 1,\ldots,\ \upsilon}$ are given by Clause 5.2.2.2.10 and 5.2.2.2.11a in \[6, TS 38.214\].

<td colspan="21">NOTE: the bitwidth for <span class="math inline">\({{\{ i}_{1,7,l}\}}_{l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon}\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\({{\{ i}_{2,4,l}\}}_{l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon}\)</span> and <span class="math inline">\({{\{ i}_{2,5,l}\}}_{l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon}\)</span> shown in Table 6.3.2.1.2-1C is the total bitwidth of <span class="math inline">\({\{ i}_{1,7,l}\}\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\({\{ i}_{2,4,l}\}\)</span> and <span class="math inline">\({\{ i}_{2,5,l}\}\)</span> up to Rank = <span class="math inline">\(\upsilon\)</span>, respectively, and the corresponding per layer bitwidths are <span class="math inline">\(2LM_{\upsilon}Q\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\(3\left( K_{l}^{NZ} - 1 \right)\)</span>, and 4<span class="math inline">\(\left( K_{l}^{NZ} - 1 \right)\)</span>, (i.e., 1, 3, and 4 bits for each respective indicator elements <span class="math inline">\(k_{l,i,f,\tau}^{(3)}\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\(k_{l,i,f,\tau}^{(2)}\)</span>, and <span class="math inline">\(c_{l,i,f,\tau}\)</span>, respectively), where <span class="math inline">\(K_{l}^{NZ}\)</span> as defined in Clause 5.2.2.2.10 in [6, TS 38.214] is the number of nonzero coefficients for layer <span class="math inline">\(l\)</span> such that <span class="math inline">\(K^{NZ} = \sum_{l = 1}^{\upsilon}K_{l}^{NZ}\)</span>.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

The bitwidth for PMI of *codebookType= typeII-PortSelection* is provided in Tables 6.3.2.1.2-2, where the values of $P_{CSI-RS}$, $d$, $L$, $N_{\text{PSK}}$, $M_{1}$, $M_{2}$, and $K^{\left(2\right)}$ are given by Clause 5.2.2.2.4 in \[6, TS 38.214\].

</tr>

</table>

If *CSI-ReportSubConfig* is configured, for a corresponding CSI sub-report, the mapping order of CSI fields of one CSI sub-report is determined following the procedure in this clause 6.3.2.1.2, by replacing CSI report \#n in the following Tables 6.3.2.1.2-3 and 6.3.2.1.2-4 with CSI sub-report \#n, and taking only Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1/1A/1B/2/2A/3/4 for the determination of the bitwidth of a CSI field.

The bitwidth for PMI of *codebookType=typeII-CJT-PortSelection* is provided in Tables 6.3.2.1.2-2C, where the values of $P_{CSI - RS}$,$\ \left\{ K_{1,n} \right\}$,$\ K^{NZ}$, $N_{3}$, $N$, $M$ and $\sigma(n)$ are given by Clause 5.2.2.2.9 in \[6, TS 38.214\].

<td colspan="2"><span class="math display">\[\left\lceil \log_{2}\begin{pmatrix} P_{CSI - RS}/2 \\ K_{1}/2 \end{pmatrix} \right\rceil\]</span></td>
<td colspan="2"><span class="math inline">\(\left\lceil \log_{2}(N - 1) \right\rceil\)</span> if <em>N &gt; M=2</em>, N/A otherwise</td>
<td colspan="2"><span class="math display">\[\left\lceil \log_{2}(K_{1}M) \right\rceil\]</span></td>
<td colspan="3"><span class="math display">\[\left\lceil \log_{2}(K_{1}M) \right\rceil\]</span></td>
<td colspan="2"><span class="math display">\[\left\lceil \log_{2}(K_{1}M) \right\rceil\]</span></td>
<td><span class="math display">\[\left\lceil \log_{2}{(K}_{1}M) \right\rceil\]</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td rowspan="2"></td>
<td colspan="12">Information fields <span class="math inline">\(\mathbf{X}_{\mathbf{2}}\)</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><span class="math display">\[\mathbf{i}_{\mathbf{2,3,1}}\]</span></td>
<td colspan="2"><span class="math display">\[\mathbf{i}_{\mathbf{2,3,2}}\]</span></td>
<td colspan="2"><span class="math display">\[\mathbf{i}_{\mathbf{2,3,3}}\]</span></td>
<td colspan="2"><span class="math display">\[\mathbf{i}_{\mathbf{2,3,4}}\]</span></td>
<td><span class="math display">\[{\mathbf{\{ i}_{\mathbf{2,4,l}}\mathbf{\}}}_{\mathbf{l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon}}\]</span></td>
<td colspan="2"><span class="math display">\[{\mathbf{\{ i}_{\mathbf{2,5,l}}\mathbf{\}}}_{\mathbf{l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon}}\]</span></td>
<td colspan="2"><span class="math display">\[{\mathbf{\{ i}_{\mathbf{1,7,l}}\mathbf{\}}}_{\mathbf{l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon}}\]</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Rank=1</td>
<td>4</td>
<td colspan="2">N/A</td>
<td colspan="2">N/A</td>
<td colspan="2">N/A</td>
<td><span class="math display">\[3(K^{NZ} - 1)\]</span></td>
<td colspan="2"><span class="math display">\[4(K^{NZ} - 1)\]</span></td>
<td colspan="2"><p>N/A if <span class="math inline">\(K^{NZ} = K_{1}M\)</span>;</p>
<p><span class="math inline">\(K_{1}M\)</span> otherwise</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Rank=2</td>
<td>4</td>
<td colspan="2">4</td>
<td colspan="2">N/A</td>
<td colspan="2">N/A</td>
<td><span class="math display">\[3(K^{NZ} - 2)\]</span></td>
<td colspan="2"><span class="math display">\[4(K^{NZ} - 2)\]</span></td>
<td colspan="2"><p>N/A if <span class="math inline">\(K^{NZ} = {2K}_{1}M\)</span>;</p>
<p><span class="math inline">\({2K}_{1}M\)</span> otherwise</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Rank=3</td>
<td>4</td>
<td colspan="2">4</td>
<td colspan="2">4</td>
<td colspan="2">N/A</td>
<td><span class="math display">\[3(K^{NZ} - 3)\]</span></td>
<td colspan="2"><span class="math display">\[4(K^{NZ} - 3)\]</span></td>
<td colspan="2"><span class="math display">\[3K_{1}M\]</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Rank=4</td>
<td>4</td>
<td colspan="2">4</td>
<td colspan="2">4</td>
<td colspan="2">4</td>
<td><span class="math display">\[3(K^{NZ} - 4)\]</span></td>
<td colspan="2"><span class="math display">\[4(K^{NZ} - 4)\]</span></td>
<td colspan="2"><span class="math display">\[4K_{1}M\]</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="13">NOTE: the bitwidth for <span class="math inline">\({{\{ i}_{1,7,l}\}}_{l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon}\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\({{\{ i}_{2,4,l}\}}_{l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon}\)</span> and <span class="math inline">\({{\{ i}_{2,5,l}\}}_{l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon}\)</span> shown in Table 6.3.2.1.2-2D is the total bitwidth of <span class="math inline">\({\{ i}_{1,7,l}\}\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\({\{ i}_{2,4,l}\}\)</span> and <span class="math inline">\({\{ i}_{2,5,l}\}\)</span> up to Rank = <span class="math inline">\(\upsilon\)</span>, respectively, and the corresponding per layer bitwidths are <span class="math inline">\(K_{1}M\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\(3\left( K_{l}^{NZ} - 1 \right)\)</span>, and 4<span class="math inline">\(\left( K_{l}^{NZ} - 1 \right)\)</span>, (i.e., 1, 3, and 4 bits for each respective indicator elements <span class="math inline">\(k_{l,i,f}^{(3)}\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\(k_{l,i,f}^{(2)}\)</span>, and <span class="math inline">\(c_{l,i,f}\)</span>, respectively), where <span class="math inline">\(K_{l}^{NZ}\)</span> as defined in Clause 5.2.2.2.11 in [6, TS 38.214] is the number of nonzero coefficients for layer <span class="math inline">\(l\)</span> such that <span class="math inline">\(K^{NZ} = \sum_{l = 1}^{\upsilon}K_{l}^{NZ}\)</span>.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

The bitwidth for MRI and SRS-RSRP are provided in Table 6.3.2.1.2-2E.

Table 6.3.2.1.2-2E: Bitwidth for MRI and SRS-RSRP

<table style="width:63%;">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 17%" />
<col style="width: 46%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;"><strong>Field</strong></th>
<th style="text-align: center;"><strong>Bitwidth</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">MRI</td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\left\lceil \log_{2}\left( K_{s}^{SRS - RSRP} \right) \right\rceil\]</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">SRS-RSRP</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">7</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Differential RSRP</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">4</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="2" style="text-align: center;">NOTE: <span class="math inline">\(K_{s}^{SRS - RSRP}\)</span> is the number of SRS-RSRP measurement resources in the corresponding resource set.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

For CSI on PUSCH, two UCI bit sequences are generated, $a_{0}^{(1)},a_{1}^{(1)},a_{2}^{(1)},a_{3}^{(1)},...,a_{A^{(1)}-1}^{(1)}$ and $a_{0}^{(2)},a_{1}^{(2)},a_{2}^{(2)},a_{3}^{(2)},...,a_{A^{(2)}-1}^{(2)}$. The CSI fields of all CSI reports, in the order from upper part to lower part in Table 6.3.2.1.2-6, are mapped to the UCI bit sequence $a_{0}^{(1)},a_{1}^{(1)},a_{2}^{(1)},a_{3}^{(1)},...,a_{A^{(1)}-1}^{(1)}$ starting with $a_{0}^{(1)}$. The CSI fields of all CSI reports, in the order from upper part to lower part in Table 6.3.2.1.2-7, are mapped to the UCI bit sequence $a_{0}^{(2)},a_{1}^{(2)},a_{2}^{(2)},a_{3}^{(2)},...,a_{A^{(2)}-1}^{(2)}$ starting with $a_{0}^{(2)}$.

The mapping order of CSI fields of one report for CSI reporting for L1/L2‑triggered mobility and handover as defined in Clause 5.2.4a of \[6, TS 38.214\] is provided in Table 6.3.1.1.2-7 by taking only Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1/3 for the determination of the bitwidth of a CSI field. The mapping order of CSI fields of one report for CRI/RSRP or SSBRI/RSRP or CRI/RSRP/CapabilityIndex or SSBRI/RSRP/CapabilityIndex reporting is provided in Table 6.3.1.1.2-8. The mapping order of CSI fields of one report for inter-cell SSBRI/RSRP reporting is provided in Table 6.3.1.1.2-8. The mapping order of CSI fields of one report for CRI/SINR or SSBRI/SINR or CRI/SINR/CapabilityIndex or SSBRI/SINR/CapabilityIndex reporting is provided in Table 6.3.1.1.2-8A. The mapping order of CSI fields of one report for group-based CRI/RSRP or SSBRI/RSRP reporting is provided in Table 6.3.1.1.2-8B. The mapping order of CSI fields of one report for SSBRI/RSRP or CRI/RSRP reporting for L1/L2‑triggered mobility is provided in Table 6.3.1.1.2-8C. The mapping order of CSI fields of one report for MRI/CLI-RSSI is provided in Table 6.3.1.1.2-8D. The mapping order of CSI fields of one report for predicted CRI/RSRP or predicted SSBRI/RSRP reporting is provided in Table 6.3.1.1.2-8E. The mapping order of CSI fields of one report for time instance indicator/predicted CRI/predicted RSRP or time instance indicator/predicted SSBRI/predicted RSRP reporting is provided in Table 6.3.1.1.2-8F. The mapping order of CSI fields of one report for CRI/RSRP or SSBRI/RSRP if *nrofReportedRS* is configured is provided in Table 6.3.1.1.2-8G. The mapping order of CSI fields of one report for RS-PAI is provided in Table 6.3.1.1.2-8H. The procedure in clause 6.3.2 described for CSI part 1 is also applicable for one report for CRI/RSRP, SSBRI/RSRP, predicted CRI/RSRP, predicted SSBRI/RSRP, time instance indicator/predicted CRI/predicted RSRP, time instance indicator/predicted SSBRI/predicted RSRP, RS-PAI, CSI-PAI, CRI/SINR, SSBRI/SINR, MRI/SRS-RSRP, MRI/CLI-RSSI reporting, CSI reporting for L1/L2‑triggered mobility, CSI reporting for handover, TDCP reporting, delay offset reporting, frequency offset reporting, both delay offset and frequency offset reporting, and phase offset reporting.

Table 6.3.2.1.2-3: Mapping order of CSI fields of one CSI report, CSI part 1

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 20%" />
<col style="width: 79%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>CSI report number</th>
<th>CSI fields</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td rowspan="9"><p>CSI report #n</p>
<p>CSI part 1</p></td>
<td>CRI as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3/4/6, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Rank Indicator as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3//3C 4/5 or 6.3.2.1.2-8/8A/8B/9/9A, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Wideband CQI for the first TB as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3//3C 4/5 or 6.3.2.1.2-8/8A/8B/9/9A, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Subband differential CQI for the first TB with increasing order of subband number as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3//3C 4/5 or 6.3.2.1.2-8/8A/8B/9/9A, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Indicator of the number of non-zero wideband amplitude coefficients <span class="math inline">\(M_{0}\)</span> for layer 0 as in Table 6.3.1.1.2-5, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Indicator of the number of non-zero wideband amplitude coefficients <span class="math inline">\(M_{1}\)</span> for layer 1 as in Table 6.3.1.1.2-5 (if the rank according to the reported RI is equal to one, this field is set to all zeros), if 2-layer PMI reporting is allowed according to the rank restriction in Clauses 5.2.2.2.3 and 5.2.2.2.4 [6, TS 38.214] and if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Indicator of the <span class="math inline">\(N_{0}\)</span> selected CSI-RS resources by a bitmap with <span class="math inline">\(N_{TRP}\)</span> bits, this field is present only if <span class="math inline">\(N_{TRP} &gt; 1\)</span> and <em>restrictedCMR-Selection</em> is configured to <em>OFF</em></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Indicator of selected <span class="math inline">\(L_{n}\)</span> value combination or <span class="math inline">\(\alpha_{n}\)</span> value combination with bitwidth of <span class="math inline">\(\left\lceil \log_{2}{(N_{L})} \right\rceil\)</span>, this field is present only if <span class="math inline">\(N_{L} &gt; 1\)</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Indicator of the total number of non-zero coefficients summed across all layers <span class="math inline">\(K^{NZ}\)</span> as in Tables 6.3.2.1.2-8/8A/8B/9/9A, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="2">NOTE: Subbands for given CSI report <em>n</em> indicated by the higher layer parameter <em>csi-ReportingBand</em> are numbered continuously in the increasing order with the lowest subband of <em>csi-ReportingBand</em> as subband 0.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<tr>

</tr>
<tr>

</tr>
<tr>
<td><p>Rank Combination Indicator as in Table 6.3.1.1.2-3A, if associated with one CSI-RS resource pair and if reported;</p>
<p>Rank Indicator as in Table 6.3.1.1.2-3B, if associated with one CSI-RS resource and if reported;</p>
<p>Zero padding bits <span class="math inline">\(O_{P}\)</span>, if needed</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><p>Wideband CQI for the first TB as in Table 6.3.1.1.2-3A, if associated with one CSI-RS resource pair and if reported;</p>
<p>Wideband CQI for the first TB as in Table 6.3.1.1.2-3B, if associated with one CSI-RS resource and if reported</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>

</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="2">NOTE: Subbands for given CSI report <em>n</em> indicated by the higher layer parameter <em>csi-ReportingBand</em> are numbered continuously in the increasing order with the lowest subband of <em>csi-ReportingBand</em> as subband 0.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

The number of zero padding bits $O_{P}$ in Table 6.3.2.1.2-3B is 0 for 1 CSI-RS port and $O_{P} = N_{\max} - N_{reported}(R)$ for more than 1 CSI-RS port, where:

- $N_{\max} = \max_{r \in S_{Rank}}{N(r)}\$. $S_{Rank}\$is the set of rank and rank combination values *r* that are allowed to be reported. $N(r)$ is obtained according to Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3A/3B for rank combination indicator and rank indicator respectively.

- $N_{reported}\ (R)\$is obtained according to Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3A for rank combination indicator and *R* is the reported rank combination

- $N_{reported}\ (R)$ is obtained according to Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3B for rank indicator and *R* is the reported rank

</tr>

<table>
<colgroup>

</tr>

<tr>
<td rowspan="23" style="text-align: center;"><p>CSI report #n</p>
<p>CSI part 1</p></td>
<td style="text-align: center;">Rank Indicator for the first CRI among the non-reported <em>M<sub>R</sub></em> CRIs as in Tables 6.3.2.1.2-8C or 6.3.2.1.2-8D, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Wideband CQI for the first TB for the first CRI among the non-reported <em>M<sub>R</sub></em> CRIs as in Tables 6.3.2.1.2-8C or 6.3.2.1.2-8D, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Subband differential CQI for the first TB with increasing order of subband number for the first CRI among the non-reported <em>M<sub>R</sub></em> CRIs as in Tables 6.3.2.1.2-8C or 6.3.2.1.2-8D, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Indicator of the total number of non-zero coefficients summed across all layers <span class="math inline">\(K^{NZ}\)</span> for the first CRI among the non-reported <em>M<sub>R</sub></em> CRIs as in Tables 6.3.2.1.2-8D, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">…</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Rank Indicator for the <em>M<sub>R</sub></em>-th CRI among the non-reported <em>M<sub>R</sub></em> CRIs as in Tables 6.3.2.1.2-8C or 6.3.2.1.2-8D, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Wideband CQI for the first TB for the <em>M<sub>R</sub></em>-th CRI among the non-reported <em>M<sub>R</sub></em> CRIs as in Tables 6.3.2.1.2-8C or 6.3.2.1.2-8D, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Subband differential CQI for the first TB with increasing order of subband number for the <em>M<sub>R</sub></em>-th CRI among the non-reported <em>M<sub>R</sub></em> CRIs as in Tables 6.3.2.1.2-8C or 6.3.2.1.2-8D, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Indicator of the total number of non-zero coefficients summed across all layers <span class="math inline">\(K^{NZ}\)</span> for the <em>M<sub>R</sub></em>-th CRI among the non-reported <em>M<sub>R</sub></em> CRIs as in Tables 6.3.2.1.2-8D, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">…</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">First reported CRI as in Tables 6.3.2.1.2-8C or 6.3.2.1.2-8D, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Rank Indicator for the first reported CRI as in Tables 6.3.2.1.2-8C or 6.3.2.1.2-8D, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Zero padding bits <span class="math inline">\(O_{P,1}\)</span> for the first reported CRI, if needed</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Wideband CQI for the first TB for the first reported CRI as in Tables 6.3.2.1.2-8C or 6.3.2.1.2-8D, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Subband differential CQI for the first TB with increasing order of subband number for the first reported CRI as in Tables 6.3.2.1.2-8C or 6.3.2.1.2-8D, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Indicator of the total number of non-zero coefficients summed across all layers <span class="math inline">\(K^{NZ}\)</span> for the first reported CRI as in Tables 6.3.2.1.2-8D, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">…</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">The (<em>M</em>-<em>M<sub>R</sub></em>)-th reported CRI as in Tables 6.3.2.1.2-8C or 6.3.2.1.2-8D, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Rank Indicator for the (<em>M</em>-<em>M<sub>R</sub></em>)-th reported CRI as in Tables 6.3.2.1.2-8C or 6.3.2.1.2-8D, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Zero padding bits <span class="math inline">\(O_{P,M - M_{R}}\)</span> for the (<em>M</em>-<em>M<sub>R</sub></em>)-th reported CRI, if needed</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Wideband CQI for the first TB for the (<em>M</em>-<em>M<sub>R</sub></em>)-th reported CRI as in Tables 6.3.2.1.2-8C or 6.3.2.1.2-8D, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Subband differential CQI for the first TB with increasing order of subband number for the (<em>M</em>-<em>M<sub>R</sub></em>)-th reported CRI as in Tables 6.3.2.1.2-8C or 6.3.2.1.2-8D, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Indicator of the total number of non-zero coefficients summed across all layers <span class="math inline">\(K^{NZ}\)</span> for the (<em>M</em>-<em>M<sub>R</sub></em>)-th reported CRI as in Tables 6.3.2.1.2-8D, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="2" style="text-align: center;"><p>NOTE1: Subbands for given CSI report <em>n</em> indicated by the higher layer parameter <em>csi-ReportingBand</em> are numbered continuously in the increasing order with the lowest subband of <em>csi-ReportingBand</em> as subband 0.</p>
<p>NOTE2: <em>M<sub>R</sub></em> is set to 0 if higher layer parameter <em>mr-SelectedResources</em> is not configured for the CSI report.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

The number of zero padding bits $O_{P,k}$ in Table 6.3.1.1.2-3D is 0 for 1 CSI-RS port and $O_{P,k} = N_{\max} - N_{reported,k}$ for more than 1 CSI-RS port, where:

- $N_{\max} = \max_{i \in S}\left( N_{i} \right)$, where *S* is the set of CRIs corresponding to the *K<sub>s</sub>*-*M<sub>R</sub>* resources and $N_{i}$ is the payload size of RI field for the *i*-th CRI obtained according to Table 6.3.2.1.2-8C or 6.3.2.1.2-8D;

- $N_{reported,k}$ is the payload size of RI field for the *k*-th CRI obtained according to Table 6.3.2.1.2-8C or 6.3.2.1.2-8D.

Table 6.3.2.1.2-3E: Mapping order of CSI fields of one CSI report for *reportQuantity*=*cjtc-Dd*

<table style="width:100%;">
<colgroup>

<col style="width: 79%" />
</colgroup>

<tr>
<td rowspan="3" style="text-align: center;">CSI report #n</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">The index of reference CSI-RS resource set for tracking as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-11.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">The delay offset for each CSI-RS resource set for tracking configured for the CSI report as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-11, except that indicated by reference CSI-RS resource set for tracking, ordered from the lowest to highest CSI-RS resource set ID.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">The inside/outside CP indicator for each CSI-RS resource set for tracking configured for the CSI report as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-11, except that indicated by reference CSI-RS resource set for tracking, ordered from the lowest to highest CSI-RS resource set ID.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</tr>

</tr>

Table 6.3.2.1.2-3H: Mapping order of CSI fields of one CSI report for *reportQuantity*=*cjtc-P*

<table style="width:100%;">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 20%" />
<col style="width: 79%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;"><strong>CSI report number</strong></th>
<th style="text-align: center;"><strong>CSI fields</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td rowspan="5" style="text-align: center;">CSI report #n</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">The reference CSI-RS resource index as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-13.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Subband phase offsets for CSI-RS resource #0 as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-13 if subband reporting is configured, with increasing order of subband number; otherwise, wideband phase offset for CSI-RS resource #0 as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-13.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Subband phase offsets for CSI-RS resource #1 as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-13 if subband reporting is configured, with increasing order of subband number; otherwise, wideband phase offset for CSI-RS resource #1 as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-13.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">…</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Subband phase offsets for CSI-RS resource #<span class="math inline">\((N_{TRP} - 1)\)</span> as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-13 if subband reporting is configured, with increasing order of subband number; otherwise, wideband phase offset for CSI-RS resource #<span class="math inline">\((N_{TRP} - 1)\)</span> as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-13.</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="2" style="text-align: center;">NOTE: The CSI-RS resources are ordered from the lowest to highest CSI-RS resource ID, except that indicated by reference CSI-RS resource index. <span class="math inline">\(N_{TRP}\)</span> is the number of CSI-RS resources configured for the CSI report.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

Table 6.3.2.1.2-3I: Mapping order of CSI fields of one report for CSI report configuration indicator/CRI/RSRP/Condition met indicator, or CSI report configuration indicator/SSBRI/RSRP/Condition met indicator

<table style="width:97%;">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 33%" />
<col style="width: 63%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;"><strong>CSI report number</strong></th>
<th style="text-align: center;"><strong>CSI fields</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td rowspan="15" style="text-align: center;">CSI report #n</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">CSI report configuration indicator as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-14, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">CRI or SSBRI #1 as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-14</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">CRI or SSBRI #2 as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-14, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">CRI or SSBRI #3 as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-14, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">CRI or SSBRI #4 as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-14, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">RSRP #1 as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-14</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Differential RSRP #2 as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-14, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Differential RSRP #3 as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-14, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Differential RSRP #4 as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-14, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Condition met indicator #1 in Table 6.3.2.1.2-14, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Condition met indicator #2 in Table 6.3.2.1.2-14, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Condition met indicator #3 in Table 6.3.2.1.2-14, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Condition met indicator #4 in Table 6.3.2.1.2-14, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;"><p>RSRP as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-14 for the reference signal in the indicated TCI state or the SS/PBCH block which is QCLed with the reference signal in the indicated TCI state if <em>eventTypeUE-Initiated</em> is set to ‘event1’ and <em>currentBeamReport</em> is configured;</p>
<p>Differentatial RSRP as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-14 for the reference signal in the indicated TCI state or the SS/PBCH block which is QCLed with the reference signal in the indicated TCI state if <em>eventTypeUE-Initiated</em> is set to ‘event2’ and if <em>currentBeamReport</em> is configured;</p>
<p>Differentatial RSRP as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-14 for the reference signal with the <em>valueOfQ</em> highest L1-RSRP out of reference signals among the activated TCI states or the SS/PBCH block which is QCLed with the reference signal with the <em>valueOfQ</em> highest L1-RSRP out of refence signals among the activated TCI states if <em>eventTypeUE-Initiated</em> is set to ‘event7’ and if <em>currentBeamReport</em> is configured;</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Zero padding bits <span class="math inline">\(O_{P}\)</span>, if needed</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="2" style="text-align: center;"><p>NOTE:</p>
<p>The CSI report configuration indicator with value <span class="math inline">\(i\)</span> indicates the (<span class="math inline">\(i\)</span><em>+1)</em>-th CSI report configuration among the CSI report configurations associated with a same PUCCH resource configured by <em>ue-InitiatedResourceConfig</em>, which are ordered in ascending order of <em>CSI-ReportConfigID</em>.</p>
<p>The condition met indicators are reported only when the UE is configured with higher layer parameter <em>conditionFulfillmentIndicator</em>. If the reference signal associated with CRI or SSBRI #<em>j</em> satisfies the condition for transmitting UEIRI as defined in Clause 5.2.1.5.4.1 in [6, TS 38.214] when <em>eventDetectionTimeWindow</em> is configured, the value of the condition met indicator #<em>j</em> is set to 1; otherwise set to 0.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

The number of zero padding bits $O_{P}$ in Table 6.3.2.1.2-3I is 0 if there is only one CSI report configuration associated with the PUCCH carrying UEIRI; otherwise $O_{P} = N_{\max} - N_{reported}$, where:

- $N_{\max} = \max_{i \in S}\left( N_{i} \right)$, where *S* is the set of CSI report configurations associated with a same PUCCH resource configured by *ue-InitiatedResourceConfig*, and $N_{i}$ is the payload size of the report for the *i*-th CSI report configuration, obtained according to Table 6.3.2.1.2-3I prior to padding;

- $N_{reported}$ is the payload size of the current report, obtained according to Table 6.3.2.1.2-3I prior to padding.

For layer <span class="math inline">\(l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon\)</span>, where<span class="math inline">\(\ \upsilon\)</span> is the value of the reported Rank Indicator for the associated CSI report of predicted PMI configured with the higher layer parameter <em>csi-InferencePrediction</em>, the values of <span class="math inline">\({SGCS}_{l}^{1}\)</span> and <span class="math inline">\({SGCS}_{l}^{2}\)</span> are determined according to Clause 5.2.1.4.6 TS 38.214 [6]. For layer <span class="math inline">\(l = \upsilon + 1,\ldots,\upsilon_{Max}\)</span>, if any, the values of <span class="math inline">\({SGCS}_{l}^{1}\)</span> and <span class="math inline">\({SGCS}_{l}^{2}\)</span> are set to ‘1111’.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

Table 6.3.2.1.2-4: Mapping order of CSI fields of one CSI report, CSI part 2 wideband

</tr>

</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

Table 6.3.2.1.2-4A: Mapping order of CSI fields of one CSI report, CSI part 2 wideband,  
*csi-ReportMode= Mode 1*

<table style="width:99%;">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 17%" />
<col style="width: 81%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>CSI report number</th>
<th>CSI fields</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td rowspan="13"><p>CSI report #n</p>
<p>CSI part 2 wideband</p></td>
<td>Two Layer Indicators as in Table 6.3.1.1.2-3A, where the first Layer Indicator and the second Layer Indicator are associated with the first resource and the second resource within the resource pair respectively and if reported;</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>PMI wideband information fields $X_{1}$, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1 associated with the first resource within the CSI-RS resource pair, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>PMI wideband information fields $X_{2}$, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] associated with the first CSI-RS resource within the CSI-RS resource pair, if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>widebandPMI</em> and if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>PMI wideband information fields $X_{1}$, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1 associated with the second resource within the CSI-RS resource pair, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>PMI wideband information fields $X_{2}$, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] associated with the second CSI-RS resource within the CSI-RS resource pair, if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>widebandPMI</em> and if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><p>Wideband CQI for the second TB as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3B, if associated with CRI in CSI part 1, <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 1 and if reported;</p>
<p>Wideband CQI for the second TB as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3B, if associated with the first CRI in CSI part 1, <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 2 and if reported</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><p>Layer Indicator as in Table 6.3.1.1.2-3B, if associated with CRI in CSI part 1, <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 1 and if reported;</p>
<p>Layer Indicator as in Table 6.3.1.1.2-3B, if associated with the first CRI in CSI part 1, <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 2 and if reported</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><p>PMI wideband information fields $X_{1}$, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, if associated with CRI in CSI part 1, <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 1 and if reported;</p>
<p>PMI wideband information fields $X_{1}$, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, if associated with the first CRI in CSI part 1, <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 2 and if reported</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><p>PMI wideband information fields $X_{2}$, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214], if associated with CRI in CSI part 1, <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>widebandPMI</em>, <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 1 and if reported;</p>
<p>PMI wideband information fields $X_{2}$, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214], if associated with the first CRI in CSI part 1, <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>widebandPMI,</em> <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 2 and if reported</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Wideband CQI for the second TB as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3B, if associated with the second CRI in CSI part 1, <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 2 and if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>Layer Indicator as in Table 6.3.1.1.2-3B, if associated with the second CRI in CSI part 1, <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 2 and if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>PMI wideband information fields $X_{1}$, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, if associated with the second CRI in CSI part 1, <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 2 and if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>PMI wideband information fields $X_{2}$, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214], if associated with the second CRI in CSI part 1, <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>widebandPMI</em>, <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 2 and if reported</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

Table 6.3.2.1.2-4B: Mapping order of CSI fields of one CSI report, CSI part 2 wideband,  
*csi-ReportMode= Mode 2*

<table style="width:98%;">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 18%" />
<col style="width: 79%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>CSI report number</th>
<th>CSI fields</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td rowspan="8"><p>CSI report #n</p>
<p>CSI part 2 wideband</p></td>
<td>Wideband CQI for the second TB as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3B, if reported part 1 is associated with one CSI-RS resource and if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><p>Two Layer Indicators as in Table 6.3.1.1.2-3A, if reported part 1 is associated with one CSI-RS resource pair, where the first Layer Indicator and the second Layer Indicator are associated with the first resource and the second resource within the resource pair respectively and if reported;</p>
<p>Layer Indicator as in Table 6.3.1.1.2-3B, if reported part 1 is associated with one CSI-RS resource and if reported</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>PMI wideband information fields $X_{1}$, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1 associated with the first resource within the CSI-RS resource pair, if reported part 1 is associated with one CSI-RS resource pair and if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>PMI wideband information fields $X_{2}$, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] associated with the first CSI-RS resource within the CSI-RS resource pair, if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>widebandPMI</em> and reported part 1 is associated with one CSI-RS resource pair and if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>PMI wideband information fields $X_{1}$, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1 associated with the second CSI-RS resource within the CSI-RS resource pair, if reported part 1 is associated with one CSI-RS resource pair and if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>PMI wideband information fields $X_{2}$, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] associated with the second CSI-RS resource within the CSI-RS resource pair, if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>widebandPMI</em> and reported part 1 is associated with one CSI-RS resource pair and if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>PMI wideband information fields $X_{1}$, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, if reported part 1 is associated with one CSI-RS resource and if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>PMI wideband information fields $X_{2}$, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214], if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>widebandPMI</em> and reported part 1 is associated with one CSI-RS resource and if reported</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

Table 6.3.2.1.2-4C: Mapping order of CSI fields of one CSI report configured with *valueOfM*, CSI part 2 wideband

<table style="width:98%;">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 18%" />
<col style="width: 80%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;"><strong>CSI report number</strong></th>
<th style="text-align: center;"><strong>CSI fields</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td rowspan="19" style="text-align: center;"><p>CSI report #n</p>
<p>CSI part 2 wideband</p></td>
<td style="text-align: center;">Wideband CQI for the second TB for the first CRI among the non-reported <em>M<sub>R</sub></em> CRIs as in Tables 6.3.2.1.2-8C, if present and reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Layer Indicator for the first CRI among the non-reported <em>M<sub>R</sub></em> CRIs as in Tables 6.3.2.1.2-8C, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">PMI wideband information fields <span class="math inline">\(X_{1}\)</span> for the first CRI among the non-reported <em>M<sub>R</sub></em> CRIs, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">PMI wideband information fields <span class="math inline">\(X_{2}\)</span> for the first CRI among the non-reported <em>M<sub>R</sub></em> CRIs, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports for the first CRI among the non-reported <em>M<sub>R</sub></em> CRIs according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214], if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>widebandPMI</em> and if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">…</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Wideband CQI for the second TB for the <em>M<sub>R</sub></em>-th CRI among the non-reported <em>M<sub>R</sub></em> CRIs as in Tables 6.3.2.1.2-8C, if present and reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Layer Indicator for the <em>M<sub>R</sub></em>-th CRI among the non-reported <em>M<sub>R</sub></em> CRIs as in Tables 6.3.2.1.2-8C, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">PMI wideband information fields <span class="math inline">\(X_{1}\)</span> for the <em>M<sub>R</sub></em>-th CRI among the non-reported <em>M<sub>R</sub></em> CRIs, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">PMI wideband information fields <span class="math inline">\(X_{2}\)</span> for the <em>M<sub>R</sub></em>-th CRI among the non-reported <em>M<sub>R</sub></em> CRIs, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports for the <em>M<sub>R</sub></em>-th CRI among the non-reported <em>M<sub>R</sub></em> CRIs according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214], if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>widebandPMI</em> and if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">…</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Wideband CQI for the second TB for the first reported CRI as in Tables 6.3.2.1.2-8C, if present and reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Layer Indicator for the first reported CRI as in Tables 6.3.2.1.2-8C, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">PMI wideband information fields <span class="math inline">\(X_{1}\)</span> for the first reported CRI, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">PMI wideband information fields <span class="math inline">\(X_{2}\)</span> for the first reported CRI, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports for the first reported CRI according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214], if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>widebandPMI</em> and if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">…</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Wideband CQI for the second TB for the (<em>M</em>-<em>M<sub>R</sub></em>)-th reported CRI as in Tables 6.3.2.1.2-8C, if present and reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Layer Indicator for the (<em>M</em>-<em>M<sub>R</sub></em>)-th reported CRI as in Tables 6.3.2.1.2-8C, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">PMI wideband information fields <span class="math inline">\(X_{1}\)</span> for the (<em>M</em>-<em>M<sub>R</sub></em>)-th reported CRI, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">PMI wideband information fields <span class="math inline">\(X_{2}\)</span> for the (<em>M</em>-<em>M<sub>R</sub></em>)-th reported CRI, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports for the (<em>M</em>-<em>M<sub>R</sub></em>)-th reported CRI according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214], if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>widebandPMI</em> and if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="2" style="text-align: center;">NOTE: <em>M<sub>R</sub></em> is set to 0 if higher layer parameter <em>mr-SelectedResources</em> is not configured for the CSI report.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

Table 6.3.2.1.2-5: Mapping order of CSI fields of one CSI report, CSI part 2 subband

<table style="width:98%;">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 15%" />
<col style="width: 82%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th rowspan="4"><p>CSI report #n</p>
<p>Part 2 subband</p></th>
<th>Subband differential CQI for the second TB of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number, as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3/3C/4/5, if <em>cqi-FormatIndicator=subbandCQI</em> and if reported</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th>PMI subband information fields $X_{2}$ of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1/1A/1B/2/2A or 6.3.2.1.2-1/2, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number, if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>subbandPMI</em> and if reported</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th>Subband differential CQI for the second TB of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number, as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3/3C/4/5, if <em>cqi-FormatIndicator=subbandCQI</em> and if reported</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th>PMI subband information fields $X_{2}$ of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1/1A/1B/2/2A or 6.3.2.1.2-1/2, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number, if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>subbandPMI</em> and if reported</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td colspan="2">NOTE: Subbands for given CSI report <em>n</em> indicated by the higher layer parameter <em>csi-ReportingBand</em> are numbered continuously in the increasing order with the lowest subband of <em>csi-ReportingBand</em> as subband 0.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

Table 6.3.2.1.2-5A: Mapping order of CSI fields of one CSI report, CSI part 2 of  
*codebookType=typeII-r16 or typeII-PortSelection-r16*

<table style="width:98%;">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 18%" />
<col style="width: 80%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>CSI report number</th>
<th>CSI fields</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td><p>CSI report #n</p>
<p>CSI part 2, group 0</p></td>
<td>PMI fields <span class="math inline">\(X_{1}\)</span>, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.2.1.2-1A/2A, if reported</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><p>CSI report #n</p>
<p>CSI part 2, group 1</p></td>
<td><p>The following PMI fields <span class="math inline">\(X_{2}\)</span>, from left to right, as in Tables 6.3.2.1.2-1A/2A:<span class="math inline">\(\ \left\{ i_{2,3,l}:l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon \right\}\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\(i_{1,5}\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\(\left\{ i_{1,6,l}:l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon \right\}\)</span> and <span class="math inline">\(max(0,\left\lceil \frac{K^{NZ}}{2} \right\rceil - \upsilon) \times 3\)</span> highest priority bits of</p>
<p><span class="math inline">\(\left\{ i_{2,4,l}:l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon \right\},max(0,\left\lceil \frac{K^{NZ}}{2} \right\rceil - \upsilon) \times 4\)</span> highest priority bits of <span class="math inline">\(\{ i_{2,5,l}:l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon\}\)</span> and<span class="math inline">\(\ \nu*2LM_{\upsilon} - \left\lfloor K^{NZ}/2 \right\rfloor\)</span> highest priority bits of<span class="math inline">\(\left\{ i_{1,7,l}:l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon \right\}\)</span>, in decreasing order of priority based on the corresponding function <span class="math inline">\(Pri(l,i,f)\)</span> defined in clause 5.2.3 of TS 38.214 [6], if reported</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><p>CSI report #n</p>
<p>CSI part 2, group 2</p></td>
<td>The following PMI fields <span class="math inline">\(X_{2}\)</span>, from left to right, as in Tables 6.3.2.1.2-1A/2A<span class="math inline">\(:\ \min\left( K^{NZ} - v,\ \left\lfloor \frac{K^{NZ}}{2} \right\rfloor \right) \times 3\)</span> lowest priority bits of <span class="math inline">\(\left\{ i_{2,4,l}:l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon \right\},\min\left( K^{NZ} - v,\ \left\lfloor \frac{K^{NZ}}{2} \right\rfloor \right) \times 4\)</span> lowest priority bits of <span class="math inline">\(\left\{ i_{2,5,l}:l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon \right\}\)</span> and <span class="math inline">\(\left\lfloor K^{NZ}/2 \right\rfloor\)</span> lowest priority bits of <span class="math inline">\(\left\{ i_{1,7,l}:l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon \right\}\)</span>, in decreasing order of priority based on the corresponding function <span class="math inline">\(Pri(l,i,f)\)</span> defined in clause 5.2.3 of TS 38.214 [6], if reported</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

*codebookType=typeII-PortSelection-r17* or *typeII-Doppler-PortSelection*

<p>CSI part 2, group 1</p></td>
