<p><span class="math inline">\(\left\{ i_{2,4,l}:l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon \right\},(max(0,\left\lceil \frac{K^{NZ}}{2} \right\rceil - \upsilon)) \times 4\)</span> highest priority bits of <span class="math inline">\(\{ i_{2,5,l}:l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon\}\)</span> and<span class="math inline">\(\ \nu*K_{1}M - \left\lfloor K^{NZ}/2 \right\rfloor\)</span> highest priority bits of<span class="math inline">\(\left\{ i_{1,7,l}:l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon \right\}\)</span>, in decreasing order of priority based on the corresponding function <span class="math inline">\(Pri(l,i,f)\)</span> defined in clause 5.2.3 of TS 38.214 [6], if reported</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><p>CSI report #n</p>
<p>CSI part 2, group 2</p></td>
<td>The following PMI fields <span class="math inline">\(X_{2}\)</span>, from left to right, as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-2B/2D<span class="math inline">\(:\ (\min\left( K^{NZ} - v,\ \left\lfloor \frac{K^{NZ}}{2} \right\rfloor \right)) \times 3\)</span> lowest priority bits of <span class="math inline">\(\left\{ i_{2,4,l}:l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon \right\},(\min\left( K^{NZ} - v,\ \left\lfloor \frac{K^{NZ}}{2} \right\rfloor \right)) \times 4\)</span> lowest priority bits of <span class="math inline">\(\left\{ i_{2,5,l}:l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon \right\}\)</span> and <span class="math inline">\(\left\lfloor K^{NZ}/2 \right\rfloor\)</span> lowest priority bits of <span class="math inline">\(\left\{ i_{1,7,l}:l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon \right\}\)</span>, in decreasing order of priority based on the corresponding function <span class="math inline">\(Pri(l,i,f)\)</span> defined in clause 5.2.3 of TS 38.214 [6], if reported</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

Table 6.3.2.1.2-5C: Mapping order of CSI fields of one CSI report, CSI part 2 subband,  
*ReportMode= Mode 1*

<table style="width:98%;">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 15%" />
<col style="width: 82%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th rowspan="12"><p>CSI report #n</p>
<p>Part 2 subband</p></th>
<th>PMI subband information fields $X_{2}$ of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports associated with the first resource within the CSI-RS resource pair, according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number, if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>subbandPMI</em> and if reported</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th>PMI subband information fields $X_{2}$ of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports associated with the second resource within the CSI-RS resource pair, according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number, if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>subbandPMI</em> and if reported</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th><p>Subband differential CQI for the second TB of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number associated with CRI in CSI part 1, as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3B, if <em>cqi-FormatIndicator=subbandCQI</em>, <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 1 and if reported;</p>
<p>Subband differential CQI for the second TB of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number associated with the first CRI in CSI part 1, as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3B, if <em>cqi-FormatIndicator=subbandCQI</em>, <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 2 and if reported</p></th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th><p>PMI subband information fields $X_{2}$ of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports associated with CRI in CSI part 1 according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number, if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>subbandPMI</em>, <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 1 and if reported;</p>
<p>PMI subband information fields $X_{2}$ of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports associated with the first CRI in CSI part 1 according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number, if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>subbandPMI</em>, <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 2 and if reported</p></th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th>Subband differential CQI for the second TB of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number associated with the second CRI in CSI part 1, as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3B, if <em>cqi-FormatIndicator=subbandCQI</em>, <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 2 and if reported</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th>PMI subband information fields $X_{2}$ of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports associated with the second CRI in CSI part 1 according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number, if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>subbandPMI</em>, <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 2 and if reported</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th>PMI subband information fields $X_{2}$ of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports associated with the first resource within the CSI-RS resource pair, according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number, if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>subbandPMI</em> and if reported</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th>PMI subband information fields $X_{2}$ of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports associated with the second resource within the CSI-RS resource pair, according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number, if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>subbandPMI</em> and if reported</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th><p>Subband differential CQI for the second TB of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number associated with CRI in CSI part 1, as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3B, if <em>cqi-FormatIndicator=subbandCQI</em>, <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 1 and if reported;</p>
<p>Subband differential CQI for the second TB of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number associated with the first CRI in CSI part 1, as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3B, if <em>cqi-FormatIndicator=subbandCQI</em>, <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 2 and if reported</p></th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th><p>PMI subband information fields $X_{2}$ of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports associated with CRI in CSI part 1 according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number, if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>subbandPMI</em>, <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 1 and if reported;</p>
<p>PMI subband information fields $X_{2}$ of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports associated with the first CRI in CSI part 1 according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number, if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>subbandPMI</em>, <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 2 and if reported</p></th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th>Subband differential CQI for the second TB of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number associated with the second CRI in CSI part 1, as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3B, if <em>cqi-FormatIndicator=subbandCQI</em>, <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 2 and if reported</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th>PMI subband information fields $X_{2}$ of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports associated with the second CRI in CSI part 1 according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number, if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>subbandPMI</em>, <em>numberOfSingleTRP-CSI-Mode1 =</em> 2 and if reported</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
</tbody>
</table>

Table 6.3.2.1.2-5D: Mapping order of CSI fields of one CSI report, CSI part 2 subband,  
*ReportMode= Mode 2*

<table style="width:98%;">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 15%" />
<col style="width: 82%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th rowspan="8"><p>CSI report #n</p>
<p>Part 2 subband</p></th>
<th>PMI subband information fields $X_{2}$ of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports associated with the first resource within the CSI-RS resource pair, according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number, if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>subbandPMI</em> and reported part 1 is associated with one CSI-RS resource pair and if reported</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th>PMI subband information fields $X_{2}$ of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports associated with the second resource within the CSI-RS resource pair, according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number, if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>subbandPMI</em> and reported part 1 is associated with one CSI-RS resource pair and if reported</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th>PMI subband information fields $X_{2}$ of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports associated with the first resource within the CSI-RS resource pair, according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number, if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>subbandPMI</em> and reported part 1 is associated with one CSI-RS resource pair and if reported</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th>PMI subband information fields $X_{2}$ of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports associated with the second resource within the CSI-RS resource pair, according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number, if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>subbandPMI</em> and reported part 1 is associated with one CSI-RS resource pair and if reported</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th>Subband differential CQI for the second TB of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number associated with one CSI-RS resource, as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3B, if <em>cqi-FormatIndicator=subbandCQI</em> and reported part 1 is associated with one CSI-RS resource and if reported</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th>PMI subband information fields $X_{2}$ of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports associated with one CSI-RS resource according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number, if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>subbandPMI</em> and reported part 1 is associated with one CSI-RS resource and if reported</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th>Subband differential CQI for the second TB of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number associated with one CSI-RS resource, as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3B, if <em>cqi-FormatIndicator=subbandCQI</em> and reported part 1 is associated with one CSI-RS resource and if reported</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th>PMI subband information fields $X_{2}$ of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1/2, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports associated with one CSI-RS resource according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number, if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>subbandPMI</em> and reported part 1 is associated with one CSI-RS resource and if reported</th>
</tr>

Table 6.3.2.1.2-5E: Mapping order of CSI fields of one CSI report, CSI part 2 of  
*codebookType=typeII-CJT*

<table style="width:98%;">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 18%" />
<col style="width: 80%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;"><strong>CSI report number</strong></th>
<th style="text-align: center;"><strong>CSI fields</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;"><p>CSI report #n</p>
<p>CSI part 2, group 0</p></td>
<td style="text-align: center;">PMI fields <span class="math inline">\(X_{1}\)</span>, from left to right as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-1B, if reported;</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;"><p>CSI report #n</p>
<p>CSI part 2, group 1</p></td>
<td style="text-align: center;">The following PMI fields <span class="math inline">\(X_{2}\)</span>, from left to right, as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-1B:<span class="math inline">\(\ \left\{ i_{2,3,l}:l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon \right\}\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\(i_{1,5}\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\(\left\{ i_{1,6,l}:l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon \right\}\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\(i_{1,9}\)</span>, and <span class="math inline">\(max(0,\left\lceil \frac{K^{NZ}}{2} \right\rceil - \upsilon) \times 3\)</span> highest priority bits of <span class="math inline">\(\left\{ i_{2,4,l}:l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon \right\},max(0,\left\lceil \frac{K^{NZ}}{2} \right\rceil - \upsilon) \times 4\)</span> highest priority bits of <span class="math inline">\(\{ i_{2,5,l}:l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon\}\)</span> and<span class="math inline">\(\ \nu*2 \bullet \sum_{n = 1}^{N_{0}}L_{\sigma(n)}M_{\upsilon} - \left\lfloor K^{NZ}/2 \right\rfloor\)</span> highest priority bits of<span class="math inline">\(\left\{ i_{1,7,l}:l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon \right\}\)</span>, in decreasing order of priority based on the corresponding function<span class="math inline">\(\ Pri\left( l,\ i_{j},f,j \right)\)</span> defined in clause 5.2.3 of TS 38.214 [6] where <span class="math inline">\(j\)</span> is equivalent to <span class="math inline">\(n\)</span>, if present and if reported;</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;"><p>CSI report #n</p>
<p>CSI part 2, group 2</p></td>
<td style="text-align: center;">The following PMI fields <span class="math inline">\(X_{2}\)</span>, from left to right, as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-1B<span class="math inline">\(:\ \min\left( K^{NZ} - v,\ \left\lfloor \frac{K^{NZ}}{2} \right\rfloor \right) \times 3\)</span> lowest priority bits of <span class="math inline">\(\left\{ i_{2,4,l}:l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon \right\},\min\left( K^{NZ} - v,\ \left\lfloor \frac{K^{NZ}}{2} \right\rfloor \right) \times 4\)</span> lowest priority bits of <span class="math inline">\(\left\{ i_{2,5,l}:l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon \right\}\)</span> and <span class="math inline">\(\left\lfloor K^{NZ}/2 \right\rfloor\)</span> lowest priority bits of <span class="math inline">\(\left\{ i_{1,7,l}:l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon \right\}\)</span>, in decreasing order of priority based on the corresponding function<span class="math inline">\(\ Pri\left( l,\ i_{j},f,j \right)\)</span> defined in clause 5.2.3 of TS 38.214 [6] where <span class="math inline">\(j\)</span> is equivalent to <span class="math inline">\(n\)</span>, if reported;</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

*codebookType= typeII-Doppler*

<td style="text-align: center;"><p>CSI report #n</p>

<p>The second time-domain wideband CQI as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-8B, if present and reported</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;"><p>CSI report #n</p>
<p>CSI part 2, group 1</p></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><p>The following PMI fields <span class="math inline">\(X_{2}\)</span>, from left to right, as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-1C:<span class="math inline">\(\ \left\{ i_{2,3,l}:l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon \right\}\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\(i_{1,5}\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\(\left\{ i_{1,6,l}:l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon \right\}\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\(\left\{ i_{1,10,l}:l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon \right\}\)</span>, and <span class="math inline">\(max(0,\left\lceil \frac{K^{NZ}}{2} \right\rceil - \upsilon) \times 3\)</span> highest priority bits of</p>
<p><span class="math inline">\(\left\{ i_{2,4,l}:l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon \right\},max(0,\left\lceil \frac{K^{NZ}}{2} \right\rceil - \upsilon) \times 4\)</span> highest priority bits of <span class="math inline">\(\{ i_{2,5,l}:l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon\}\)</span> and<span class="math inline">\(\ \nu*2LM_{\upsilon}Q - \left\lfloor K^{NZ}/2 \right\rfloor\)</span> highest priority bits of<span class="math inline">\(\left\{ i_{1,7,l}:l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon \right\}\)</span>, in decreasing order of priority based on the corresponding function <span class="math inline">\(Pri(l,i,f)\)</span> for <span class="math inline">\(N_{4} = 1\)</span> or <span class="math inline">\(Pri(l,i,f,j)\)</span> for <span class="math inline">\(N_{4} &gt; 1\)</span> defined in clause 5.2.3 of TS 38.214 [6], if present and if reported;</p>
<p>The second time-domain subband differential CQI of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number, as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-8B, if present and if reported</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;"><p>CSI report #n</p>
<p>CSI part 2, group 2</p></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><p>The following PMI fields <span class="math inline">\(X_{2}\)</span>, from left to right, as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-1C<span class="math inline">\(:\ \min\left( K^{NZ} - v,\ \left\lfloor \frac{K^{NZ}}{2} \right\rfloor \right) \times 3\)</span> lowest priority bits of <span class="math inline">\(\left\{ i_{2,4,l}:l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon \right\},\min\left( K^{NZ} - v,\ \left\lfloor \frac{K^{NZ}}{2} \right\rfloor \right) \times 4\)</span> lowest priority bits of <span class="math inline">\(\left\{ i_{2,5,l}:l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon \right\}\)</span> and <span class="math inline">\(\left\lfloor K^{NZ}/2 \right\rfloor\)</span> lowest priority bits of <span class="math inline">\(\left\{ i_{1,7,l}:l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon \right\}\)</span>, in decreasing order of priority based on the corresponding function <span class="math inline">\(Pri(l,i,f)\)</span> for <span class="math inline">\(N_{4} = 1\)</span> or <span class="math inline">\(Pri(l,i,f,j)\)</span> for <span class="math inline">\(N_{4} &gt; 1\)</span> defined in clause 5.2.3 of TS 38.214 [6], if reported;</p>
<p>The second time-domain subband differential CQI of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number, as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-8B, if present and if reported</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

Table 6.3.2.1.2-5H: Mapping order of CSI fields of one CSI report containing $\mathbf{N}_{\mathbf{n}}^{\mathbf{sub}}$ CSI sub-report(s), CSI part 2 subband

<th rowspan="14" style="text-align: center;"><p>CSI report #n</p>

</tr>
<tr>

</tr>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;">PMI subband information fields <span class="math inline">\(X_{2}\)</span> of CSI sub-report #2 of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1/1A/1B/2/2A, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports of CSI sub-report #2 according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number, if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>subbandPMI</em> and if reported</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;">…</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;">Subband differential CQI of CSI sub-report #<span class="math inline">\(N_{n}^{sub}\)</span> for the second TB of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number, as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3/3C/4, if <em>cqi-FormatIndicator=subbandCQI</em> and if reported</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;">PMI subband information fields <span class="math inline">\(X_{2}\)</span> of CSI sub-report #<span class="math inline">\(N_{n}^{sub}\)</span> of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1/1A/1B/2/2A, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports of CSI sub-report #<span class="math inline">\(N_{n}^{sub}\)</span> according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number, if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>subbandPMI</em> and if reported</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;">Subband differential CQI of CSI sub-report #1 for the second TB of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number, as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3/3C/4, if <em>cqi-FormatIndicator=subbandCQI</em> and if reported</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;">PMI subband information fields <span class="math inline">\(X_{2}\)</span> of CSI sub-report #1 of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1/1A/1B/2/2A, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports of CSI sub-report #1 according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number, if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>subbandPMI</em> and if reported</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;">Subband differential CQI of CSI sub-report #2 for the second TB of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number, as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3/3C4, if <em>cqi-FormatIndicator=subbandCQI</em> and if reported</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;">PMI subband information fields <span class="math inline">\(X_{2}\)</span> of CSI sub-report #2 of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1/1A/1B/2/2A, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports of CSI sub-report #2 according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number, if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>subbandPMI</em> and if reported</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;">…</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;">Subband differential CQI of CSI sub-report #<span class="math inline">\(N_{n}^{sub}\)</span> for the second TB of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number, as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-3/3C/4, if <em>cqi-FormatIndicator=subbandCQI</em> and if reported</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;">PMI subband information fields <span class="math inline">\(X_{2}\)</span> of CSI sub-report #<span class="math inline">\(N_{n}^{sub}\)</span> of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1/1A/1B/2/2A, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports of CSI sub-report #<span class="math inline">\(N_{n}^{sub}\)</span> according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number, if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>subbandPMI</em> and if reported</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td colspan="2"><p>Note:</p>
<p>Subbands for given CSI report <em>n</em> indicated by the higher layer parameter <em>csi-ReportingBand</em> with value set to '1' are numbered continuously in the increasing order with the lowest subband of <em>csi-ReportingBand</em> with value set to '1' as subband 0.</p>
<p>CSI sub-report #1, CSI sub-report #2, …, CSI sub-report #<span class="math inline">\(N_{n}^{sub}\)</span> correspond to the CSI sub-reports in increasing order of <em>reportSubConfigId</em>.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

</tr>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;">PMI subband information fields <span class="math inline">\(X_{2}\)</span> of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number for the first CRI among the non-reported <em>M<sub>R</sub></em> CRIs, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number for the first CRI among the non-reported <em>M<sub>R</sub></em> CRIs, if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>subbandPMI</em> and if reported</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;">…</th>
</tr>

</tr>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;">PMI subband information fields <span class="math inline">\(X_{2}\)</span> of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number for the <em>M<sub>R</sub></em>-th CRI among the non-reported <em>M<sub>R</sub></em> CRIs, from left to right as in Table 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number for the <em>M<sub>R</sub></em>-th CRI among the non-reported <em>M<sub>R</sub></em> CRIs, if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>subbandPMI</em> and if reported</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;">Subband differential CQI for the second TB of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number for the <em>M<sub>R</sub></em>-th CRI among the non-reported <em>M<sub>R</sub></em> CRIs, as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-8C, if <em>cqi-FormatIndicator=subbandCQI</em> and if reported</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;">PMI subband information fields <span class="math inline">\(X_{2}\)</span> of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number for the <em>M<sub>R</sub></em>-th CRI among the non-reported <em>M<sub>R</sub></em> CRIs, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number for the <em>M<sub>R</sub></em>-th CRI among the non-reported <em>M<sub>R</sub></em> CRIs, if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>subbandPMI</em> and if reported</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;">…</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;">Subband differential CQI for the second TB of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number for the first reported CRI, as in Tables 6.3.2.1.2-8C, if <em>cqi-FormatIndicator=subbandCQI</em> and if reported</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;">PMI subband information fields <span class="math inline">\(X_{2}\)</span> of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number for the first reported CRI, from left to right as in Table 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number for the first reported CRI, if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>subbandPMI</em> and if reported</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;">Subband differential CQI for the second TB of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number for the first reported CRI, as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-8C, if <em>cqi-FormatIndicator=subbandCQI</em> and if reported</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;">PMI subband information fields <span class="math inline">\(X_{2}\)</span> of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number for the first reported CRI, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number for the first reported CRI, if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>subbandPMI</em> and if reported</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;">…</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;">Subband differential CQI for the second TB of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number for the (<em>M</em>-<em>M<sub>R</sub></em>)-th reported CRI, as in Tables 6.3.2.1.2-8C, if <em>cqi-FormatIndicator=subbandCQI</em> and if reported</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;">PMI subband information fields <span class="math inline">\(X_{2}\)</span> of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number for the (<em>M</em>-<em>M<sub>R</sub></em>)-th reported CRI, from left to right as in Table 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] of all even subbands with increasing order of subband number for the (<em>M</em>-<em>M<sub>R</sub></em>)-th reported CRI, if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>subbandPMI</em> and if reported</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;">Subband differential CQI for the second TB of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number for the (<em>M</em>-<em>M<sub>R</sub></em>)-th reported CRI, as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-8C, if <em>cqi-FormatIndicator=subbandCQI</em> and if reported</th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;">PMI subband information fields <span class="math inline">\(X_{2}\)</span> of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number for the (<em>M</em>-<em>M<sub>R</sub></em>)-th reported CRI, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.1.1.2-1, or codebook index for 2 antenna ports according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 in [6, TS38.214] of all odd subbands with increasing order of subband number for the (<em>M</em>-<em>M<sub>R</sub></em>)-th reported CRI, if <em>pmi-FormatIndicator=</em> <em>subbandPMI</em> and if reported</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td colspan="2"><p>NOTE1: Subbands for given CSI report <em>n</em> indicated by the higher layer parameter <em>csi-ReportingBand</em> are numbered continuously in the increasing order with the lowest subband of <em>csi-ReportingBand</em> as subband 0.</p>
<p>NOTE2: <em>M<sub>R</sub></em> is set to 0 if higher layer parameter <em>mr-SelectedResources</em> is not configured for the CSI report.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

Table 6.3.2.1.2-5J: Mapping order of CSI fields of one CSI report configured with *valueOfM*, CSI part 2 of *codebookType=typeII-r16*

<table style="width:98%;">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 14%" />
<col style="width: 83%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;"><strong>CSI report number</strong></th>
<th style="text-align: center;"><strong>CSI fields</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;"><p>CSI report #n</p>
<p>CSI part 2, group 0</p></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><p>PMI fields <span class="math inline">\(X_{1}\)</span> for the first CRI among the non-reported <em>M<sub>R</sub></em> CRIs, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.2.1.2-1A, if reported</p>
<p>…</p>
<p>PMI fields <span class="math inline">\(X_{1}\)</span> for the <em>M<sub>R</sub></em>-th CRI among the non-reported <em>M<sub>R</sub></em> CRIs, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.2.1.2-1A, if reported</p>
<p>PMI fields <span class="math inline">\(X_{1}\)</span> for the first reported CRI, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.2.1.2-1A, if reported</p>
<p>…</p>
<p>PMI fields <span class="math inline">\(X_{1}\)</span> for the (<em>M</em>-<em>M<sub>R</sub></em>)-th reported CRI, from left to right as in Tables 6.3.2.1.2-1A, if reported</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;"><p>CSI report #n</p>
<p>CSI part 2, group 1</p></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><p>The following PMI fields <span class="math inline">\(X_{2}\)</span> for the first CRI among the non-reported <em>M<sub>R</sub></em> CRIs, from left to right, as in Tables 6.3.2.1.2-1A:<span class="math inline">\(\ \left\{ i_{2,3,l}:l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon \right\}\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\(i_{1,5}\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\(\left\{ i_{1,6,l}:l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon \right\}\)</span> and <span class="math inline">\(max(0,\left\lceil \frac{K^{NZ}}{2} \right\rceil - \upsilon) \times 3\)</span> highest priority bits of</p>
<p><span class="math inline">\(\left\{ i_{2,4,l}:l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon \right\},max(0,\left\lceil \frac{K^{NZ}}{2} \right\rceil - \upsilon) \times 4\)</span> highest priority bits of <span class="math inline">\(\{ i_{2,5,l}:l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon\}\)</span> and<span class="math inline">\(\ \nu*2LM_{\upsilon} - \left\lfloor K^{NZ}/2 \right\rfloor\)</span> highest priority bits of<span class="math inline">\(\left\{ i_{1,7,l}:l = 1,\ldots,\upsilon \right\}\)</span>, in decreasing order of priority based on the corresponding function <span class="math inline">\(Pri(l,i,f)\)</span> defined in clause 5.2.3 of TS 38.214 [6], if reported</p></td>

Table 6.3.2.1.2-6: Mapping order of CSI reports to UCI bit sequence $a_{0}^{(1)},a_{1}^{(1)},a_{2}^{(1)},a_{3}^{(1)},...,a_{A^{(1)}-1}^{(1)}$,  
with two-part CSI report(s)

<table style="width:74%;">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 19%" />
<col style="width: 54%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>UCI bit sequence</th>
<th>CSI report number</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td rowspan="4">$\begin{matrix} a_{0}^{(1)} \\ a_{1}^{(1)} \\ a_{2}^{(1)} \\ a_{3}^{(1)} \\ \ \vdots  \\ a_{A^{(1)}-1}^{(1)} \end{matrix}$</td>
<td>CSI part 1 of CSI report #1 as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-3/3A/3B/3C/3D/3E/3F/3G/3H/3I/3J/3K or Table 6.3.1.1.2-7/8/8A/8B/8C/8D/8E/8F/8G/8H</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>CSI part 1 of CSI report #2 as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-3/3A/3B/3C/3D/3E/3F/3G/3H/3I/3J/3K or Table 6.3.1.1.2-8/8A/8B/8C/8D/8E/8F/8G/8H</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>…</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>CSI part 1 of CSI report #n as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-3/3A/3B/3C/3D/3E/3F/3G/3H/3I/3J/3K or Table 6.3.1.1.2-8/8A/8B/8C/8D/8E/8F/8G/8H</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="2">NOTE: For a CSI report #i containing <span class="math inline">\(N_{i}^{sub}\)</span> CSI sub-reports, where <span class="math inline">\(i \in \{ 1,2,\ldots,n\}\)</span>, CSI part 1 of all CSI sub-reports are mapped to the corresponding segment of the UCI bit sequence of CSI report #i, from upper part to lower part of the segment, in increasing order of CSI sub-report number. CSI sub-report #1, CSI sub-report #2, …, CSI sub-report #<span class="math inline">\(N_{i}^{sub}\)</span> correspond to the CSI sub-reports in increasing order of <em>reportSubConfigId</em>.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

where CSI report \#1, CSI report \#2, …, CSI report \#n in Table 6.3.2.1.2-6 correspond to the CSI reports in increasing order of CSI report priority values according to Clause 5.2.5 of \[6, TS38.214\].

Table 6.3.2.1.2-7: Mapping order of CSI reports to UCI bit sequence $a_{0}^{(2)},a_{1}^{(2)},a_{2}^{(2)},a_{3}^{(2)},...,a_{A^{(2)}-1}^{(2)}$,  
with two-part CSI report(s)

<table style="width:74%;">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 19%" />
<col style="width: 54%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th>UCI bit sequence</th>
<th>CSI report number</th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td rowspan="8">$\begin{matrix} a_{0}^{(2)} \\ a_{1}^{(2)} \\ a_{2}^{(2)} \\ a_{3}^{(2)} \\ \ \vdots  \\ a_{A^{(2)}-1}^{(2)} \end{matrix}$</td>
<td><p>CSI report #1, CSI part 2 wideband, as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-4/4A/4B/4C,</p>
<p>or CSI part 2 with group 0, as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-5A/5B/5E/5F/5G/5J,<br />
if CSI part 2 exists for CSI report #1</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><p>CSI report #2, CSI part 2 wideband, as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-4/4A/4B/4C,</p>
<p>or CSI part 2 with group 0, as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-5A/5B/5E/5F/5G/5J,<br />
if CSI part 2 exists for CSI report #2</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>…</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><p>CSI report #n, CSI part 2 wideband, as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-4/4A/4B/4C,</p>
<p>or CSI part 2 with group 0, as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-5A/5B/5E/5F/5G/5J,<br />
if CSI part 2 exists for CSI report #n</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><p>CSI report #1, CSI part 2 subband, as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-5/5C/5D/5H/5I,</p>
<p>or CSI part 2 with group 1 and 2, as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-5A/5B/5E/5F/5G/5J,<br />
if CSI part 2 exists for CSI report #1</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><p>CSI report #2, CSI part 2 subband, as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-5/5C/5D/5H/5I,</p>
<p>or CSI part 2 with group 1 and 2, as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-5A/5B/5E/5F/5G/5J,</p>
<p>if CSI part 2 exists for CSI report #2</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td>…</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td><p>CSI report #n, CSI part 2 subband, as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-5/5C/5D/5H/5I,</p>
<p>or CSI part 2 with group 1 and 2, as in Table 6.3.2.1.2-5A/5B/5E/5F/5G/5J,</p>
<p>if CSI part 2 exists for CSI report #n</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="2"><p>NOTE: For a CSI report #i containing <span class="math inline">\(N_{i}^{sub}\)</span> CSI sub-reports, where <span class="math inline">\(i \in \{ 1,2,\ldots,n\}\)</span>,</p>
<blockquote>
<p>- CSI part 2 wideband of all CSI sub-reports are mapped to the corresponding segment of the UCI bit sequence of CSI report #i, from upper part to lower part of the segment, in increasing order of CSI sub-report number;</p>
<p>- CSI sub-report #1, CSI sub-report #2, …, CSI sub-report #<span class="math inline">\(N_{i}^{sub}\)</span> correspond to the CSI sub-reports in increasing order of <em>reportSubConfigId</em>.</p>
</blockquote></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

where CSI report \#1, CSI report \#2, …, CSI report \#n in Table 6.3.2.1.2-7 correspond to the CSI reports in increasing order of CSI report priority values according to Clause 5.2.5 of \[6, TS38.214\].

The bitwidth for RI/CQI of *codebookType= typeII-r16,* *codebookType=typeII-PortSelection-r16* or *codebookType=* *eTypeII-r19* is provided in Table 6.3.2.1.2-8.

Table 6.3.2.1.2-8: RI and CQI of codebookType=typeII-r16, typeII-PortSelection-r16 or codebookType= eTypeII-r19

<table style="width:82%;">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 45%" />
<col style="width: 36%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;"><strong>Field</strong></th>
<th style="text-align: center;"><strong>Bitwidth</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Rank Indicator</td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\min\left( 2,\left\lceil \log_{2}n_{RI} \right\rceil \right)\]</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Wide-band CQI</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">4</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Subband differential CQI</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">2</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Indicator of the total number of non-zero coefficients summed across all layers <span class="math inline">\(K^{NZ}\)</span></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><p><span class="math inline">\(\left\lceil \log_{2}{(K_{0})} \right\rceil\)</span> if max allowed rank is 1;</p>
<p><span class="math inline">\(\left\lceil \log_{2}{({2K}_{0})} \right\rceil\)</span> otherwise</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="2" style="text-align: center;">NOTE: <span class="math inline">\(n_{RI}\)</span> is the number of allowed rank indicator values according to Clauses 5.2.2.2.5, 5.2.2.2.5a and 5.2.2.2.6 [6, TS 38.214],<span class="math inline">\(\ K_{0} = \left\lceil 2L\left\lceil p_{1} \times \frac{N_{3}}{R} \right\rceil\beta \right\rceil\)</span>, where <span class="math inline">\(p_{1}\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\(N_{3}\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\(R\)</span>, and <span class="math inline">\(\beta\)</span> are given by Clause 5.2.2.2.5, 5.2.2.2.5a and 5.2.2.2.6 in [6, TS 38.214]. The values of the rank indicator field are mapped to allowed rank indicator values with increasing order, where '0' is mapped to the smallest allowed rank indicator value. The values of the <span class="math inline">\(K^{NZ}\)</span> indicator field are mapped to the allowed values of <span class="math inline">\(K^{NZ}\)</span>, according to Clauses 5.2.2.2.5, 5.2.2.2.5a and 5.2.2.2.6 [6, TS 38.214], with increasing order, where '0' is mapped to <span class="math inline">\(K^{NZ} = 1\)</span>.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

The bitwidth for RI/CQI of *codebookType*= *typeII-CJT* is provided in Table 6.3.2.1.2-8A.

Table 6.3.2.1.2-8A: RI and CQI of *codebookType= typeII-CJT*

<table style="width:85%;">
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 45%" />
<col style="width: 39%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;"><strong>Field</strong></th>
<th style="text-align: center;"><strong>Bitwidth</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Rank Indicator</td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\min\left( 2,\left\lceil \log_{2}n_{RI} \right\rceil \right)\]</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Wide-band CQI</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">4</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Subband differential CQI</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">2</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Indicator of the total number of non-zero coefficients summed across all layers, and all CSI-RS resources if configured, <span class="math inline">\(K^{NZ}\)</span></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><p><span class="math inline">\(\left\lceil \log_{2}\left\lceil 2\beta M_{1}L_{\max} \right\rceil \right\rceil\)</span> if max allowed rank is 1;</p>
<p><span class="math inline">\(\left\lceil {\log_{2}2}\left\lceil 2\beta M_{1}L_{\max} \right\rceil \right\rceil\)</span>otherwise</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="2" style="text-align: center;">NOTE: <span class="math inline">\(n_{RI}\)</span> is the number of allowed rank indicator values according to Clause 5.2.2.2.8 TS 38.214 [6],<span class="math inline">\(\ L_{\max}\)</span> is the maximum of <span class="math inline">\(\sum_{n = 1}^{N_{TRP}}L_{n}\)</span> for all <span class="math inline">\(N_{L}\)</span> configured <span class="math inline">\(L_{n}\)</span> combinations, where<span class="math inline">\(\ \left\{ L_{n} \right\}_{n = 1,\ldots,\ N_{TRP}}\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\(M_{1}\)</span>, and <span class="math inline">\(\beta\)</span> are given by Clause 5.2.2.2.8 in TS 38.214 [6]. The values of the rank indicator field are mapped to allowed rank indicator values with increasing order, where '0' is mapped to the smallest allowed rank indicator value. The values of the <span class="math inline">\(K^{NZ}\)</span> indicator field are mapped to the allowed values of <span class="math inline">\(K^{NZ}\)</span>, according to Clause 5.2.2.2.8 TS 38.214 [6], with increasing order, where '0' is mapped to <span class="math inline">\(K^{NZ} = 1\)</span>.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

The bitwidth for RI/CQI of *codebookType*= *typeII-Doppler* and *codebookType=* *typeII-Doppler-r19* is provided in Table 6.3.2.1.2-8B.

<td style="text-align: center;">Indicator of the total number of non-zero coefficients summed across all layers <span class="math inline">\(K^{NZ}\)</span></td>

<p><span class="math inline">\(\left\lceil \log_{2}{({2K}_{0})} \right\rceil\)</span> otherwise</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="2" style="text-align: center;">NOTE: <span class="math inline">\(n_{RI}\)</span> is the number of allowed rank indicator values according to Clause 5.2.2.2.10 and 5.2.2.2.11a in [6, TS 38.214],<span class="math inline">\(\ K_{0} = \left\lceil 2L\left\lceil p_{1} \times \frac{N_{3}}{R} \right\rceil\beta Q \right\rceil\)</span>, where <span class="math inline">\(p_{1}\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\(N_{3}\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\(R\)</span>, <span class="math inline">\(\beta\)</span> and <span class="math inline">\(Q\)</span> are given by Clause 5.2.2.2.10 and 5.2.2.2.11a in [6, TS 38.214]. The values of the rank indicator field are mapped to allowed rank indicator values with increasing order, where '0' is mapped to the smallest allowed rank indicator value. The values of the <span class="math inline">\(K^{NZ}\)</span> indicator field are mapped to the allowed values of <span class="math inline">\(K^{NZ}\)</span>, according to Clause 5.2.2.2.10 and 5.2.2.2.11a in [6, TS 38.214], with increasing order, where '0' is mapped to <span class="math inline">\(K^{NZ} = 1\)</span>.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

<thead>
<tr>
<th rowspan="3" style="text-align: center;"><strong>Field</strong></th>
<th colspan="4" style="text-align: center;"><strong>Bitwidth</strong></th>
</tr>
<tr>
<th rowspan="2" style="text-align: center;"><strong>2 antenna ports</strong></th>
<th rowspan="2" style="text-align: center;"><strong>4 antenna ports</strong></th>

<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Rank Indicator</td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\min\left( 1,\left\lceil \log_{2}n_{RI} \right\rceil\  \right)\]</span></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\min\left( 2,\left\lceil \log_{2}n_{RI} \right\rceil\  \right)\]</span></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\left\lceil \log_{2}n_{RI} \right\rceil\]</span></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\left\lceil \log_{2}n_{RI} \right\rceil\]</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Layer Indicator</td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\left\lceil \log_{2}\upsilon \right\rceil\]</span></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\min\left( 2,\left\lceil \log_{2}\upsilon \right\rceil\  \right)\]</span></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\min\left( 2,\left\lceil \log_{2}\upsilon \right\rceil\  \right)\]</span></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\min\left( 2,\left\lceil \log_{2}\upsilon \right\rceil\  \right)\]</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Wide-band CQI for the first TB</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">4</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">4</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">4</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">4</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Wideband CQI for the second TB</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">0</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">0</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">0</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">4</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Subband differential CQI for the first TB</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">2</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">2</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">2</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">2</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Subband differential CQI for the second TB</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">0</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">0</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">0</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">2</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">CRI</td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\left\lceil \log_{2}\left( K_{S}^{CSI - RS} - M_{R} \right) \right\rceil\]</span></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\left\lceil \log_{2}\left( K_{S}^{CSI - RS} - M_{R} \right) \right\rceil\]</span></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\left\lceil \log_{2}\left( K_{S}^{CSI - RS} - M_{R} \right) \right\rceil\]</span></td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><span class="math display">\[\left\lceil \log_{2}\left( K_{S}^{CSI - RS} - M_{R} \right) \right\rceil\]</span></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td colspan="5" style="text-align: center;">NOTE: <span class="math inline">\(n_{RI}\)</span> is the number of allowed rank indicator values according to Clause 5.2.2.2.1 [6, TS 38.214]. <span class="math inline">\(\upsilon\)</span> is the value of the rank. The value of <span class="math inline">\(K_{S}^{CSI - RS}\)</span> is the number of CSI-RS resources in the corresponding resource set, <span class="math inline">\(M_{R}\)</span> is the number of CSI-RS resources that are configured to report their corresponding CSI (except CRI), and is set to 0 if higher layer parameter <em>mrSelectedResources</em> is not configured for the CSI report. The values of the rank indicator field are mapped to allowed rank indicator values with increasing order, where '0' is mapped to the smallest allowed rank indicator value.</td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

|    **Field**    | **Bitwidth** |
|:---------------:|:------------:|
| Amplitude value |      4       |
|   Phase value   |      4       |

The bitwidth for *reportQuantity*=*cjtc-Dd* is provided in Table 6.3.2.1.2-11.

Table 6.3.2.1.2-11: Index of reference CSI-RS resource set for tracking, delay offset and inside/outside indicator for *reportQuantity*=*cjtc-Dd*

| **Field** | **Bitwidth** |
|:--:|:--:|
| Index of reference CSI-RS resource set for tracking | $\left\lceil \log_{2}N_{TRP} \right\rceil$, where $N_{TRP}$ is the number of CSI-RS resource sets for tracking configured for the CSI report. |
| Delay offset | $\left\lceil \log_{2}M_{D} \right\rceil$, where $M_{D}$ is configured by *valueOfMD*. |
| Inside/outside CP indicator | 1 |

The bitwidth for *reportQuantity*=*cjtc-F* is provided in Table 6.3.2.1.2-12.

Table 6.3.2.1.2-12: Index of reference CSI-RS resource set for tracking,, and frequency offset for *reportQuantity*=*cjtc-F*

| **Field** | **Bitwidth** |
|:--:|:--:|
| Index of reference CSI-RS resource set for tracking | $\left\lceil \log_{2}N_{TRP} \right\rceil$, where $N_{TRP}$ is the number of CSI-RS resource sets for tracking configured for the CSI report. |
| Frequency offset | $\left\lceil \log_{2}M_{FO} \right\rceil$, where $M_{FO}$ is configured by *valueofMFO*. |

The bitwidth for *reportQuantity*=*cjtc-P* is provided in Table 6.3.2.1.2-13.

Table 6.3.2.1.2-13: Reference CSI-RS resource index, and phase offset for *reportQuantity*=*cjtc-P*

| **Field** | **Bitwidth** |
|:--:|:--:|
| Reference CSI-RS resource index | $\left\lceil \log_{2}N_{TRP} \right\rceil$, where $N_{TRP}$ is the number of CSI-RS resources configured for the CSI report. |
| Wideband Phase offset | $\left\lceil \log_{2}M_{\Phi} \right\rceil$, where $M_{\Phi}$ is configured by *valueOfMPhi*. |
| Subband Phase offset | $\left\lceil \log_{2}M_{\Phi} \right\rceil$, where $M_{\Phi}$ is configured by *valueOfMPhi*. |

The bitwidth for CRI, SSBRI, RSRP, differential RSRP, CSI report configuration indicator and Condition met indicator are provided in Table 6.3.2.1.2-14.

##### 6.3.2.1.3 CG-UCI

For CG-UCI bits transmitted on a CG PUSCH when the higher layer parameter *cg-RetransmissionTimer* is configured, the CG-UCI bit sequence $a_{0},\ a_{1},\ a_{2},\ a_{3},\ \ldots,a_{A - 1}\$ is determined as follows:

- set $a_{i} = {\widetilde{o}}_{i}^{CG - UCI}$ for $i = 0,1,\ \ldots,\ O^{CG - UCI} - 1$ and $A = O^{CG - UCI}$, where the CG-UCI bit sequence ${\widetilde{o}}_{0}^{CG - UCI},\ {\widetilde{o}}_{1}^{CG - UCI},\ \ldots,\ {\widetilde{o}}_{O^{CG - UCI} - 1}^{CG - UCI}$ is given by Table 6.3.2.1.3-1, mapped in the order from upper part to lower part.

Table 6.3.2.1.3-1: Mapping order of CG-UCI fields

<table>
<colgroup>
<col style="width: 47%" />
<col style="width: 52%" />
</colgroup>
<thead>
<tr>
<th style="text-align: center;"><strong>Field</strong></th>
<th style="text-align: center;"><strong>Bitwidth</strong></th>
</tr>
</thead>
<tbody>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">HARQ process number</td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><p>5 if <em>nrofHARQ-Processes-v1700</em> in <em>ConfiguredGrantConfig</em> is configured;</p>
<p>4 otherwise.</p></td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Redundancy version</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">2</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">New data indicator</td>
<td style="text-align: center;">1</td>
</tr>
<tr>
<td style="text-align: center;">Channel Occupancy Time (COT) sharing information</td>
<td style="text-align: center;"><p><span class="math inline">\(\left\lceil \log_{2}C \right\rceil\)</span> if both higher layer parameter <em>ul-toDL-COT-SharingED-Threshold</em> and higher layer parameter <em>cg-COT-SharingList</em> are configured, or if both higher layer parameter <em>semiStaticChannelAccessConfigUE</em> and higher layer parameter <em>cg-COT-SharingList</em> are configured, or if higher layer parameter <em>cg-COT-SharingList</em> is configured in frequency range 2-2, where <em>C</em> is the number of combinations configured in <em>cg-COT-SharingList;</em></p>
<p>1 if higher layer parameter <em>ul-toDL-COT-SharingED-Threshold</em> is not configured, and if higher layer parameter <em>semiStaticChannelAccessConfigUE</em> is not configured, and if higher layer parameter <em>cg-COT-SharingOffset</em> is configured;</p>
<p>0 otherwise.</p>
<p>If a UE indicates COT sharing other than "no sharing" in a CG PUSCH within the UE's initiated COT, the UE should provide consistent COT sharing information in all the subsequent CG PUSCHs, if any, occurring within the same UE's initiated COT such that the same DL starting point and duration are maintained.</p></td>
</tr>
</tbody>
</table>

##### 6.3.2.1.3A UTO-UCI

For UTO-UCI bits transmitted on a CG PUSCH when the higher layer parameter *nrofBitsInUTO-UCI* is configured, the UTO-UCI bit sequence $a_{0},\ a_{1},\ a_{2},\ a_{3},\ \ldots,a_{A - 1}\$ is determined as follows:

> \- set $a_{i} = {\widetilde{o}}_{i}^{UTO - UCI}$ for $i = 0,1,\ \ldots,\ O^{UTO - UCI} - 1$ and $A = O^{UTO - UCI}$, where $O^{UTO - UCI}$ is provided by *nrofBitsInUTO-UCI*, and the UTO-UCI bit sequence ${\widetilde{o}}_{0}^{UTO - UCI},\ {\widetilde{o}}_{1}^{UTO - UCI},\ \ldots,\ {\widetilde{o}}_{O^{UTO - UCI} - 1}^{UTO - UCI}$ is given by clause 9.3.1 of \[5, TS 38.213\].

##### 6.3.2.1.3B UEIRI

If UEIRI bits are transmitted on a PUSCH, the UEIRI bit sequence $a_{0},\ a_{1},\ a_{2},\ a_{3},\ \ldots,a_{A - 1}\$ is determined as follows:

> \- set $a_{i} = {\widetilde{o}}_{i}^{UEIRI}$ for $i = 0,1,\ \ldots,\ O^{UEIRI} - 1$ and $A = O^{UEIRI}$, where $O^{UEIRI}$ is the number of PUCCH resources for UEIRI given by Clause 9 of \[5, TS38.213\], and the UEIRI bit sequence ${\widetilde{o}}_{0}^{UEIRI},\ {\widetilde{o}}_{1}^{UEIRI},\ \ldots,\ {\widetilde{o}}_{O^{UEIRI} - 1}^{UEIRI}$ is mapped to the PUCCH resources according to an ascending order of *pucch-ResourceId*. If the associated UEIRI of ${\widetilde{o}}_{i}^{UEIRI}$ is positive, ${\widetilde{o}}_{i}^{UEIRI} = 1$; otherwise, ${\widetilde{o}}_{i}^{UEIRI} = 0$.

##### 6.3.2.1.4 HARQ-ACK and CG-UCI/UTO-UCI

If the higher layer parameter *nrofBitsInUTO-UCI* is configured, the procedure in this clause 6.3.2.1.4 applies by replacing CG-UCI with UTO-UCI in all the notations and texts, replacing "When higher layer parameter *cg-UCI-Multiplexing* is configured" with "When UTO-UCI and HARQ-ACK have the same priority index and are jointly encoded and transmitted on a PUSCH" and replacing "is given by Table 6.3.2.1.3-1 mapped in the order from upper part to lower part " with "is given by Clause 9.3.1 of \[5, TS 38.213\]".

If a UE would multiplex UEIRI and HARQ-ACK in a PUSCH \[5, TS 38.213\], the procedure in this clause 6.3.2.1.4 applies by replacing CG-UCI with UEIRI in all the notations and texts assuming higher layer parameter *cg-UCI-Multiplexing* is configured and replacing "is given by Table 6.3.2.1.3-1 mapped in the order from upper part to lower part " with "is determined as described in clause 6.3.2.1.3B ".

When higher layer parameter *cg-UCI-Multiplexing* is configured, the UCI bit sequence $a_{0},\ a_{1},\ a_{2},\ a_{3},\ \ldots,a_{A - 1}\$ is determined as follows, where $A = {O^{CG - UCI} + O}^{ACK}$.

- The CG-UCI bits are mapped to the UCI bit sequence$a_{0},\ a_{1},\ a_{2},\ a_{3},\ \ldots,a_{O^{CG - UCI} - 1}\$, where $a_{i} = {\widetilde{o}}_{i}^{CG - UCI\ }$for $i = 0,1,\ \ldots,\ O^{CG - UCI} - 1$. The CG-UCI bit sequence ${\widetilde{o}}_{0}^{CG - UCI},\ {\widetilde{o}}_{1}^{CG - UCI},\ \ldots,\ {\widetilde{o}}_{O^{CG - UCI} - 1}^{CG - UCI}$ is given by Table 6.3.2.1.3-1 mapped in the order from upper part to lower part, and $O^{CG - UCI}$ is number of CG-UCI bits;

- The HARQ-ACK bits are mapped to the UCI bit sequence $a_{O^{CG - UCI}},\ a_{O^{CG - UCI} + 1},\ \ \ldots,a_{{O^{CG - UCI} + O}^{ACK} - 1}$, where $a_{i + O^{CG - UCI}} = {\widetilde{o}}_{i}^{ACK}$ for $i = 0,1,\ \ldots,\ O^{ACK} - 1$. The HARQ-ACK bit sequence ${\widetilde{o}}_{0}^{ACK},\ {\widetilde{o}}_{1}^{ACK},\ \ldots,\ {\widetilde{o}}_{O^{ACK} - 1}^{ACK}$ is given by Clause 9.1 of \[5, TS38.213\], and $O^{ACK}$ is number of HARQ-ACK bits.

##### 6.3.2.1.5 UCI with different priority indexes

If the higher layer parameter *nrofBitsInUTO-UCI* is configured, the procedure in this clause 6.3.2.1.5 applies by replacing CG-UCI with UTO-UCI in all the notations and texts, and replacing "is given by Table 6.3.2.1.3-1 mapped in the order from upper part to lower part" with "is given by clause 9.3.1 of \[5, TS 38.213\]".

If *uci-MuxWithDiffPrio* is configured, and HARQ-ACK bits associated with priority index 0, and CSI part 1 if any are transmitted on a PUSCH associated with priority index 1, the following UCI bit sequences are generated, $a_{0}^{(1)},\ a_{1}^{(1)},\ a_{2}^{(1)},\ a_{3}^{(1)},\ \ldots,a_{A^{(1)} - 1}^{(1)}$, and $a_{0}^{(2)},\ a_{1}^{(2)},\ a_{2}^{(2)},\ a_{3}^{(2)},\ \ldots,a_{A^{(2)} - 1}^{(2)}$ if any, according to the following:

- If CSI part 1 is also transmitted on the PUSCH,

- Set $a_{i}^{(1)}$ for $i = 0,1,\ldots,A^{(1)} - 1$ as the bit sequence of CSI part 1, where the CSI fields of all CSI reports, in the order from upper part to lower part in Table 6.3.2.1.2-6, are mapped to the UCI bit sequence $a_{0}^{(1)},\ a_{1}^{(1)},\ a_{2}^{(1)},\ a_{3}^{(1)},\ \ldots,a_{A^{(1)} - 1}^{(1)}$ starting with $a_{0}^{(1)}$.

- Set $a_{i}^{(2)} = {\widetilde{o}}_{i}^{ACK - LP}$ for $i = {0,1,\ldots,O}^{ACK - LP} - 1$ and $A^{(2)} = O^{ACK - LP}$, where the HARQ-ACK bit sequence ${\widetilde{o}}_{0}^{ACK - LP},\ {\widetilde{o}}_{1}^{ACK - LP},\ \ldots,\ {\widetilde{o}}_{O^{ACK - LP} - 1}^{ACK - LP}$ associated with priority index 0 is given by Clause 9.1 of \[5, TS 38.213\].

- Otherwise, set $a_{i}^{(1)} = {\widetilde{o}}_{i}^{ACK - LP}$ for $i = {0,1,\ldots,O}^{ACK - LP} - 1$ and $A^{(1)} = O^{ACK - LP}$, where the HARQ-ACK bit sequence ${\widetilde{o}}_{0}^{ACK - LP},\ {\widetilde{o}}_{1}^{ACK - LP},\ \ldots,\ {\widetilde{o}}_{O^{ACK - LP} - 1}^{ACK - LP}$ associated with priority index 0 is given by Clause 9.1 of \[5, TS 38.213\].

If *uci-MuxWithDiffPrio* is configured, and HARQ-ACK bits associated with priority index 1, and CSI if any are transmitted on a PUSCH associated with priority index 0, the following UCI bit sequences are generated,$\ a_{0},\ a_{1},\ a_{2},\ a_{3},\ \ldots,a_{A - 1}$, $a_{0}^{(1)},\ a_{1}^{(1)},\ a_{2}^{(1)},\ a_{3}^{(1)},\ \ldots,a_{A^{(1)} - 1}^{(1)}$ if any, and $a_{0}^{(2)},\ a_{1}^{(2)},\ a_{2}^{(2)},\ a_{3}^{(2)},\ \ldots,a_{A^{(2)} - 1}^{(2)}$ if any, according to the following:

- If HARQ-ACK bits associated with priority index 1 and CSI are transmitted on the PUSCH without UL-SCH and the CSI includes CSI part 1 without CSI part 2, and there is only one HARQ-ACK bit associated with priority index 1 given by Clause 9.1 of \[5, TS 38.213\], set $a_{0} = {\widetilde{o}}_{0}^{ACK - HP}$, $a_{1} = 0$, and $A = 2$; otherwise, set $a_{i} = {\widetilde{o}}_{i}^{ACK - HP}$ for $i = {0,1,\ldots,O}^{ACK - HP} - 1$ and $A = O^{ACK - HP}$, where the HARQ-ACK bit sequence ${\widetilde{o}}_{0}^{ACK - HP},\ {\widetilde{o}}_{1}^{ACK - HP},\ \ldots,\ {\widetilde{o}}_{O^{ACK - HP} - 1}^{ACK - HP}$ associated with priority index 1 is given by Clause 9.1 of \[5, TS 38.213\];

- Set $a_{i}^{(1)}$ for $i = 0,1,\ldots,A^{(1)} - 1$ as the bit sequence of CSI part 1, if CSI part 1 is also transmitted on the PUSCH, where the CSI fields of all CSI reports, in the order from upper part to lower part in Table 6.3.2.1.2-6, are mapped to the UCI bit sequence $a_{0}^{(1)},\ a_{1}^{(1)},\ a_{2}^{(1)},\ a_{3}^{(1)},\ \ldots,a_{A^{(1)} - 1}^{(1)}$ starting with $a_{0}^{(1)}$;

- Set $a_{i}^{(2)}$ for $i = 0,1,\ldots,A^{(2)} - 1$ as the bit sequence of CSI part 2, if CSI part 2 is also transmitted on the PUSCH, where the CSI fields of all CSI reports, in the order from upper part to lower part in Table 6.3.2.1.2-7, are mapped to the UCI bit sequence $a_{0}^{(2)},\ a_{1}^{(2)},\ a_{2}^{(2)},\ a_{3}^{(2)},\ \ldots,a_{A^{(1)} - 1}^{(2)}$ starting with $a_{0}^{(2)}$.

If *uci-MuxWithDiffPrio* is configured, and HARQ-ACK bits associated with priority index 0, HARQ-ACK bits associated with priority index 1 and/or CG-UCI associated with priority index 1, and CSI part 1 if any are transmitted on a PUSCH, the following UCI bit sequences are generated,$\ a_{0},\ a_{1},\ a_{2},\ a_{3},\ \ldots,a_{A - 1}$, $a_{0}^{(1)},\ a_{1}^{(1)},\ a_{2}^{(1)},\ a_{3}^{(1)},\ \ldots,a_{A^{(1)} - 1}^{(1)}$, and $a_{0}^{(2)},\ a_{1}^{(2)},\ a_{2}^{(2)},\ a_{3}^{(2)},\ \ldots,a_{A^{(2)} - 1}^{(2)}$ if any, according to the following:

- Set $a_{i} = {\widetilde{o}}_{i}^{ACK - HP}$ for $i = {0,1,\ldots,O}^{ACK - HP} - 1$ and $A = O^{ACK - HP}$ if HARQ-ACK bits associated with priority index 1 are transmitted without CG-UCI associated with priority index 1, where the HARQ-ACK bit sequence ${\widetilde{o}}_{0}^{ACK - HP},\ {\widetilde{o}}_{1}^{ACK - HP},\ \ldots,\ {\widetilde{o}}_{O^{ACK - HP} - 1}^{ACK - HP}$ associated with priority index 1 is given by Clause 9.1 of \[5, TS 38.213\];

- Set $a_{i} = {\widetilde{o}}_{i}^{CG - UCI}$ for $i = 0,1,\ldots,O^{CG - UCI} - 1$ and $A = O^{CG - UCI}$ if CG-UCI associated with priority index 1 is transmitted without HARQ-ACK bits associated with priority index 1, where the CG-UCI bit sequence ${\widetilde{o}}_{0}^{CG - UCI},\ {\widetilde{o}}_{1}^{CG - UCI},\ \ldots,\ {\widetilde{o}}_{O^{CG - UCI} - 1}^{CG - UCI}$ associated with priority index 1 is given by Table 6.3.2.1.3-1 mapped in the order from upper part to lower part;

- Set $a_{0},\ a_{1},\ a_{2},\ a_{3},\ \ldots,a_{A - 1}$ as follows, if both CG-UCI associated with priority index 1 and HARQ-ACK bits associated with priority index 1 are transmitted, where $A = O^{CG - UCI} + O^{ACK - HP}$

- The CG-UCI bits are mapped to the UCI bit sequence $a_{0},\ a_{1},\ a_{2},\ a_{3},\ \ldots,a_{O^{CG - UCI} - 1}$, where $a_{i} = {\widetilde{o}}_{i}^{CG - UCI\ }$for $i = 0,1,\ \ldots,\ O^{CG - UCI} - 1$. The CG-UCI bit sequence ${\widetilde{o}}_{0}^{CG - UCI},\ {\widetilde{o}}_{1}^{CG - UCI},\ \ldots,\ {\widetilde{o}}_{O^{CG - UCI} - 1}^{CG - UCI}$ is given by Table 6.3.2.1.3-1 mapped in the order from upper part to lower part, and $O^{CG - UCI}$ is number of CG-UCI bits

- The HARQ-ACK bits are mapped to the UCI bit sequence $a_{O^{CG - UCI}},\ a_{O^{CG - UCI} + 1},\ \ \ldots,a_{O^{CG - UCI} + O^{ACK - HP} - 1}$, where $a_{i + O^{CG - UCI}} = {\widetilde{o}}_{i}^{ACK - HP}$ for $i = 0,1,\ \ldots,\ O^{ACK - HP} - 1$. The HARQ-ACK bit sequence ${\widetilde{o}}_{0}^{ACK - HP},\ {\widetilde{o}}_{1}^{ACK - HP},\ \ldots,\ {\widetilde{o}}_{O^{ACK - HP} - 1}^{ACK - HP}$ associated with priority index 1 is given by Clause 9.1 of \[5, TS 38.213\].

- If CSI part 1 is also transmitted on the PUSCH and the PUSCH is associated with priority index 1,

- Set $a_{i}^{(1)}$ for $i = 0,1,\ldots,A^{(1)} - 1$ as the bit sequence of CSI part 1, where the CSI fields of all CSI reports, in the order from upper part to lower part in Table 6.3.2.1.2-6, are mapped to the UCI bit sequence $a_{0}^{(1)},\ a_{1}^{(1)},\ a_{2}^{(1)},\ a_{3}^{(1)},\ \ldots,a_{A^{(1)} - 1}^{(1)}$ starting with $a_{0}^{(1)}$.

- Set $a_{i}^{(2)} = {\widetilde{o}}_{i}^{ACK - LP}$ for $i = {0,1,\ldots,O}^{ACK - LP} - 1$ and $A^{(2)} = O^{ACK - LP}$, where the HARQ-ACK bit sequence ${\widetilde{o}}_{0}^{ACK - LP},\ {\widetilde{o}}_{1}^{ACK - LP},\ \ldots,\ {\widetilde{o}}_{O^{ACK - LP} - 1}^{ACK - LP}$ associated with priority index 0 is given by Clause 9.1 of \[5, TS 38.213\].

- Otherwise,

- Set $a_{i}^{(1)} = {\widetilde{o}}_{i}^{ACK - LP}$ for $i = {0,1,\ldots,O}^{ACK - LP} - 1$ and $A^{(1)} = O^{ACK - LP}$, where the HARQ-ACK bit sequence ${\widetilde{o}}_{0}^{ACK - LP},\ {\widetilde{o}}_{1}^{ACK - LP},\ \ldots,\ {\widetilde{o}}_{O^{ACK - LP} - 1}^{ACK - LP}$ associated with priority index 0 is given by Clause 9.1 of \[5, TS 38.213\].

- Set $a_{i}^{(2)} = {\widetilde{a}}_{i}^{(1)}$ for $i = 0,1,\ldots,{\widetilde{A}}^{(1)} - 1\$and $A^{(2)} = {\widetilde{A}}^{(1)}$, if CSI part 1 is also transmitted on the PUSCH and the PUSCH is associated with priority index 0, where the CSI part 1 sequence ${\widetilde{a}}_{0}^{(1)},\ {\widetilde{a}}_{1}^{(1)},\ {\widetilde{a}}_{2}^{(1)},\ {\widetilde{a}}_{3}^{(1)},\ \ldots,{\widetilde{a}}_{{\widetilde{A}}^{(1)} - 1}^{(1)}$ is given by Table 6.3.2.1.2-6 by replacing $a_{0}^{(1)},\ a_{1}^{(1)},\ a_{2}^{(1)},\ a_{3}^{(1)},\ \ldots,a_{A^{(1)} - 1}^{(1)}$, and the CSI fields of all CSI reports, in the order from upper part to lower part in Table 6.3.2.1.2-6, are mapped to the CSI part 1 sequence ${\widetilde{a}}_{0}^{(1)},\ {\widetilde{a}}_{1}^{(1)},\ {\widetilde{a}}_{2}^{(1)},\ {\widetilde{a}}_{3}^{(1)},\ \ldots,{\widetilde{a}}_{{\widetilde{A}}^{(1)} - 1}^{(1)}$ starting with ${\widetilde{a}}_{0}^{(1)}$.

If *uci-MuxWithDiffPrio* is configured, and CG-UCI associated with priority index 0 and HARQ-ACK bits associated with priority index 0 if any, HARQ-ACK bits associated with priority index 1, and CSI part 1 if any are transmitted on a PUSCH associated with priority index 0, the following UCI bit sequences are generated,$\ a_{0},\ a_{1},\ a_{2},\ a_{3},\ \ldots,a_{A - 1}$, $a_{0}^{(1)},\ a_{1}^{(1)},\ a_{2}^{(1)},\ a_{3}^{(1)},\ \ldots,a_{A^{(1)} - 1}^{(1)}$, and $a_{0}^{(2)},\ a_{1}^{(2)},\ a_{2}^{(2)},\ a_{3}^{(2)},\ \ldots,a_{A^{(2)} - 1}^{(2)}$ if any, according to the following:

- Set $a_{i} = {\widetilde{o}}_{i}^{ACK - HP}$ for $i = {0,1,\ldots,O}^{ACK - HP} - 1$ and $A = O^{ACK - HP}$, where the HARQ-ACK bit sequence ${\widetilde{o}}_{0}^{ACK - HP},\ {\widetilde{o}}_{1}^{ACK - HP},\ \ldots,\ {\widetilde{o}}_{O^{ACK - HP} - 1}^{ACK - HP}$ associated with priority index 1 is given by Clause 9.1 of \[5, TS 38.213\];

- Set $a_{i}^{(1)} = {\widetilde{o}}_{i}^{CG - UCI}$ for $i = 0,1,\ \ldots,\ O^{CG - UCI} - 1$ and $A^{(1)} = O^{CG - UCI}$ if CG-UCI associated with priority index 0 is transmitted without HARQ-ACK bits associated with priority index 0, where the CG-UCI bit sequence ${\widetilde{o}}_{0}^{CG - UCI},\ {\widetilde{o}}_{1}^{CG - UCI},\ \ldots,\ {\widetilde{o}}_{O^{CG - UCI} - 1}^{CG - UCI}$ associated with priority index 0 is given by Table 6.3.2.1.3-1 mapped in the order from upper part to lower part;

- Set $a_{0}^{(1)},\ a_{1}^{(1)},\ a_{2}^{(1)},\ a_{3}^{(1)},\ \ldots,a_{A^{(1)} - 1}^{(1)}$ as follows if both CG-UCI associated with priority index 0 and HARQ-ACK bits associated with priority index 0 are transmitted, where $A^{(1)} = O^{CG - UCI} + O^{ACK - LP}$

- The CG-UCI bits are mapped to the UCI bit sequence $a_{0}^{(1)},\ a_{1}^{(1)},\ a_{2}^{(1)},\ a_{3}^{(1)},\ \ldots,a_{O^{CG - UCI} - 1}^{(1)}$, where $a_{i}^{(1)} = {\widetilde{o}}_{i}^{CG - UCI\ }$for $i = 0,1,\ \ldots,\ O^{CG - UCI} - 1$. The CG-UCI bit sequence ${\widetilde{o}}_{0}^{CG - UCI},\ {\widetilde{o}}_{1}^{CG - UCI},\ \ldots,\ {\widetilde{o}}_{O^{CG - UCI} - 1}^{CG - UCI}$ is given by Table 6.3.2.1.3-1 mapped in the order from upper part to lower part, and $O^{CG - UCI}$ is number of CG-UCI bits

- The HARQ-ACK bits are mapped to the UCI bit sequence $a_{O^{CG - UCI}}^{(1)},\ a_{O^{CG - UCI} + 1}^{(1)},\ \ \ldots,a_{O^{CG - UCI} + O^{ACK - LP} - 1}^{(1)}$, where $a_{i + O^{CG - UCI}}^{(1)} = {\widetilde{o}}_{i}^{ACK - LP}$ for $i = 0,1,\ \ldots,\ O^{ACK - LP} - 1$. The HARQ-ACK bit sequence ${\widetilde{o}}_{0}^{ACK - LP},\ {\widetilde{o}}_{1}^{ACK - LP},\ \ldots,\ {\widetilde{o}}_{O^{ACK - LP} - 1}^{ACK - LP}$ associated with priority index 0 is given by Clause 9.1 of \[5, TS 38.213\].

- Set $a_{i}^{(2)} = {\widetilde{a}}_{i}^{(1)}$ for $i = 0,1,\ldots,{\widetilde{A}}^{(1)} - 1\$and $A^{(2)} = {\widetilde{A}}^{(1)}$, if CSI part 1 is also transmitted on the PUSCH and the PUSCH is associated with priority index 0, where the CSI part 1 sequence ${\widetilde{a}}_{0}^{(1)},\ {\widetilde{a}}_{1}^{(1)},\ {\widetilde{a}}_{2}^{(1)},\ {\widetilde{a}}_{3}^{(1)},\ \ldots,{\widetilde{a}}_{{\widetilde{A}}^{(1)} - 1}^{(1)}$ is given by Table 6.3.2.1.2-6 by replacing $a_{0}^{(1)},\ a_{1}^{(1)},\ a_{2}^{(1)},\ a_{3}^{(1)},\ \ldots,a_{A^{(1)} - 1}^{(1)}$, and the CSI fields of all CSI reports, in the order from upper part to lower part in Table 6.3.2.1.2-6, are mapped to the CSI part 1 sequence ${\widetilde{a}}_{0}^{(1)},\ {\widetilde{a}}_{1}^{(1)},\ {\widetilde{a}}_{2}^{(1)},\ {\widetilde{a}}_{3}^{(1)},\ \ldots,{\widetilde{a}}_{{\widetilde{A}}^{(1)} - 1}^{(1)}$ starting with ${\widetilde{a}}_{0}^{(1)}$.

#### 6.3.2.2 Code block segmentation and CRC attachment

Denote the bits of the payload by $a_{0},a_{1},a_{2},a_{3},...,a_{A-1}$, where $A$ is the payload size. The procedure in 6.3.2.2.1 applies for $A\geq 12$ and the procedure in Clause 6.3.2.2.2 applies for $A\leq 11$.

##### 6.3.2.2.1 UCI encoded by Polar code

Code block segmentation and CRC attachment is performed according to Clause 6.3.1.2.1.

##### 6.3.2.2.2 UCI encoded by channel coding of small block lengths

The procedure in Clause 6.3.1.2.2 applies.

#### 6.3.2.3 Channel coding of UCI

##### 6.3.2.3.1 UCI encoded by Polar code

Channel coding is performed according to Clause 6.3.1.3.1, except that the rate matching output sequence length $E_{r}$ is given in Clause 6.3.2.4.1.

##### 6.3.2.3.2 UCI encoded by channel coding of small block lengths

Information bits are delivered to the channel coding block. They are denoted by $c_{0},c_{1},c_{2},c_{3},...,c_{K-1}$, where $K$ is the number of bits.

The information bits are encoded according to Clause 5.3.3.

After encoding the bits are denoted by $d_{0},d_{1},d_{2},d_{3},...,d_{N-1}$, where $N$ is the number of coded bits.

#### 6.3.2.4 Rate matching

In case where there are more than one UL-SCH transport blocks for the PUSCH transmission, the UCI information is multiplexed only on the UL-SCH transport block with highest *I<sub>MCS</sub>* value for the initial PUSCH, where *I<sub>MCS</sub>* is as defined in Clause 6.1.4.1 in \[6, TS 38.214\]. In case the two transport blocks have the same *I<sub>MCS</sub>* value for the initial PUSCH, the UCI information is multiplexed with data only on the first transport block. The PUSCH for UCI multiplexing in this Clause refers to the UL-SCH transport block for UCI multiplexing.

##### 6.3.2.4.1 UCI encoded by Polar code

If the higher layer parameter *nrofBitsInUTO-UCI* is configured, the procedures in this clause and the clauses it refers to apply by replacing CG-UCI with UTO-UCI in all the notations and texts, when applicable.

##### 6.3.2.4.1.1 HARQ-ACK

For HARQ-ACK transmission on PUSCH not using repetition type B with UL-SCH and if *numberOfSlotsTBoMS* is not present in the resource allocation table, or if *numberOfSlotsTBoMS* is present in the resource allocation table and the value of *numberOfSlotsTBoMS* in the row indicated by the Time domain resource assignment field in DCI is equal to 1, the number of coded modulation symbols per layer for HARQ-ACK transmission, denoted as $Q'_{\text{ACK}}$, is determined as follows:

$$Q'_{\text{ACK}}=\min \left\{\left\lceil \frac{\left(O_{\text{ACK}}+L_{\text{ACK}}\right)\cdot \beta _{\text{offset}}^{\text{PUSCH}}\cdot \sum_{l=0}^{N_{\text{symb,all}}^{\text{PUSCH}}-1}{M_{\text{sc}}^{\text{UCI}}\left(l\right)}}{\sum_{r=0}^{C_{\text{UL}-\text{SCH}}-1}{K_{r}}}\right\rceil ,\left\lceil \alpha \cdot \sum_{l=l_{0}}^{N_{\text{symb,all}}^{\text{PUSCH}}-1}{M_{\text{sc}}^{\text{UCI}}\left(l\right)}\right\rceil \right\}$$

where

- $O_{\text{ACK}}$ is the number of HARQ-ACK bits;

- if $O_{\text{ACK}}\geq 360$, $L_{\text{ACK}}=11$; otherwise $L_{\text{ACK}}$ is the number of CRC bits for HARQ-ACK determined according to Clause 6.3.1.2.1;

- $\beta _{\text{offset}}^{\text{PUSCH}}=\beta _{\text{offset}}^{\text{HARQ}-\text{ACK}}$;

- $C_{\text{UL}-\text{SCH}}$ is the number of code blocks for UL-SCH of the PUSCH transmission;

- if the DCI format scheduling the PUSCH transmission includes a CBGTI field indicating that the UE shall not transmit the $r$-th code block, $K_{r}$=0; otherwise, $K_{r}$ is the $r$-th code block size for UL-SCH of the PUSCH transmission;

- $M_{\text{sc}}^{\text{PUSCH}}$ is the scheduled bandwidth of the PUSCH transmission, expressed as a number of subcarriers;

- If the PUSCH transmission is in SBFD symbols, $M_{\text{sc}}^{\text{PUSCH}}$ only includes subcarriers of the scheduled bandwidth that are both in the active UL BWP and in the UL sub-band, as described in Clause 6.1.2.2.1 in \[6, TS 38.214\];

- $M_{\text{sc}}^{\text{PT-RS}}\left(l\right)$ is the number of subcarriers in OFDM symbol $l$ that carries PTRS, in the PUSCH transmission;

- $M_{\text{sc}}^{Muted}(l)$ is the number of muted subcarriers in OFDM symbol $l$ in the PUSCH transmission;

- $M_{\text{sc}}^{\text{UCI}}\left(l\right)$ is the number of resource elements that can be used for transmission of UCI in OFDM symbol $l$, for $l=0,\ 1,\ 2,\ ...,N_{\text{symb,all}}^{\text{PUSCH}}-1$, in the PUSCH transmission and $N_{\text{symb,all}}^{\text{PUSCH}}$ is the total number of OFDM symbols of the PUSCH, including all OFDM symbols used for DMRS;

- for any OFDM symbol that carries DMRS of the PUSCH, $M_{\text{sc}}^{\text{UCI}}\left(l\right)=0$;

- for any OFDM symbol that does not carry DMRS of the PUSCH, $M_{\text{sc}}^{\text{UCI}}(l) = M_{\text{sc}}^{\text{PUSCH}} - M_{\text{sc}}^{\text{PT-RS}}(l) - M_{\text{sc}}^{Muted}(l)$;

- $\alpha$ is configured by higher layer parameter *scaling*;

- $l_{0}$ is the symbol index of the first OFDM symbol that does not carry DMRS of the PUSCH, after the first DMRS symbol(s), in the PUSCH transmission.

For HARQ-ACK transmission on PUSCH not using repetition type B with UL-SCH, and if *numberOfSlotsTBoMS* is present in the resource allocation table and the value of *numberOfSlotsTBoMS* in the row indicated by the Time domain resource assignment field in DCI is larger than 1, the number of coded modulation symbols per layer for HARQ-ACK transmission, denoted as $Q_{ACK}'$, is determined as follows:

$Q_{ACK}' = \min\left\{ \left\lceil \frac{\left( O_{ACK} + L_{ACK} \right) \bullet \beta_{offset}^{PUSCH} \bullet \sum_{l = 0}^{N_{symb,all}^{PUSCH} - 1}{M_{sc}^{UCI}(l)}}{\frac{1}{N_{s}}\sum_{r = 0}^{C_{UL - SCH} - 1}K_{r}} \right\rceil,\left\lceil \alpha \bullet \sum_{l = l_{0}}^{N_{symb,all}^{PUSCH} - 1}{M_{sc}^{UCI}(l)} \right\rceil \right\}$

where

- $N_{s}$ is the value of *numberOfSlotsTBoMS* in the row indicated by the Time domain resource assignment field in DCI;

- $M_{sc}^{PT - RS}(l)\$is the number of subcarriers in OFDM symbol $l$ that carries PTRS, in the PUSCH transmission of TB processing over multiple slots in the slot with the HARQ-ACK transmission;

- $M_{\text{sc}}^{Muted}(l)$ is the number of muted subcarriers in OFDM symbol $l$ in the PUSCH transmission of TB processing over multiple slots in the slot with the HARQ-ACK transmission;

- $M_{sc}^{UCI}(l)$ is the number of resource elements that can be used for transmission of UCI in OFDM symbol $l$, for $l = 0,1,2,\ldots,N_{symb,all}^{PUSCH} - 1$, in the PUSCH transmission of TB processing over multiple slots in the slot with the HARQ-ACK transmission and $N_{symb,all}^{PUSCH}\$is the total number of OFDM symbols of the PUSCH in the slot, including all OFDM symbols used for DMRS;

- $l_{0}\$is the symbol index of the first OFDM symbol that does not carry DMRS of the PUSCH, after the first DMRS symbol(s), in the PUSCH transmission of TB processing over multiple slots in the slot with the HARQ-ACK transmission;

- and all the other notations in the formula are defined the same as for PUSCH not using repetition type B and if *numberOfSlotsTBoMS* is not present in the resource allocation table.

For HARQ-ACK transmission on an actual repetition of a PUSCH with repetition Type B with UL-SCH, the number of coded modulation symbols per layer for HARQ-ACK transmission, denoted as $Q_{ACK}'$, is determined as follows:

$Q_{ACK}' = \min\left\{ \left\lceil \frac{\left( O_{ACK} + L_{ACK} \right) \bullet \beta_{\text{offset}}^{\text{PUSCH}} \bullet \sum_{l = 0}^{N_{\text{symb,nominal}}^{\text{PUSCH}} - 1}{M_{\text{sc,nominal}}^{\text{UCI}}(l)}}{\sum_{r = 0}^{C_{\text{UL-SCH}} - 1}K_{r}} \right\rceil,\ \ \ \left\lceil \alpha \bullet \sum_{l = 0}^{N_{\text{symb,nominal}}^{\text{PUSCH}} - 1}{M_{\text{sc,nominal}}^{\text{UCI}}(l)} \right\rceil,\ \ \sum_{l = 0}^{N_{\text{symb,actual}}^{\text{PUSCH}} - 1}{M_{\text{sc,actual}}^{\text{UCI}}(l)} \right\}\$

where

- $M_{\text{sc,nominal}}^{\text{UCI}}(l)$ is the number of resource elements that can be used for transmission of UCI in OFDM symbol $l$, for $l = 0,\ 1,\ 2,\ \cdots,\ N_{\text{symb,nominal}}^{\text{PUSCH}} - 1$, in the PUSCH transmission assuming a nominal repetition without segmentation, and $N_{\text{symb,nominal}}^{\text{PUSCH}}$ is the total number of OFDM symbols in a nominal repetition of the PUSCH, including all OFDM symbols used for DMRS;

- for any OFDM symbol that carries DMRS of the PUSCH assuming a nominal repetition without segmentation, $M_{\text{sc,nominal}}^{\text{UCI}}(l) = 0$;

- for any OFDM symbol that does not carry DMRS of the PUSCH assuming a nominal repetition without segmentation, $M_{\text{sc,nominal}}^{\text{UCI}}(l) = M_{\text{sc}}^{\text{PUSCH}} - M_{\text{sc,nominal}}^{\text{PT-RS}}(l) - M_{\text{sc,nominal}}^{Muted}(l)$ where $M_{\text{sc,nominal}}^{\text{PT-RS}}(l)$ is the number of subcarriers in OFDM symbol $l$ that carries PTRS, in the PUSCH transmission assuming a nominal repetition without segmentation, $M_{\text{sc,nominal}}^{Muted}(l)$ is the number of muted subcarriers in OFDM symbol $l$ in the PUSCH transmission, assuming a nominal repetition without segmentation;

- $M_{\text{sc,actual}}^{\text{UCI}}(l)$ is the number of resource elements that can be used for transmission of UCI in OFDM symbol $l$ , for $l = 0,\ 1,\ 2,\ \cdots,\ N_{\text{symb,actual}}^{\text{PUSCH}} - 1$, in the actual repetition of the PUSCH transmission, and $N_{\text{symb,actual}}^{\text{PUSCH}}$ is the total number of OFDM symbols in the actual repetition of the PUSCH transmission, including all OFDM symbols used for DMRS;

- for any OFDM symbol that carries DMRS of the actual repetition of the PUSCH transmission, $M_{\text{sc,actual}}^{\text{UCI}}(l) = 0$;

- for any OFDM symbol that does not carry DMRS of the actual repetition of the PUSCH transmission, $M_{\text{sc,actual}}^{\text{UCI}}(l) = M_{\text{sc}}^{\text{PUSCH}} - M_{\text{sc,actual}}^{\text{PT-RS}}(l) - M_{\text{sc,actual}}^{Muted}(l)$ where $M_{\text{sc,actual}}^{\text{PT-RS}}(l)$ is the number of subcarriers in OFDM symbol $l$ that carries PTRS, in the actual repetition of the PUSCH transmission, $M_{\text{sc,actual}}^{Muted}(l)$ is the number of muted subcarriers in OFDM symbol $l$ in the actual repetition of the PUSCH transmission;

- and all the other notations in the formula are defined the same as for PUSCH not using repetition type B and if *numberOfSlotsTBoMS* is not present in the resource allocation table.

For HARQ-ACK transmission on PUSCH without UL-SCH, the number of coded modulation symbols per layer for HARQ-ACK transmission, denoted as $Q'_{\text{ACK}}$, is determined as follows:

$$Q'_{\text{ACK}}=\min \left\{\left\lceil \frac{\left(O_{\text{ACK}}+L_{\text{ACK}}\right)\cdot \beta _{\text{offset}}^{\text{PUSCH}}}{R\cdot Q_{m}}\right\rceil ,\left\lceil \alpha \cdot \sum_{l=l_{0}}^{N_{\text{symb,all}}^{\text{PUSCH}}-1}{M_{\text{sc}}^{\text{UCI}}\left(l\right)}\right\rceil \right\}$$

where

- $O_{\text{ACK}}$ is the number of HARQ-ACK bits;

- if $O_{\text{ACK}}\geq 360$, $L_{\text{ACK}}=11$; otherwise $L_{\text{ACK}}$ is the number of CRC bits for HARQ-ACK defined according to Clause 6.3.1.2.1;;

- $\beta _{\text{offset}}^{\text{PUSCH}}=\beta _{\text{offset}}^{\text{HARQ}-\text{ACK}}$;

- $M_{\text{sc}}^{\text{PUSCH}}$ is the scheduled bandwidth of the PUSCH transmission, expressed as a number of subcarriers;

- If the PUSCH transmission is in SBFD symbols, $M_{\text{sc}}^{\text{PUSCH}}$ only includes subcarriers of the scheduled bandwidth that are both in the active UL BWP and in the UL sub-band, as described in Clause 6.1.2.2.1 in \[6, TS 38.214\];

- $M_{\text{sc}}^{\text{PT-RS}}\left(l\right)$ is the number of subcarriers in OFDM symbol $l$ that carries PTRS, in the PUSCH transmission;

- $M_{\text{sc}}^{Muted}(l)$ is the number of muted subcarriers in OFDM symbol $l$, in the PUSCH transmission;
